Yes...Thank you...Tomorrow at five o'clock at the Biltrnore. I'll see you then. thank you, Mr.Levine.
lines.chinese: 好的。 喂，Susan Stewart想与 Levine先生通话。 他在电话上。 先生，我是 Susan Stewart。 我现在有一件很为难的事。我和别人约好共进午餐 但忘了写到工作日程。 你和我能否在明天见面吗？ 我非常感激……好的……谢谢你…… 明天五点钟在 Biltmore 饭店。 到时候见啦。 谢谢你，Levine 先生。

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.english: Welcome to the South Street Restaurant, folks. What'll it be?
lines.chinese: 欢迎光临南街餐馆，各位。 想来点什么？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: What do you recommend?
lines.chinese: 你推荐什么？

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.english: Well, the crab salad's always a big hit.
lines.chinese: 嗯，蟹肉色拉一向是很受欢迎的。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Susan, would you like the crab salad?
lines.chinese: 要不要蟹肉色拉？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I'd love the crab salad.
lines.chinese: 我要蟹肉色拉。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Michelle, would you like to try the crab salad, too?
lines.chinese: 要不要也试试蟹肉色拉？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: OK, Daddy.
lines.chinese: 好的，爸爸。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: We'll have three crab salads and a pitcher of lemonade.
lines.chinese: 我们来三份蟹肉色拉。 和一壶柠檬汁。

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.english: Help yourself to celery and carrots and other vegetables.
lines.chinese: 请你们自己盛芹菜，胡萝和其他蔬菜。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: We used to catch crabs.
lines.chinese: 我们以前常捉螃蟹。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Where was that?
lines.chinese: 在什么地方？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: We had a summer house on Fire island. Do you remember, Michelle?
lines.chinese: 我们在火岛有一所避暑房屋。 你还记得吗，Michelle？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Sure. You and Mommy used to take me on the ferryboat.
lines.chinese: 当然记得。 你和妈妈常带我坐渡船。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Sometimes, at night, we would go down to the beach and catch crabs, remember?
lines.chinese: 有时候，在夜里， 我们走到海边抓螃蟹， 记得吗？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: With a piece of meat on a string!
lines.chinese: 用一根绳子拴着一点肉。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Right. Well, I think I'm going to go get us all some vegetables.
lines.chinese: 对。 好啦，我想我去给大家盛点蔬菜来。

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.english: There you go.
lines.chinese: 东西来了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.english: And some ice-cold lemonade.
lines.chinese: 还有冰凉的柠檬汁。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Waiter
lines.english: Enjoy it.
lines.chinese: 请享用。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Michelle, can I help you with the lemonade?
lines.chinese: 我帮你倒点柠檬汁，好吗？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: No, thank you, I'll wait for my father.
lines.chinese: 不，谢谢你。 我要等我爸爸。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Michelle, can we have a talk?
lines.chinese: 我们能谈一谈吗？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Sure.
lines.chinese: 当然。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I know you miss your mother.
lines.chinese: 我知道你想念你妈妈。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: You do?
lines.chinese: 真的知道？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Yes. And I'm not trying to take her place.
lines.chinese: 是的，我并不想取代她。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Then why are you and Daddy spending so much time together?
lines.chinese: 那为什么你和爸爸 老是在一起呢？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Because we like each other. And right now, he needs a friend.
lines.chinese: 因为我们彼此喜欢。 而且现在，他需要一个朋友。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: I'm his friend.
lines.chinese: 我是他的朋友。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I know you are.
lines.chinese: 我知道你是。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Sometimes he's very sad.
lines.chinese: 他有时很忧郁。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: And so are you, I think.
lines.chinese: 我想你也是如此。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Sometimes.
lines.chinese: 有时候。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I'd like to be your friend, too. Will you let me be your friend, Michelle?
lines.chinese: 我也想成为你的朋友。 愿意让我成为你的朋友吗，Michelle?

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: So, what were you two talking about?
lines.chinese: 你们俩刚才谈什么了？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Just girl talk, daddy. It's too hard to explain.
lines.chinese: 只是女孩子之间的谈话，爸爸。 这很难解释清楚。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: You're probably right. Well, let's get started.
lines.chinese: 也许你们说得对。 好吧，我们开始吃吧。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, it's a quarter to four, and I have a production meeting at four.
lines.chinese: 噢，差十五分就四点了。 我四点钟有个生产会议。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I planned to take you for a ride in Central Park in a horse and carriage.
lines.chinese: 我原打算带你去中央公园 坐马车。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Harry, I'd love to , but I have work to do.
lines.chinese: 我确实想去。 但我有工作要做。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: OK. We'll walk back to your office with you. It's so nice out. I decided to forget about my accounting problems and just enjoy this beautiful spring day. Take the time, Susan.
lines.chinese: 好吧，我们陪你走回你的办公室去。 外面天气真好。 我已决定不再去想那些会计上的问题尽情享受这美好的春天。 别那么忙，Susan。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I know I should, but... well, there are too many things to do.
lines.chinese: 我知道应该这样， 但是……我要做的事情太多了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I understand. I'll go for a ride with Michelle.
lines.chinese: 我了解。 我和Michelle 一起去坐马车。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Right. Well, I had a really nice time.
lines.chinese: 好的。 嗯，我玩得很开心。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: So did I.
lines.chinese: 我也一样。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: So did I. I'm sorry you can't come with us, Susan.
lines.chinese: 我也一样。 很遗憾你不能同我们一起去，Susan。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: So am I.
lines.chinese: 我也很遗憾。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Bye-bye.
lines.chinese: 再见。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Harry! Michelle! Can you wait till I make a phone call?
lines.chinese: 你们等一下让我打个电话好吗？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Sure.
lines.chinese: 办公室。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Susan Stewart's office.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Sam, this is Susan.
lines.chinese: 我是 Susan。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Hi. How was lunch?
lines.chinese: 嗨，午餐怎么样？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Fine.
lines.chinese: 很好。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: You're late. The production department's waiting in the conference room.
lines.chinese: 你已经迟了。 生产部门的人正在会议室等你。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I know. Ask Paul Smith to fill in for me. He knows everything about the production schedule, and he can answer any questions.
lines.chinese: 我知道。 叫 Paul Smith 代替我出席。 他知道生产进度的一切，他也可以回答任何问题。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Right.
lines.chinese: 好的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Don't tell anyone, but I'm taking a little time to smell the flowers.
lines.chinese: 别告诉别人， 我正利用一点时间轻松一下。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Good for you. It'll be our secret.
lines.chinese: 我真为你高兴。 这只是你我之间的秘密。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: But schedule another production meeting for tomorrow. I'll be back for my six o'clock appointment with Mr. Ozawa.
lines.chinese: 务必安排在明天举行另一次生产会议。 我会回来 参加六点钟与 Ozawa 先生的会议。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: OK, Susan. And have a nice afternoon.
lines.chinese: 好的，Susan。 祝你下午玩得愉快。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Thanks
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: She likes you.
lines.chinese: 她喜欢你。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I know. I like her.
lines.chinese: 我知道。我喜欢她。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: How'd you do it?
lines.chinese: 是如何办到的？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: We had a talk.
lines.chinese: 我们谈了一下。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: About what?
lines.chinese: 谈什么？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Life. harry： And what did you decide?
lines.chinese: 生活。那你们有什么结论呢？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: That's a secret...between us women.
lines.chinese: 这是个秘密……我们女人间的秘密。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Ellen, I'd like your opinion
lines.chinese: 我想听听你的意见。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: About what?
lines.chinese: 关于什么？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, Richard and I feel that with a baby coming we need to have our own place to live.
lines.chinese: 嗯，Richard 和我觉得孩子快要出生了 我们应该有自己的房子。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh.

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, what do you think about Richard and me looking for a small house or an apartmnt at this point in our lives?
lines.chinese: 噢。 那么，你觉得我和 Richard 找一个小住宅或公寓 在这个时候怎么样？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: We love having you here, and there is room, and...and when the baby comes, the baby can stay in your room for a while.
lines.chinese: 我们喜欢你们住这儿， 并且这儿有地方， 而且……而且婴儿出生以后， 可以住在你们房间一段时间。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Richard feels we need to find a small house.
lines.chinese: 觉得我们应该要找一个小住宅。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I remember when I was pregnant with Richard. Philip and I were living with Grandma and Grandpa. Philip was a young doctor, and he kept talking about having a house of our own. It's natural.
lines.chinese: 我记得当我怀着 Richard 时。 与我和爷爷奶奶住在一起。 当时是个年轻的大夫， 他老说要有自己的家。 这是很自然的。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What did you do ?
lines.chinese: 那你们怎么办呢？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: We looked at a lot of houses.
lines.chinese: 我们看了好多房子。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Did you find one?
lines.chinese: 找到合适的吗？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, not at first. We couldn't afford it. Grandpa wanted to led us the money to buy one, but Philip is too independent. He didn't want to borrow any money.
lines.chinese: 噢，没有马上找到。 我们当时钱不够。 爷爷要借给我们钱去买房， 但是 Philip 的独立性太强了。 他不想借钱。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Sounds like Richard.
lines.chinese: 听起来跟 Richard一样。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: They're all alike. Richard is a real Stewart. He's independent, and sometimes just stubbom.
lines.chinese: 他们都是一个样子。 是个典型的 Stewart家后代。 他的独立性强， 有时候甚至近乎顽固。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: When did you buy a house?
lines.chinese: 那你们在什么时候买房子？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: After Richard was born. I was teaching music, and Philip was opening his first medical office.
lines.chinese: 等生下 Richard之后。 我当时教音乐， 而 Philip 则第一次开诊所。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Where was the house?
lines.chinese: 房子在什么地方？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Right here in Riverdate. Of course, it was a small house, but just right for us.
lines.chinese: 就在这 Riverdale。 当然，那是一栋小房子。 但正好够我们住。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It's funny. History repeats itself. Now Richard and I are having a baby, and we probably won't be able to afford a house right away, either.
lines.chinese: 真有意思。历史在重演。 现在 Richard和我有了孩子， 而我们也许也一样不能 马上负担得起一栋房子。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Why don't you look at some houses, Marilyn?
lines.chinese: 何不去看一看房子呢，Marily？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Good idea.
lines.chinese: 好主意。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Look in the real-estate section of Sunday's Times. You'll learn a lot.
lines.chinese: 查一查星期天纽约时报的房地产版面。 会增长不少见识。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Maybe we should speak to a real-estate agent about a house.
lines.chinese: 也许我们应该 和房地产中介商谈谈关于房子的事。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: And a bank about a mortgage.
lines.chinese: 还要和银行谈谈。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I'll talk to Richard about it. I think it's a good idea, Ellen. We can learn a lot by asking.
lines.chinese: 我会与 Richard 商量这些。 我觉得这是个好主意，Ellen。 我们到处问问会学到不少东西。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: And if I can be of any help, let me know. As a matter of fact, my friend Virginia Martinelli is a real-estate agent.
lines.chinese: 如果我能有所帮忙的， 告诉我。 事实上， 我的朋友 Virginia Martinelli就是个房地产中介。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Good.
lines.chinese: 好极了。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You won't believe this, but she sold us our first house and this one.
lines.chinese: 你简直不会相信， 我们的第一栋房子和这一栋都是她卖的。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, I'll tell Richard, and we'll go to see her. Do you think the skirt length is right, Ellen? Do you think it's too long?
lines.chinese: 嗯，我会告诉 Richard， 我们会去找她。 你觉得这裙子长度合适吗，Ellen? 你会不会觉得太长点？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I think the skirt is just right. Are you planning to attach a train to it?
lines.chinese: 我觉得长度合适。 你是不是准备加拖地裙摆？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: No. No train. Just the dress. But I am going to make a headpiece of lace.
lines.chinese: 不，不加裙摆。就是这样连身套装。 但我准备做一个蕾丝头饰。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: That dress is gorgeous.
lines.chinese: 这套服装真华丽。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thanks, Ellen. And thanks for the advice about the house. I'll talk to Richard about it the minute the comes home.
lines.chinese: 谢谢，Ellen。 也谢谢你关于房子的建议。 我就跟 Richard 商量 他一回来。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: And remember, we love having you here. There's no need to rush.
lines.chinese: 记住， 我们喜欢你们住这儿。 没有必要急急忙忙分开。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: I remember yuor parents' first house very well. It was on Spring Avenue, near the park.
lines.chinese: 我还清楚记得你父母的第一栋房子。 那房子是在 Spring Avenue 公园附近。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I grew up in that house.
lines.chinese: 我就在那栋房子里长大的。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: Yes, and you were such a cute baby.
lines.chinese: 对，你当时是个很逗人喜爱的婴儿。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I've seen pictures of him. He had blond hair.
lines.chinese: 我见过他的照片。 他原来是金色头发。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: I've been friendly with the Stewart family for a long time, so It's my pleasure to help you find a house now.
lines.chinese: 我和 Stewart 家处得很好 很久了， 所以现在我很乐意 帮助你们找房子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, we're not sure we can afford one.
lines.chinese: 嗯，我们还不知道我们是不是负担得起。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: But we'd like to find ut about the possibilities.
lines.chinese: 但我们想知道 有多大的可能性。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: That's a good idea. I love your house on Linden Street. I sold your father that house seventeen years ago.
lines.chinese: 这是个好主意。 我喜欢你们家在 Linden 街的房子。 十七年前是我把那栋房子卖给你父亲的。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Really?
lines.chinese: 真的？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yes, Mom was pregnant with Robbie then, and they needed the extra room.
lines.chinese: 是的，那时妈妈怀着 Robbie, 他们需要多一些空间.

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: I hear you're expecting a baby, Mrs.Stewart.
lines.chinese: 我听说你们快有小孩了,Stewart 太太.

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Mmm-hmm. So we will be needing more room.
lines.chinese: 嗯。 所以我们也将需要更大的空间.

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: Oh, so you don't need something immediately?
lines.chinese: 呵,所以你们不是急着马上就要买房子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: No. But in five or six months...
lines.chinese: 不急。但是在五六个月后……

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And time passes so quickly.
lines.chinese: 可是时间过得很快。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: Yes, it does. Well, when you called, you gave me enough information about your salaries and your savings. So I have a good idea about your financial situation. Let me show your some pictures of houses.
lines.chinese: 是的，确实如此。 嗯，你们打电话来， 已经给了我充分的的资料 关于薪资和储蓄。 所以我对你们的的财务状况很了解。 我来给你们看看一些房子的照片。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: With two bedrooms?
lines.chinese: 有两间卧室吗？

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: Yes, I think I can show you some. Of course, they won't be in Riverdale. The cost of housing's too high for you here.
lines.chinese: 是的，我想我有一些房子给你们看。 当然这些房子不在 Riverdale。 这儿的房子对你们来说太贵了

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I haven't thought about living anywhere else. We've always lived in this area.
lines.chinese: 我还没有考虑要住到别的地方去。 我们一直住在这个地区。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Where should we look for a house, Mrs.Martinelli?
lines.chinese: 我们应该到什么地方找房子，Martinelli 太太？

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: Well, we have an office in Mount Kisco. It's a lovely area, and it's only about an hour's rive from here. Here. I have a book with photos of some homes in that area. Now, let's see. Here. This is a lovely two-bedroom house in your price range.
lines.chinese: 好，我们在Mount Kisco有个办事处。 那是个可爱的地方， 而且从此地到那儿只有大约一小时的车程。 这儿。 我有一本那个地区房子的照片。 来，我们看看。这儿。 这一栋可爱的房子有两间卧室，在你们价格范围内。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It's pretty, but I prefer a two-story home.
lines.chinese: 是漂亮。 但我喜欢两层楼的的房子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I do, too. I don't care for a ranch type.
lines.chinese: 我也一样。 我不爱平房。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: OK. Oh, this is a wonderful house. I know it well. I sold it to the present owners.
lines.chinese: 好的。 啊，这是一栋很好的房子。 我非常清楚。 是我把它卖给了现在的主人。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It looks wonderful.
lines.chinese: 看起来很好看。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: This is a two-bedroom, two-bath house. It has a full basement, and it is on a half- acre lot. You can probably afford this one.
lines.chinese: 这是一栋有两间卧室、两间盥洗室的房子。 它有一个全面地下室， 用地半英亩。 你可能负担得起这栋房子。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I like this house.
lines.chinese: 我喜欢这栋房子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: So do I.
lines.chinese: 我也喜欢。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: And the price is right. Would you like to go see it?
lines.chinese: 而且价格也合适。 你们要去看看吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yes. We're planning to talk to someone at the bank next week. Perhaps we could see the house this weekend.
lines.chinese: 是的。我们计划 下星期与银行的人谈一谈。 也许这周末我们会去看看这栋房子。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: If some one doesn't buy it before then. But let's keep looking. Just to get an idea of some other possibilities.
lines.chinese: 如果在此之前没有人买走的话， 让我们继续看下去。 瞧瞧其他可能的选择。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: This is very helpful, Mrs. Martinelli.
lines.chinese: 这样很有用，Martinelli 太太。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: Here. This is a wonderful example of Spanish-style architecture.
lines.chinese: 这个。 这是栋很棒的典型西班牙式建筑。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, I love the roof tiles on a Spanish-style house.
lines.chinese: 噢， 我喜欢西班牙式房屋的屋顶瓦。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It looks like the houses in Hollywood.
lines.chinese: 看起来好像是好莱坞的房子。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: It's interesting. A house like this in Riverdale costs double the price. Oh my! Here's a real buy. It's a bargain. This house just came on the market.
lines.chinese: 很有趣。 在 Riverdale像这样的房子价格要贵一倍。 啊！ 这个价格便宜。真是大廉售。 这栋房子刚刚推出要卖。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It's quite lovely. Is it a two-bedroom house?
lines.chinese: 相当好看。 是两个卧室的吗？

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: No. It has three bedrooms and three baths. I know the house. It has a brand new kitchen. And a living room with a twelve-foot ceiling. And there's a two-car garage.
lines.chinese: 不。这栋房子有三间卧室、三间盥洗室. 我熟悉这房子。 有一个全新的厨房。 有一间十二英尺高的的客厅。 还有一个可放两辆车的车库。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Then why don't we go look at this house, too?
lines.chinese: 那我们何不也去看看这栋房？

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: It's a good investment.
lines.chinese: 这是一项好投资。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you, Mrs. Martinelli.
lines.chinese: 谢谢，Martinelli 太太。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thanks so much.
lines.chinese: 非常感谢。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: My pleasure. Give my best to your parents.
lines.chinese: 不客气。 替我向你父母问好。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: Your father's a wonderful doctor, Richard. He took care of my daughter when she was a child. He's the best pediatrician in Westchester.
lines.chinese: 你父亲是个了不起的医生，Richard。 我女儿曾受他照顾 在她小时候。 他是全 Westchester最好的小儿科医师。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks, Mrs. Martinelli, I'll give them your regards.
lines.chinese: 谢谢，Martinelli 太太。 我会向他们转达你的问候。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We really appreciate your advice.
lines.chinese: 我们非常感谢你的建议。

lines.speaker: Virginia
lines.english: I do think you should go see the houses and talk to the bank. Here. Let me give you some information sheets abo the houses. They're both very good buys.
lines.chinese: 我确实感到你们应该去看看这些房 且和银行谈一谈。 这儿。我给你们一些关于这些房子的简介。 这两栋都是性价比比较高的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, thanks so much for your help and your time, Mrs. Martinelli. We've got a lot to talk about.
lines.chinese: 好的，感谢你的帮助和花了你的时间， 太太。 我们有很多可商量的了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Mmm-hmm. Thanks.
lines.chinese: 嗯。谢谢。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: How do you do?
lines.chinese: 你好。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Hello.
lines.chinese: 哈。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: I'm Ralph Riley.
lines.chinese: 我叫 Ralph Riley。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'm Richard Stewart, and this is my wife, Marilyn.
lines.chinese: 我叫 Richard Stewart，这是我妻子 Marilyn。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Pleased to meet you.
lines.chinese: 很高兴与你见面。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Likewise, Mr.Riley. MR.Riley： Please, sit down. What can I do for you?
lines.chinese: 我们也一样 Riley先生。请坐下。我能为你们做什么？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We'd like to discuss a mortgage.
lines.chinese: 我们想请教一下关于贷款的事。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: For a house.
lines.chinese: 为了房子。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Fine. Are you buying a house or are you reinancing your present home?
lines.chinese: 好的。你们是要买房子？ 还是原有的房子重新申请贷款？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We're planning to buy a house.
lines.chinese: 我们计划买一栋房子。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And we'd like to find out about a mortgage.
lines.chinese: 我们想知道如何贷款。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We are customers of the bank. As a matter of fact, my whole Family banks here.
lines.chinese: 我们是贵行的顾客。 事实上，我们全家都是贵行的顾客。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: I have some questions to ask. Do you own your house or do you rent?
lines.chinese: 我有一些问题要问。 你们自己有房子还是租房？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Neither. We live with my parents, Dr. and Mrs. Philip Stewart.
lines.chinese: 都不是。 我们和父母住在一起，我父母是 Philip Stewart 夫妇。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: And how old are you?
lines.chinese: 你多大了？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I'm twenty-nine.
lines.chinese: 我二十九岁。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'm thirty. Mr.RIley： And, Mr. Stewat, what is your occupation?
lines.chinese: 我三十岁。那么，Stewart，请问你的职业是什么？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'm a freelance photographer.
lines.chinese: 我是自由摄影师。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: And, Mrs. Stewart, are you working?
lines.chinese: 嗯，Stewart 太太，你有工作吗？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Yes. I'm a designer, and I work in a boutique.
lines.chinese: 是的，我是位设计师，我在一家服装店工作。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Did you bring any savings or salary information? Last year's tax forms?
lines.chinese: 你是否带来储蓄和薪资的相关资料？ 譬如去年的税务申报表？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yes. Here they are.
lines.chinese: 带来了，在这儿。

lines.speaker: Mr. Riley
lines.english: OK. What...what kind of house did you have in mind?
lines.chinese: 好的，你们打算买什么样的房子？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We're talking about buying a two-bedroom house in Mount Kisco. Here are the financial details on the house.
lines.chinese: 我们在商量购买 一栋有两间卧室的的房子，在 Mount Kisco。 这是这房子的财务细节。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Thank you. Are you prepared to make a ten-percent down payment?
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。 你打算缴纳10%做头期款吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yes, we are.
lines.chinese: 是的，我们准备如此办。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Payments over thirty years?
lines.chinese: 三十年分期付款吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yes.
lines.chinese: 是的。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Do you think we can get a loan?
lines.chinese: 你觉得我们能得到贷款吗？

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Well, it depends. Do you own any other property? Any stocks or bonds?
lines.chinese: 嗯，这还要看情况。你们拥有其他什么财产吗？ 有股票或债券吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: No.

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: I see. Then you don't have any collateral. Perhaps you could get a guarantor- someone to sign for the loan for you.
lines.chinese: 没有。 我明白了。那么你们没有任何担保物。 也许你可以找一位担保人--- 找个人为你们的贷款签个名。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Why is that necessary?
lines.chinese: 为什么有此必要？

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Since you don't have enough income, and you don't already own any property, the bank needs to be sure you can pay the mortgage every month. A guarantor is responsible for the loan if you can't make the payments.
lines.chinese: 由于你们的收入不够， 而你们又没拥有其他财产。 银行需要确定 你们能每月按时付款。 担保人要对贷款负责 如果你们不能还钱。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I see. Well, the idea of buying a house is exciting.
lines.chinese: 我明白了。嗯 ，买房子的念头叫人兴奋过头。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thank you, Mr.Riley. We'll read this over carefully.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你，Riley先生。我们会仔细阅读这些文件的。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你们。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Good-bye. Thanks.
lines.chinese: 再见。谢谢。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Godd-bye. And hope to see you soon.
lines.chinese: 再见。希望很快再见到你们。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I hope so, too.
lines.chinese: 我也希望如此。

lines.speaker: Mr.Riley
lines.english: Take care.
lines.chinese: 慢走。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Good-bye.
lines.chinese: 再见。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It all sounded so easy until they mentioned needing collateral or a guarantor.
lines.chinese: 一切听起来那么容易 在他们提到担保物或担保人之前。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We have no collateral.
lines.chinese: 我们没有担保物。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And I don't think it's a good idea to ask Dad to sign as a guarantor. I don't feel right about it.
lines.chinese: 而且我觉得这不是个好主意。 请爸爸当担保人为我签字。 我觉得这样不对。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I understand your feelings about it, Richard.
lines.chinese: 我了解你对这种事的心情，Richard。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Now, tell me, what's the problem?
lines.chinese: 现在，告诉我，你有什么问题？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We can get a loan from the bank if we can put up some collateral.
lines.chinese: 我们能够从银行得到贷款 如果我们能提供担保物的话。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And we don't own anything to use as collateral.
lines.chinese: 可是我们没有任何可以做担保物的东西。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Or someone can sign with us as a guarantor.
lines.chinese: 或者有人能当担保人帮我们签字。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Why don't you speak to Dad?
lines.chinese: 你们为什么不跟爸爸说说？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: No. If we buy a house, I want to be able to handle it alone.
lines.chinese: 不，如果我们买房子，我希望能够自行负责。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Everybody needs help sometimes, Richard.
lines.chinese: 每个人都有需要帮助的时候，Richard。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I understand Richard's feelings about it, Ellen.
lines.chinese: 我了解Richard 的感受，Ellen。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: In two or three months, I'll have an advance on my book and be able to put more money down.
lines.chinese: 两到三个月后，我能得到我那本书的预付款 也就可以增加头期款的数目。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What about the house in Mount Kisco? Somebody else'll buy it by then.
lines.chinese: 在 Mount Kisco 的方子呢？ 别人也许买走了。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Then there'll be other houses, Marilyn.
lines.chinese: 还会有别的房子，Marilyn。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Richard has a point. You're just beginning to look.
lines.chinese: 说得对。你们只是开始看房子而已。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We're in no great rush. It's true.
lines.chinese: 我们不很急，这是真的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: This has been a great learning experience for us, Marilyn. Talking to the real-estate agent. Looking at the houses. Talking to the loan officer at the bank.
lines.chinese: 这对我们来讲是一次很好的学习经验，Marilyn。 与房地产中介商谈。看房子。 与银行的贷款负责人谈。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It has been a learning experience. That's true.
lines.chinese: 这真是一次学习经验，真的。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I think you're doing the right thing. Taking your time. Looking around. Especially with a purchase of this kind. You're talking about a lot of money.
lines.chinese: 我觉得你们做得对。慢慢来。 到处看看，特别是这一类买卖。 你们谈的可是一大笔钱。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We'll call Mrs. Martinelli and tell her to keep looking for us.
lines.chinese: 我们会打电话给 Martinelli 太太告诉她继续帮我们物色房子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And I'll call Mr.Riley at the bank and tell him we'll see him in a couple of months.
lines.chinese: 我打电话到银行给 Riley先生 告诉他过几个月我们再去找他。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: And if you ever need Dad or me to help you...
lines.chinese: 如果你们的确需要我和爸爸的帮助的话  …

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We know.
lines.chinese: 我们知道。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You know we'll be there for you.
lines.chinese: 你们知道我们是会伸出援手的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It's a Stewart tradition. We're a family.
lines.chinese: 这是 Stewart 家的传统。我们是一家人。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Is that you, Susan?
lines.chinese: 是你吗,Susan?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's me, Grandpa.
lines.chinese: 是我,爷爷。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Am I glad to see you!
lines.chinese: 我看到你真的很高兴!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: And am I glad to see you! I am also glad to be here.
lines.chinese: 我看见你才高兴呢! 我很高兴来这儿。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: How are things?
lines.chinese: 近况如何?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I have been talking to a group of salesmen since ten this morning, and I'm real exhausted.
lines.chinese: 我一直在与一群推销人员谈话 从今天上午十点起, 我实在是精疲力竭了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Well, you look good. What's Harry doing tonight?
lines.chinese: 嗯,你看起来蛮好的。 今晚做什么?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: He and Michelle are visiting relatives in New Jersey today.
lines.chinese: 他和 Michelle 今天去 New Jersey拜访亲戚去了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: The rest of our family went to the movies. So it's just you and me, Susan.
lines.chinese: 我们家的其余的人都去看电影了。 所以这儿只有你和我,Susan。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's nice to be alone with you, Grandpa. We don't get to see enough of each other.
lines.chinese: 很高兴单独与你在一起,爷爷。 我们在一起的时间太少了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Oh, I feel the same way, Susan. I miss seeing you. But to tell the truth, next time I'd like to go into the city and meet you there, instead of you coming here.
lines.chinese: 噢,我也有同样的感受,Susan。 我很想见到你  。 但是老实说, 下一次 我想到城里看你, 而不是到这儿来。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: You don't need to do that, Grandpa.
lines.chinese: 你不用这样,爷爷。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yeah, I do. I need to get out more. Well, I mean there's a lot to do around the house, and I love being here with the family, you know, but I'm restless. Since I retired, I've got extra time on my hands.
lines.chinese: 不,我要这样。 我需要多出门去。 我的意思是 这屋里有很多事可做, 而我也喜欢和家人在一起, 你知道的, 但我闲不住。 自从我退休以后, 我有过多空闲时间。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I understand, Grandpa.
lines.chinese: 我能体会,爷爷。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I think you do. Frankly, I'd like to use my brain a little more.
lines.chinese: 我想你能体会。 坦白地说, 我想多用用我的脑子。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Grandpa, you have so much energy and so many years of experience. There are probably a lot of places for you to work. Particularly in the construction field.
lines.chinese: 爷爷, 你精力这么好又有这么多年的经验。 也许有很多地方可以让你去工作。 特别是在建筑界。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: But at my age, I'm not looking for a full-time job. I'm retired. But I'm bored.
lines.chinese: 但是像我这般年纪, 我不会去找一个上全天班的工作。 我退休了。 但我感到无聊。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Well, there must be something. Maybe I can help.
lines.chinese: 嗯,一定可以找到事做. 也许我能帮忙。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Let's go into the kitchen, and maybe can help me set the table.
lines.chinese: 我们到厨房去, 也许你能帮我摆桌子。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Sounds good to me. What are we having?
lines.chinese: 我来。 我们吃些什么?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I prepared lamb chops, mashed potatoes, and a tossed green salad to begin with.
lines.chinese: 我准备了羊肉, 马铃薯泥,还有蔬菜色拉当头道菜。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Grandpa, you are a terrific guy!
lines.chinese: 爷爷,你真了不起!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: You're still thinking about something to do, aren't you? A job of some kind.
lines.chinese: 你还在想要找点事做,是吗? 一些工作。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: That's right. I've been thinking about it for weeks now .There must be some way to put my mind to good use.
lines.chinese: 是的。 为这事我想了几个星期。 总会有办法让我发挥才能的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: We'll find a solution. A positive solution to your finding a way to use that wonderful mind of yours.
lines.chinese: 我们会找到解决的办法。 一个好法子 能够让你施展你的学识。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Sam, would you come in ,please?
lines.chinese: 请进来一下,好吗?

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: You sound like something's brothering you, Susan. The sketches for the cover of the new doll book?
lines.chinese: 看起来好像你有什么心思,Susan。 是新玩具书的封面设计?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: That's not it. Please sit down.
lines.chinese: 不是这种事。 请坐下。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Sure.
lines.chinese: 好的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I need your advice on a personal matter, but it's not about me.
lines.chinese: 我有件私事需要听听你的高见, 但这不是关于我自己的事情。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: You need my advice on a personal matter, and it's not about. OK.
lines.chinese: 有私事需要听我的意见, 而且不是关于你自己的。好的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's about my grandfather.
lines.chinese: 是关于我爷爷的。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: What's the problem?
lines.chinese: 什么事?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It won't sound like a big deal, but it is. I had dinner with him Saturday, and he's very unhappy about not working.
lines.chinese: 这事听起来没啥严重的, 但实际上很要紧。 星期六我与他一起吃晚, 他对没有工作感到闷闷不乐。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: I thought he was retired and pleased to be living with the family.
lines.chinese: 我原以为他退休了 乐于跟家人住在一起。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: He is, but there's so much energy and talent in the man ,and he doesn't get to use it.
lines.chinese: 没错, 但是他有那么多的精力和才能, 却又用不上。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: But what can I do? What kind of advice are you looking for?
lines.chinese: 但我能帮什么忙? 希望能得到什么建议?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Simply this. John Marchetta runs this company.
lines.chinese: 简单说来是这样的。 经营这家公司。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: He founded this company.
lines.chinese: 他创办了这家公司。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Right. John Marchetta Gave me my start here six years ago, when I first graduated from college. He gave me the chance to use my talents and made me feel more confident.
lines.chinese: 对。 六年前, John Marchetta让我在这儿起步 那时我刚从大学毕业。 他提供我施展才能的机会 使我对自己更加有信心。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Right. Maybe he can do the same thing for your grandfather.
lines.chinese: 是的。 也许他能为你爷爷做同样的事。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Or at least give him some advice.
lines.chinese: 再不然至少可以给他一些忠告。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Right. Then I've solved your problem.
lines.chinese: 对。 那我替你解决问题了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I can always depend on you, Sam.
lines.chinese: 我知道我可以依靠你, Sam。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: I'm glad to help. Shall I call Mr. Marchetta for you?
lines.chinese: 我很乐意帮忙。 要我替你打电话给 Marchetta先生吗?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: No, no. I'll do that. Thanks.
lines.chinese: 不,不。我自己来。 谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Now, how's the Stewart family?
lines.chinese: 那么,Stewart 全家人怎么样?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Fine, thank you, Mr. Marchetta. Except for my grandfather.
lines.chinese: 都好,谢谢你,Mrarchetta 先生。 除了我爷爷。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: What's wrong, Susan? What's wrong with him?
lines.chinese: 怎么啦,Susan? 他怎么啦?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: He needs to work. In fact, that is the reason why I'm here to see you. I know you're building a new factory, and I thought...may be...my grandfather is so experienced in the construction trade, he could be so valuable.
lines.chinese: 他需要工作。 事实上,这是我来见你的原因。 我知道你在兴建一座厂房,我觉得……也许…… 我爷爷 在建筑这一行业经验丰富, 他或许能够有用。

lines.speaker: Sam
lines.english: Tell him to come and see me at ten 0'clock tomorrow morning. I have an idea that may solve the problem for him and help a lot of other people.
lines.chinese: 告诉他来见我 明天上午十点钟。 我有个主意也许能解决这个问题 且对其他很多人有益。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Really, Mr.Marchetta? Can I tell him that?
lines.chinese: 真的吗,Marchetta 先生? 我可以把这消息告诉他?

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: Sure can. Ten 0'clock in the morning. Here.
lines.chinese: 当然可以。 上午十点,在这儿。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, thank you!
lines.chinese: 噢,谢谢你！

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Hi. I'm Malcolm Stewart.
lines.chinese: 嗨。我是 Malcolm Stewart。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: John Marchetta. Sit down, sit down.
lines.chinese: 我是 John Marchetta。 请坐,请坐。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Susan has told me a great deal about you.She says you're quite a man.
lines.chinese: 告诉我许多关于你的事。 她说你是个了不起的人。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: She says a lot of wonderful things about you too, Mr. Stewart.
lines.chinese: 她也谈起 不少你的非凡作为,Stewart先生。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: That's always nice to hear, Mr.Marchetta.
lines.chinese: 听到这些话总是好的,Marchetta 先生。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: Call me John. May I call you Malcolm?
lines.chinese: 叫我 John 。 我能称你 Malcolm  吗?

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: Let's talk business.
lines.chinese: 我们来谈谈正经事。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: That's music to my ears.
lines.chinese: 正合我意。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: I understand you used to be in the construction business.
lines.chinese: 听说 你过去在建筑界工作。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yup. Forty-three years. Here's a brief description of forty three years of on-the-job training.
lines.chinese: 是的,四十三年。 这是一份简单的记录 关于我四十三年的工作经历。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: That is some history! You're a valuable asset, Malcolm. Very valuable.
lines.chinese: 这阅历了不得! 你是很有价值的资产, Malcolm。 很有价值。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Thank you. Yup. Forty-three years. Half that time in my own construction company. Big jobs-factories, shopping malls. That kind of thing.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。 是的。四十三年。 一半时间是在我自己的建筑公司。大的工程----工厂,  大型购物中心。 等等。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: Then you retired.
lines.chinese: 然后你退休了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yes. After my wife died, and I felt I should spend more time with my children and grandchildren. I live in Florida, and they lived in New York.
lines.chinese: 是的。 我妻子去世后, 我觉得应该多花些时间 与儿子孙子们在一起。 我住在 Florida, 而他们住在纽约。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: I understand. My daughter Cami lives in NewYork. I like being near her.
lines.chinese: 我了解。 我女儿 Cami 住在纽约。 我喜欢离她近一点。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: When I came here, I planned to take a few months off. Relax with the family and then look for some work. Put my experience on the line...but, unfortunately, there isn't any work for a retired person my age.
lines.chinese: 当我初到这儿, 我计划休息几个月。 与家人轻松一段时间然后找个工作。 考验我的阅历…… 但是,很不幸, 没有适合我这种年纪的退休老人的工作。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: Sometimes there is, and sometimes there isn't. Well, I'm evolved with an organization, and we're trying to resolve that problem.
lines.chinese: 有时候有的, 有时候没有。 嗯,我参加一个组织, 试图解决这类问题。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: What's that?
lines.chinese: 什么组织?

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: TOPS. T-O-P-S- means Talented Older People's Society.
lines.chinese: 全名是优秀老人协会。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I'd like to be a member. How much are the dues?
lines.chinese: 我想加入做为会员。 会费多少?

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: There are no dues...The organization serves major men and women like you. Experienced, talented, retired. But our members want to go out there and use their talents. They want to work.
lines.chinese: 不要会费。 这个组织为本市的各大公司服务。 为什么? 因为这个组织的会员都是像你这样的人。 有经验,有才能,也都退休了。 但会员们希望在社会上 贡献他们的才能。 他们想工作。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: That fantastic, John!
lines.chinese: 那太好了,John!

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: I've got and idea for you, Malcolm. Just fill out this form for me. It'll only take a few minutes. Sit right here, and do it while I talk to my secretary. When I get back, we'll talk about my new factory. My company is a member of TOPS. So I try hard to find opportunities for people like it. Well, I can use your brainpower on the job right now. Have you got time this morning to go over to the construction site with me? I'd like to have you meet my foreman-get some background on the job.
lines.chinese: 我倒替你想出了一个主意,Malcolm。 请把这份表填好。 只要几分钟时间。就坐在这儿填, 我去和秘书说个话。 我回来后,我们谈一谈我的新工厂。 我的公司也是优秀老人协会的会员。 所以我努力为像你这样的人寻找机会。 而且当我一看到机会, 我就可以处理。 嗯,我马上可以将你的才智用在这项工程上。 今天上午你有时间 去看一看建筑工地吗? 我希望带你去认识工头--- 了解这项工程的一些背景。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I've got plenty of time.
lines.chinese: 我有的是时间。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: I'll be right back. We'll go over to the job site together.
lines.chinese: 我马上就回来。 我们等一会儿一起去工地。

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.english: Malcolm, you worked on the Spaceport project?
lines.chinese: 你曾负责太空港的工程?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: My company was the contractor. I built the theater there with my own two hands, practically.
lines.chinese: 我的公司承包了这项工程。 我建造这个剧院 差不多是独自完成的。

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.english: I understand. Marchrtta： Well, I'm glad to see you two guys getting along so well because Danny, Malcolm is on the TOPS team. He's going to be working with you for a while. His experience will be valuable to both of us.
lines.chinese: 我明白了。好的, 看到你们两位相处得这么融洽,我很高兴 因为,Danny, 是 TOPS 的一员。 他将与你一起工作一段时间。 他的经验对你我都很有好处。

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.english: Welcome aboard, Malcolm!
lines.chinese: 欢迎加入,Malcolm。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: I'm going back to my office. Give me a call later, Malcolm. I'll tell you the time and date of the next TOPS meeting. I'd like you to meet the group.
lines.chinese: 我要回办公室了。 请稍后打电话给我,Malcolm。 我要告诉你TOPS下次聚会的时间。 我希望你能与协会的成员见面。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I will, John. And again-thanks.
lines.chinese: 我会的,John。 再次谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Marchetta
lines.english: No...thank you. And thank Susan.
lines.chinese: 不……谢谢你。 还要谢谢 Susan。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: He's quite a man.
lines.chinese: 他是个了不起的人。

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.english: A real inspiration for me.
lines.chinese: 他给我很大的启发。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: OK, Danny. I know you didn't expect to have me around, but I think I can be of some help to you.
lines.chinese: 好啦,Danny。 我知道你没有预料到我出现在你身边, 但我觉得我可以对你有所帮助。

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.english: Let me tell you something, Malcolm. With your background and experience, I can learn something...and I do need some advice on a difficult problem. Let me show you this.
lines.chinese: 我跟你说,Malcolm。 依你的背景和经验, 我能学到一些东西。我的确有一个难题想请教你。 我给你看看这个。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I don't want to give you a final opinion without studying these building plans more carefully. But a simple solution might be to move the air-conditioning units instead of redesigning the entire system. It might be simpler and less expensive.
lines.chinese: 我不想给你最终的意见 在没有仔细研究这些建筑图的情况下。 但是一个简单的解决方法可能是 移动这些空调设备 而不必重新设计整个系统。 这样可能比较简单而且省钱。

lines.speaker: Danny
lines.english: You just earned your weight in gold, Malcolm. Welcome aboard!
lines.chinese: 你可帮了我大忙,Malcolm。 欢迎加入我们的行列!

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: It all happened so quickly! I can't believe it!
lines.chinese: 这一切发生得太快了! 我简直不敢相信!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I'm glad Mr. Marchetta was so helpful.
lines.chinese: 我真高兴 Marchetta 先生如此帮忙。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: He was more than helpful. He actually took me to meet his foreman.
lines.chinese: 他不仅仅是帮忙。 事实上他带着我去见他的工头。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I'm thrilled for you, Grandpa.
lines.chinese: 我真为你兴奋,爷爷。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I don't know how to thank you, Susan. You're a wonderful granddaughter.
lines.chinese: 我不知道如何感谢你,Susan。 你真是个好孙女。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's good to see you so happy.
lines.chinese: 能看见你如此高兴,我太快乐了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I'll be at the construction site tomorrow. What are you doing tomorrow night?
lines.chinese: 明天我要去工地。 明天晚上做什么?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I'm not doing anything. Why?
lines.chinese: 我没有要做什么。 怎样?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: How about a date with your grandfather? I owe you a good steak dinner.
lines.chinese: 和你爷爷约会好吗? 我欠你一顿牛排晚餐。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I'll accept. Tomorrow night. You and me. Dinner. What time?
lines.chinese: 我接受邀请。 明天晚上。 你和我。吃晚饭。 什么时候?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I'll pick you up here at seven. Is that OK?
lines.chinese: 我七点钟到这儿来接你。 可以吗?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I can't wait! And you can tell me all about your first full day back on the job.
lines.chinese: 我盼望着那个时候! 到时候你就可以告诉我` 关于你重回工作第一天的全部事情了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: It's a deal!
lines.chinese: 就这么约定!

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: There's nothing more joyous than the arrival of a new baby.
lines.chinese: 没有任何事能比新生儿的到来更令人喜悦了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I am so excited, Mother! Just imagine-Marilyn and Richard must be thrilled! Oh, a new baby!
lines.chinese: 我好兴奋,妈妈! 想像得出 Marilyn 和 Richard 一定乐坏了! 噢,一个新生儿!

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Max...Max...Max. Oh, it's a sweet-sounding name for a sweet little boy. My first grandchild.
lines.chinese: 噢,好一个甜美的名字配个甜美的小孩。 我的第一个孙子。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: And my first nephew. Isn't he just adorable? He looks a lot like you, Mom. He does.
lines.chinese: 我的第一个侄子。 他真是太可爱了! 他看起来像你,妈妈。他是的。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Do you think so? Well, I guess. He does look a lot like Richard, and I guess he looks a lot like me. Oh, he's got Richard's eyes, though.
lines.chinese: 你真的这么认为? 嗯,有点像。他的确很像 Richard, 我想他看起来也蛮像我。噢,他的眼睛跟 Richard 的像极了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I really want Harry and Michelle to see Max.
lines.chinese: 我真希望 Harry和 Michelle 来看看 Max。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: When are they coming?
lines.chinese: 他们什么时候来?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Tomorrow. Harry has an account to work on today. Yes, he does have Richard's eyes. Big blue eyes. The baby even looks at you like Richard does.
lines.chinese: 明天。Harry今天有项会计工作要做。 是的,他的眼睛很像 Richard。蓝色的大眼睛。这婴儿看起来很像 Richard 小时侯那样。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Well, children usually referable their parents.
lines.chinese: 嗯,孩子总是像父亲的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's true Michelle is a lot like Harry in so many ways. And she's shy with new people, just like he is.
lines.chinese: 对。 在许多地方很像 Harry。 她见了生人就害羞,与他一样。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You really like Michelle, don't you?
lines.chinese: 你的确很喜欢 Michelle,是吗?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Yes. I'm very fond of her.
lines.chinese: 是的。我很喜欢她。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: And Harry, too?
lines.chinese: 也喜欢 Harry，是吗?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Well...

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Uh, it's four-thirty. Oh, my! Marilyn and Richard will be home from the hospital any minute, and we must prepare this room.
lines.chinese: 啊,已经四点半了。天啦! 和 Richard 随时都会从医院回来。 我们得收拾好房间。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Where will we put all the presents?
lines.chinese: 我们把这些礼物放在哪儿?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Well, let's take everything to the living room. Marilyn and Richard and the baby need the space. It's crowded in here.
lines.chinese: 嗯,我们把东西都放到客厅去。 和 Richard 及婴儿需要空间。这房间太挤了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: The welcome sign is up： "Welcome home, Max."
lines.chinese: 欢迎你回家来,Max 。”

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Isn't it exciting, Grandpa?
lines.chinese: 真令人兴奋,是吗,爷爷?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Your first great-grandchild.
lines.chinese: 你的第一个曾孙。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yes. Yes, sir. A great-grandchild. A great-grandson. Another generation to carry on the Stewart name.
lines.chinese: 是的。是的。 一个曾孙。  一个曾孙。 这是承继 Stewart家家族香火的新一代。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I love you, Grandpa. You make me feel so proud to be part of our family.
lines.chinese: 我爱你,爷爷。 你让我以成为我们家庭的一员而自豪。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: One day, You'll have your own family, and I'll be proud to be part of it.
lines.chinese: 总有一天,你会有自己的家庭的, 那时我会以成为其中一份子而自豪。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Now you understand my feelings, Susan. I'm Grandpa's daughter-in-law, but I feel like a Stewart. He's always made me feel like his own daughter.
lines.chinese: 现在你能理解我的感受了,Susan。 我是爷爷的儿媳父,但我觉得我是 Stewart 家的一员。他总是让我觉得我是他的亲生女儿。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Well, that's because you're so much like us-wonderful!
lines.chinese: 嗯, 那是因为你太像我们了---太好了!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Mom, got it. This will make a nice gift for Marilyn and dates and information about Max's life here. Let's see. Name： Max Stewart. Does he have a middle name?
lines.chinese: 妈妈,找到了。 这是给 Richard和 Marilyn 的一件好礼物。 他们可以记录下这个重要的日子 关于 Max 在这儿的生活。姓名:Max Stewart。 他有中间名字吗?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: No, just Max. I like that. No middle name. No middle initial.
lines.chinese: 没有,就是 Max。我喜欢这样。 没有中间的名字。没有中间的字母。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Like me. I'm Malcolm Stewart. Just Malcolm Stewart.
lines.chinese: 像我一样。我叫 Malcolm Stewart。 就是 Malcolm Stewart。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: And Max has your initials, Grandpa：M.S.
lines.chinese: 的名字开头字母与你的一样,爷爷,都是 M.S.

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Uh, it must mean something.
lines.chinese: 噢,这很有意思。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Weight： eight pounds six ounces.
lines.chinese: 体重:八磅六盎司。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Eight-six. Big boy! All the Stewart men were big.
lines.chinese: 八磅六。是个大婴儿! 家的男人都大。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Well, Robbie was eight pounds two ounces, and Richard was eight pounds three.
lines.chinese: 嗯,Robbie生下来八磅二盎司, 生下来时八磅三。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: And me?
lines.chinese: 我呢?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Eight pounds six. You were big, just like Max.
lines.chinese: 八磅六。 你很重,像Max 一样。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Eight pounds six, just like me. That's nice. Length. Length?
lines.chinese: 八磅六,跟我一样。 太好了。身高多少?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Richard says Max is twenty-one inches long.
lines.chinese: 说 Max 是二十一英尺长。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Tall. All the Stewart men are tall.
lines.chinese: 高。Stewart 家的男人都高。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Well, Grandpa, you're about five-nine or five-ten. I wouldn't call that tall.
lines.chinese: 嗯，爷爷。你大概是五十九或者五十。 我不觉得这样算高。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I take after my mother's family. They were...they were... they were average.
lines.chinese: 我像我母亲家的人。 他们……他们……他们是普通身材。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Mother：Marily.Father：Richard. And lots of pages for Richard's photos of Max.
lines.chinese: 母亲：Marilyn。父亲：Richard。 有好多页可用来贴 Richard给 Max 拍的照片。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Speaking of mother and of father-and speaking of Max hear the car. They're here!
lines.chinese: 谈到孩子的爸和孩子的妈 谈到小孩 Max---我听见汽车声了。 他们到了！

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh! Oh, quickly! Go, go, go!
lines.chinese: 噢，噢，赶快！走，走，走！

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Let's see. Oh, welcome home. Oh, let her in. Wait with your pictures for a second. Come on, darlings. Sweetheart... Sit down right here.
lines.chinese: 让我看看。噢  欢迎回家来。 噢，让她进来。 先别忙着你的摄影。来，亲爱的。 心肝宝贝……就坐这儿。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, he's so cute! Oh, Marilyn!
lines.chinese: 噢，他真逗人喜爱！噢，Marilyn！

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Max looks just like Grandpa.
lines.chinese: 长得真像爷爷。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: A real Stewart.
lines.chinese: 一个真正的 Stewart 家后代。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I'm so happy to be home with my family-and with Max.
lines.chinese: 我真高兴回到家和我的家庭----还有和 Max 在一起。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's almost ten 0'clock. I've got to go. Tomorrow is Monday, and work begins at eight in the morning for me. Oh, I'm so happy that Max is home. He's the sweetest little thing.
lines.chinese: 差不多十点了。 我得走了。明天是星期一。 早晨八点我就得开始工作。 噢，我真高兴 Max 回到家了。 他是最可爱的小家伙。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I'll drive you to Grand Grandpa, Robbie, and I can finish wrapping all these gifts.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你，Philip。 那么爷爷，Robbie， 和我可以把这些礼物包装完。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It's so good to have you home again...and to see Max asleep in his bassinet at home with us.
lines.chinese: 真高兴又回家了...... 真高兴看到 Max 和我们一起睡在家里的摇篮。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: To be with our family and all that Stewart TLC.
lines.chinese: 真高兴和全家人在一起，受到 Stewart 家族无微不至的照顾。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: TLC-tender loving care. That's our motto.
lines.chinese: 温暖的爱护和关怀。 这是我们的格言。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Did you see the washcloth and the towels with the teddy bears on them? Alexandra and the Molinas sent them for max.
lines.chinese: 你看见浴巾 上面有泰迪绒毛玩具熊的？ 那是 Alexandra 和 Molina 全家送给 Max 的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It was so kind of them. Now Max has come into everyone's life.
lines.chinese: 他们太客气了。 现在 Max 走进了每个人的生活。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: The house is so alive with him here. The welcome sign over the door. The boxes of presents. The M-Z-X over his bassinet. Robbie put that there. Susan's teddy bear. So cuddly. The beautiful crib from Mom and Dad.
lines.chinese: 有了他在这间屋显得生气蓬勃。 门上的欢迎标语。 那些礼物盒。 摇篮上的MAX字样。 这是 Robbie 弄的。 送的泰迪绒玩具熊。 抱起来真舒服。爸爸妈妈送的漂亮婴儿床。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, and Grandpa's baseball glove. You know, it hung over my crib, too. And it hung over Robbie's crib.
lines.chinese: 噢，还有爷爷的棒球手套。 要知道，我的小床也曾有过它。 的小床也有过。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Part of Grandpa's magic?
lines.chinese: 这是爷爷的魔法的一部分吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, that's not all. It hung over Susan's crib.
lines.chinese: 噢，还没完呢。 的小床也曾有过。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Part of Grandpa's magic?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, that's not all. It hung over Susan's crib.

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: The same baseball glove?
lines.chinese: 同一个棒球手套吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: That's right. Grandpa hangs it there for good luck. He says it always brought him good luck on the baseball team. He believes it'll bring good luck to all the Stewart babies.
lines.chinese: 是的。爷爷把它挂起以求好运。 他说当年他在棒球队时手套总是带给他好运。 他相信这手套会给 Stewart 家所有的婴儿带来好运。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And then he takes it back when Max is ready to use it?
lines.chinese: 然后他就把它收着 当 Max 长大能使用这手套时？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yes, and replaces it with a new glove so the old one will be ready for a new member of the Stewart family
lines.chinese: 是的， 用一个新的手套来换 而这个旧的又可准备 给 Stewart 家的新成员了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Grandpa really loves his family, doesn't he? So do I.
lines.chinese: 爷爷确实很爱这个家庭，是吗？ 我也一样。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And so do I.
lines.chinese: 我也一样。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And so does Max.
lines.chinese: 也一样。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: After he eats!
lines.chinese: 等他吃饱了！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thanks, Michelle.
lines.chinese: 谢谢，Michelle  。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: I hope you like it.
lines.chinese: 希望你喜欢。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Isn't this baby outfit adorable? With his name on it "Max." Thanks so much, Harry and Michelle.
lines.chinese: 这套婴儿装不是很可爱吗？ 有他的名字在上面“Ｍａｘ”。 太感谢了，Harry和 Michelle。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We really appreciate it.
lines.chinese: 我们实在很感激。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I'm glad you like it. Michelle Picked it out.
lines.chinese: 我很高兴你们喜欢它。 是Michelle 选的。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Yes. I told Daddy to pick blue ones. Blue is for boys, and pink is for girls.
lines.chinese: 是的。我告诉爸爸买蓝色的。 蓝色是男孩子的，而红色是女孩子的。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: And Max is some boy.
lines.chinese: 是个好男孩。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: He's a real Stewart.
lines.chinese: 他是个十足的 Stewart 家的人。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Right!
lines.chinese: 对。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: That was so thoughtful of you, Michelle. Especially to pick it out in blue.
lines.chinese: 想得真周到，Michelle。 还特地选了一件蓝色的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Would you like to see baby Max, Michelle?
lines.chinese: 想看看Max 这小家伙吗，Michelle ?

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Could she? Could we?
lines.chinese: 她能看吗？我们能看吗？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Could I?
lines.chinese: 我能看一看吗？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Take them upstairs, Susan. Harry and Michelle can watch Max sleeping.
lines.chinese: 带他们上楼去，Susan。 和 Michelle 可以去看，Max在睡觉。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Let's go.
lines.chinese: 我们走。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Come on, Michelle. Before Max wakes up.
lines.chinese: 来吧，Michelle。 趁Max 还没有醒来。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Will you please take this upstairs, Susan?
lines.chinese: 麻烦你把这个带上楼，好吗，Susan  ？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Uh-hum.
lines.chinese: 好--好。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Michelle is very grown up for a ten-year-old, huh?
lines.chinese: 以一个十岁的孩子看，Michelle 很懂事，是吗？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: She's smart and sensitive for her age.
lines.chinese: 就这样的年龄来说她很聪明而且懂事。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Growing up without a mother is difficult. You mature quickly.
lines.chinese: 在没有母亲的情况下长大不简单。 这种人成熟得快。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Susan's like a mother to Michelle. They have a good relationship. Do you think Susan and Harry will get married?
lines.chinese: 就像是 Michelle 的母亲。 他们关系不错。 你觉得 Susan和Harry会结婚吗？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Yeah. You can count on it.
lines.chinese: 当然。你等着瞧吧。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: I think so. Yes. They get along so well.
lines.chinese: 我也觉得是这样。他们相处得很好。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I like him. He's good for Susan.
lines.chinese: 我喜欢他。他很适合 Susan。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: He's a little quiet.
lines.chinese: 他有点沉静。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It's hard to do anything but listen in this family.
lines.chinese: 在这个家庭除了听，别人还能怎样。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: How can anybody get a word in around here?
lines.chinese: 在这儿谁有机会插话呢？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: You're right, Ellen.
lines.chinese: 你说得对，Ellen。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Oh, really?
lines.chinese: 噢，是吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It's the way it should be. The Stewarts are the Stewarts!
lines.chinese: 事情本该如此。 这就是 Stewart 家人的本性！

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: They always were, and they always will be.
lines.chinese: 他们过去如此， 将来也永远会如此。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Right!
lines.chinese: 对！

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: They've always got an opinion. Always got something to say.
lines.chinese: 他们总有自己的看法。 总有话要说。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And now there's Max Stewart, and if he talks as loudly as he cries, we're all in for trouble.
lines.chinese: 现在又加上 Max Stewart。 如果他说话像他哭那样大声， 我们都会吃不消的。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: He's quiet now.
lines.chinese: 他现在很安静。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Mmm-hmm.That's because he's sleeping.
lines.chinese: 嗯。那是因为他睡着。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: He's really cute.
lines.chinese: 他真是逗人喜爱。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's not necessary to whisper, Harry. A baby gets used to voices.
lines.chinese: 没有必要压低音，Harry。 婴儿习惯各种声音。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I remember now. We always whispered when Michelle was born.
lines.chinese: 我现在记起来了。 当 Michelle 出生的时候我们总是压低嗓门说话。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: And I didn't sleep well. Daddy told me. I never slept. And when I did, I woke up when I heard someone speak.
lines.chinese: 而我总是睡不好，爸爸告诉我的。 我总睡不着。 就算我睡着了， 一听到有人说话我就醒了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I bet you were cute.
lines.chinese: 我打赌当时你很逗人喜爱。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: She sure was.
lines.chinese: 她小时候很逗人喜爱。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Not as cute as Max. He's like a little doll.
lines.chinese: 不如Max 这么逗人喜爱。 他像个小洋娃娃。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Oh, good. Now I can help diaper him.
lines.chinese: 噢，好了。 我现在可以帮他换尿布了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Let's get Marilyn. What do you do when he cries like that?
lines.chinese: 还是叫Marilyn 来吧。 像他哭得这个样子你们怎么办？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: You pick him up.
lines.chinese: 把他抱起来呀。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: He's so little...so new. Let's call Marilyn.
lines.chinese: 他这么小……这么清新。 我们还是叫 Marilyn来吧。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Time for a feeding and time for a diapering. You're a real Stewart.
lines.chinese: 是到了喂奶，换尿布的时候了。 你是个真正的 Stewart 家后代。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: You like living in New York, don't you?
lines.chinese: 你喜欢住在纽约,是吗？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, I love it. It's so convenient. I can take the bus to work...or the subway...or a taxi. And there's so much to do. Lots of movie houses, and the theater.
lines.chinese: 噢，我喜欢住在纽约。 一切都很方便。 我可以坐公共汽车上班…… 或者搭地铁…… 或者坐计程车。而且这儿总是有许多事可做。 很多电影院，还有剧院。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I know what you mean. I'd like to live in the city, but living in New Jersey and the suburbs is better for Michelle. Trees, grass.
lines.chinese: 我懂得你的意思。 我也喜欢住在这。 但住在 New Jersey和郊区 对 Michelle 会好些。 有绿树，有草地。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: There's a lot of good things about suburban living. I grew up in Riverdale, Remember? So I know. But, as a working woman, I think New York has all the conveniences-including the best tomatoes.
lines.chinese: 郊区生活是有许多优点。 你记得吗，我就是在 Riverdale 长大的？ 所以我了解这些。 但是就职业妇女来讲， 我觉得纽约具备了一切便利的条件。 包括上好的西红柿。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: The truth is, I'd like to live in the city. Michelle's the right age. There are lots of things for her here.
lines.chinese: 老实说我也想住到城里来。 正处于成长的年龄。 这里有许多适合她的东西。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: You're right, Harry. Today is the perfect example. Michelle and her friends are at the aquarium in Brooklyn. They come back here for lunch, then go uptown to the Museum of Natural History. There's so much for young people to see and do. It's just incredible!
lines.chinese: 你说的对，Harry。 今天就是一个很好的例子。 和她的朋友们在参观 Brooklyn 的水族馆。他们回到这儿来吃午饭。 然后再往北去自然历史博物馆。 这儿有很多可供年轻人看和做的事情。 简直是难以置信！

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Not just for young people. What about me? I've never been to the aquarium or Museum of Natural History. Have you?
lines.chinese: 不仅仅对年轻人来说是这样。 对我还不是一样？ 我从来没有去过水族馆 或自然历史博物馆。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh yes, Harry. My mother and father often took us somewhere in the city on the weekends. Dad was a busy doctor, but he usually managed to squeeze a Sunday in with Richard, Robbie, and me. I used to love to go to the Metropolitan Museum of Art.
lines.chinese: 你呢？ 去过了，Harry。 我父母 经常在周末带我们去城里的一些地方。爸爸是个很忙的医生， 但是他总会设法挤出星期天 和 Richard， Robbie及我在一起。 我过去很喜欢去大都会美术馆。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I've been there several times. Twice with Michelle.
lines.chinese: 我到那儿去过好几次。 其中两次是和 Michelle 去的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: You ought to think about spending more time with Michelle at all the great places in the city.
lines.chinese: 你应当考虑多花点时间陪 Michelle 去城里所有的好地方看看。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, perhaps, you'll help me select some of the great places. And perhaps, you'll join us?
lines.chinese: 好的，也许， 你能帮我挑一些好地方。 也许，你能跟我们一起去？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Perhaps I will.
lines.chinese: 也许我会吧。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Well, there we are .All set for lunch, Harry.
lines.chinese: 好了，就这样了。 午餐的一切都准备好了，Harry。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: It looks inviting. I wish Michelle and her friends would get here. I'm starving, aren't you?
lines.chinese: 它看起来很令人食欲大开。 我希望Michelle 和她的朋友很快就到这儿。 我饿了，你呢？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I can't wait to take a bite of the pumpernickel. It smells so delicious. Coming! Coming! Who is it?
lines.chinese: 我简直迫不及待想吃一口黑面包。 它闻起来好香啊。 来了！来了！ 是谁呀？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Michelle. It's us, Susan.
lines.chinese: 是我们，Susan。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Come in Michelle, and bring your friends along. We're on the fifth floor. Oh you're been here before
lines.chinese: 请进，Michelle。 请带你的朋友们进来。 我们在五楼。 噢，你以前来过的。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Susan, I really appreciate your doing this Michelle and her friends.
lines.chinese: 我真的很感谢 你为 Michelle 和她的朋友们所做的这些。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, please, Harry. It's nothing. I'm not just doing it for Michelle. I'm doing it for you.
lines.chinese: 噢，别客气，Harry。 这没什么。 我不仅是为 Michelle 做这些。 也是为你做的。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I'm doing it for us, Harry.
lines.chinese: 我是为我们大家做的，Harry。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, it's...it's important for Michelle to see us together more often. That's true.
lines.chinese: 是的， 让Michelle 看到我们更常在一起是……是很重要的。 确实是这样。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's important for Michelle and me to get to know each other better. That's important for us.
lines.chinese: 这是很重要的。 让 Michelle和我彼此更了解。 这对我们很重要。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: That makes me feel good.
lines.chinese: 那使我感到很高兴。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: What, Harry? What makes you feel good?
lines.chinese: 什么，Harry？ 什么使你感到高兴？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, that you care about Michelle, that you care about me, and that you care about us.
lines.chinese: 哦，我是说你关心 Michelle， 关心我 ，而且 关心我们大家。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Well, Harry, that's because I do. I do care.
lines.chinese: 哦，Harry  ，那是因为我关心。 我真的关心。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: They'll be here any minute .Susan, I'd like to continue this conversation later.
lines.chinese: 他们马上就到了。 我希望等一下能继续我们的谈话。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Of course, Harry. We'll finish the conversation when they go to the museum.
lines.chinese: 当然可以  ，Harry。 他们去博物馆之后我们再聊。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I'd like that.
lines.chinese: 我很乐意。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: So would I.
lines.chinese: 我也是。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Oh, Daddy! We had such a good time at the aquarium. I saw a real shark. I could almost touch it.
lines.chinese: 噢，爸爸！ 我们在水族馆玩得真开心。 我看到一条真正的鲨鱼。 我几乎可以摸到它。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I'm so glad. Hi, Michelle, why don't you introduce everybody to Susan?
lines.chinese: 我真高兴。 嗨，Michelle。 何不将她们每一位介绍给 Susan。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Hi, Susan.
lines.chinese: 嗨  ，Susan。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Hi.

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: I'd like you to meet Audrey and her mother, Mrs. Cooper.
lines.chinese: 我来向你介绍 Audrey和她母亲，Cooper太太。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Hello, Audrey. Nice to meet you, Mrs. Cooper. I'm Susan Stewart.
lines.chinese: 哈，Audrey。 很高兴见到你 Cooper太太。 我是 Susan Stewart。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: And this is Shirley and Nicole.
lines.chinese: 这是 Shirley  和 Nicole。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Hi, girls.
lines.chinese: 嗨 ，女孩们好。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Come on in. Come on in, Mrs. Cooper on in, everybody.
lines.chinese: 请进。 请进  ，Cooper太太。 大家请进来。

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.english: The aquarium was so exciting. I had never been there before. The girls learned a great deal. Frankly, so did I.
lines.chinese: 水族馆真的很好玩。 我以前从来没有去过那儿。 女孩们学到了许多东西。坦白说，我也是。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Well, please sit down and tell us all about it during lunch.
lines.chinese: 嗯  ，请坐下来 一面吃午餐，一面告诉我们一切。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: We prepared some tuna fish and cheese sandwiches for lunch.
lines.chinese: 我们准备了金枪鱼 和奶酪三明治当午餐。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: I love tuna fish. Don't you , Audrey?
lines.chinese: 我喜欢吃金枪鱼。 喜欢吗， Audrey ？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I remember when I used to b a Girl Scout. My mother would take us everywhere, too.
lines.chinese: 我记得我还是一名女童子军的时候。 我妈妈也是带我们各处走。

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.english: Susan, I understand you're in the toy business.
lines.chinese: 听说你是在玩具公司工作。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Yes, I am. My company manufactures toys and games for children.
lines.chinese: 是的。 我们公司专为孩子们生产玩具和游戏器具。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Susan's vice-president in charge of new toys and games.
lines.chinese: 是主管新玩具和游戏器具的副总裁。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: And the marketing of new toys and games.
lines.chinese: 还有新玩具和游戏器具的营销。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Can we test a new game for you, Susan?
lines.chinese: 我们可以帮你试验新游戏吗，Susan？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: That's an excellent idea, Michelle. I happen to have a game which I brought home to study.
lines.chinese: 这是个好主意，Michelle。 我这儿碰巧有一个 我带回来研究的游戏。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Let us try it ! Don't you want to play? Nicole ? Shirley? Audrey?
lines.chinese: 让我们试试吧！ 你们想玩吗？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: We tested it last week on twelve-to fifteen-year-olds, and they found it to be too easy-in other words...boring. We think it might be just right for ten-to twelve-year- olds.
lines.chinese: 我们上一次曾在十二岁至十五岁的孩子中试过这项游戏， 他们发现字太简单--- 也就是说---无聊。 我们想它或许对十岁到十二岁大的孩子正合适。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: That's us, Susan.
lines.chinese: 那正是我们，Susan。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: First, you shuffle the deck and lay them face down. Then you select the leader. I'll be the leader. The leader takes the first ten cards and lays them face up on this stand. h-t-e-r-c-z-e-p-e-s. Everyone gets a turn, going counterclockwise, left to right. You have thirty seconds to make a word, using as many letters as possible. You get one point for each letter, plus the person with the longest word gets ten extra point. The first one to get one hundred points wins.
lines.chinese: 首先，你得洗一下牌 然后将牌盖住。 然后，你们选出指挥。 我来当指挥。 指挥拿出上面的十张 将它们掀开放在这个架子上。 每一个人按逆时针方向 从左到右轮流玩。 你们有三十秒的时间造一个词， 尽可能多用字。 一个字母可得一分， 所造的字最长的人多得十分。 首先达到一百分的人赢。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: That's easy.
lines.chinese: 那太容易了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: OK, let's go around the atble. You first, Audrey.
lines.chinese: 好，让我们顺着桌子来。 你先来 ，Audrey。

lines.speaker: Audrey
lines.english: Chest-c-h-e-s-t.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Good. That's five points also. It's your turn, Shirley.
lines.chinese: 好。 也是五分。 轮到你了，Shirley。

lines.speaker: Shirley
lines.english: Creep-c-r-e-e-p.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Creep. That's another five-letter word. Five points. So far, you're all tied.
lines.chinese: 又是五个字母的字。 五分。 到现在为止，你们不分胜负。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Sheet -s-h-e-e-t-sheet.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Sheet is a five-letter word. Michelle, your turn.
lines.chinese: 是五个字母的单字 该你了。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Cheese-c-h-e-e-s-e.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Terrific! Michelle wins with a six-letter word, plus she gets an additional ten points for a total of sixteen points. How are you enjoying the game so far?
lines.chinese: 好极了！ 赢了，她的单字有六个字母， 加上另外的十分 她一共得十六分。 到现在为止你们喜欢这个游戏吗？

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: I think it's too easy.
lines.chinese: 我觉得它太简单了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Too easy?
lines.chinese: 太简单？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Michelle!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's OK, Harry Michelle is quite right. She just said it's too easy.
lines.chinese: 没关系，Harry。 说得对。 她仅仅说它太容易了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, I like the game.
lines.chinese: 哦 ，我喜欢这个游戏。

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.english: I enjoyed lunch, Susan. Thank you so much, but we have fo get going to meet the rest of the troop at two 0'clock at the museum.
lines.chinese: 这顿午餐真不错，Susan。 非常感谢你。 但我们必须走了 我们得在两点钟到博物馆与其他童子军会合。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, it was nice meeting all of you. I hope you have a wonderful time at the Museum of Natural History.
lines.chinese: 哦  ，很高兴见到各位。 希望你们玩得开心 在自然历史博物馆。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Good-bye, everybody. Have a good time.
lines.chinese: 各位再见。 玩得痛快。

lines.speaker: Girls
lines.english: Bye.
lines.chinese: 再见。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: See you in front of the museum at five 0'clock.
lines.chinese: 五点钟博物馆前再见。

lines.speaker: Mother
lines.english: Oh, OK. Thanks, Mr. Bennett. That'll be fine. And thanks again for the lunch and for the game.
lines.chinese: 噢 ，好的。 谢谢 ，Bennett 先生。 就这样说定了。 再次谢谢你们提供的午餐和游戏。

lines.speaker: Michelle
lines.english: Good-bye, Susan It's not a bad game. It's just... slow.
lines.chinese: 再见， Susan。 那游戏不错。 只是……太慢了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Just slow. You helped save our company a lot of money. Bye-bye.
lines.chinese: 只是太慢了。 你帮我们公司节约了许多钱。 再见。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: You are wonderful with kids.
lines.chinese: 你对小孩子有一套。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I am.
lines.chinese: 的确这样。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Isn't there a conversation that we have to finish?
lines.chinese: 我们不是有话还没说完？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: There's a conversation we have to finish.
lines.chinese: 我们有一段话还没有谈完呢？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: What was it about?
lines.chinese: 刚才谈什么？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: It was about caring.
lines.chinese: 谈到关心。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh?

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Yes, caring. I said that it makes me feel good that you care about Michelle.
lines.chinese: 噢？ 是的  ，关心。 我是说我感到很高兴 你能关心 Michelle。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Well, I do care about Michelle.
lines.chinese: 是的，我真的关心 Michelle  。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: And it makes me feel good that you care about me.
lines.chinese: 而且你关心我也让我感到很高兴。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: You know I do. You're a...wonderful friend.
lines.chinese: 你知道我是关心的。 你是个……极好的朋友。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: And it makes me feel good that you care about us.
lines.chinese: 这使我太高兴了 你能关心我们大家。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Well...well, I do, Harry. It's only natural that if I care about Michelle and I care about you, then I care about us.
lines.chinese: 哦……哦， 我是关心，Harry。 假如我关心 Michelle 也关心你， 那么我便关心我们大家。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: It's OK, Susan. We had this part of the conversation before Michelle and her friends arrived. Now comes the good part.
lines.chinese: 太好了  ，Susan。 我们正谈到这儿 在 Michelle和她的朋友们到来之前。 现在最精彩的的部分来了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Like what?
lines.chinese: 什么？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, assume that if you care about Michelle and you care about me and you care abut us, that we can talk about us. I mean-you and me. Isn't that right?
lines.chinese: 噢，假如你关心 Michelle 和我 且关心我们大家， 我们就可以谈谈我们。 我的意思是说---你和我。 对不对？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: What are you trying to say, Harry?
lines.chinese: 你到底想说什么，Harry  ？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: What I'm trying to say...since this is so easy ...Here are ten letters that express my feelings for you.
lines.chinese: 我想说…… 既然那么简单…… 这儿有十个字母可以表达我对你的感情。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Don't be silly, Harry.
lines.chinese: 别闹了，Harry。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: You want a hint? It's two words.
lines.chinese: 需要提示吗？ 两个字。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: You're making the game harder.
lines.chinese: 你使游戏变难了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Can't you figure it out? Seven letters... two words...that express the feeling that I feel for you in my heart.
lines.chinese: 你找不到答案？ 七个字母……两个字…… 可以表达我内心对你的感情。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Harry, I get it!
lines.chinese: 我想到了！

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well? Does that mean you'll marry me?
lines.chinese: 噢，你的意思是你答应嫁给我？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Is that a proposal, or are we just playing a game?
lines.chinese: 这是求婚呢  ，还是仅仅在玩游戏？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Sit down, Susan. There's something I have to ask you. Susan, will you marry me?
lines.chinese: 坐下，Susan。 我有一件事要问你。 愿意嫁给我吗？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: It's such an important decision .There's so many things to talk about. There are three things that have to be done first, Harry.
lines.chinese: 这是一个如此重要的决定。 有许多事情需要讨论。 首先我们必须做三件事 ，Harry。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Three things?
lines.chinese: 三件事？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: First, we have to decide where to live.
lines.chinese: 首先  ，我们得决定住在哪儿？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, that's easy. We talked about that earlier. We'll live in New York.
lines.chinese: 哦  ，那很简单。 我们先前谈过这个问题。 我们会住在 New York。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: All right. Second, we have to talk to Michelle together about being married.
lines.chinese: 好。 第二个， 我们必须一起跟 Michelle 谈谈我们结婚的事。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: We'll do that immediately. What' the third thing?
lines.chinese: 我们可以立即跟她谈。 第三件事情是什么？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: You have to ask for my father's permission.
lines.chinese: 你必须征得我父亲的同意。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Absolutely! But first things first. We have to go pick up Michelle.
lines.chinese: 那当然！ 但事有先后。 我们得去接 Michelle 了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, I'm so happy, Harry!
lines.chinese: 噢  ，我太高兴了  ，Harry !

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Not as happy as I am.
lines.chinese: 我比你更高兴。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What's this for?
lines.chinese: 为什么要送我花？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Just because. How's the baby?
lines.chinese: 没有原因。孩子好吗？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Fast asleep.
lines.chinese: 正熟睡着呢。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'm sorry I missed him. I had to work late.
lines.chinese: 很抱歉我错过了他醒着的时候。我必须工作到很晚。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What are the flowers for, Richard?
lines.chinese: 究竟为什么送花 ，Richard？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Five years of happiness. Happy anniversary.
lines.chinese: 为了五年的幸福。祝结婚周年快乐。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: But our anniversary isn't until Saturday.
lines.chinese: 但是要到星期六才是我们结婚周年呀。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I couldn't wait. Besides, we are not going to be here Saturday.
lines.chinese: 我迫不及待啦。 另外，我们星期六将不在这儿了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We're not?
lines.chinese: 不在这儿？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Uh-uh.

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Where are we going to be?
lines.chinese: 我们会在哪儿？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: If you had your choice of all the places in the world, where would you choose to spend our anniversary?
lines.chinese: 假如你可以选择世界上的任何地方。 会选哪儿去度我们的结婚周年纪念日？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: The Watermill Inn. I loved that place when we went on our honeymoon.
lines.chinese: 当初我们度蜜月时我便很喜欢那个地方。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Perfect! You picked the right place.
lines.chinese: 棒极了，你选对了地方。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I don't understand.
lines.chinese: 我不懂你在讲什么。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You and I, Mrs. Stewart, are going to spend a second honeymoon at the Watermill Inn.
lines.chinese: 太太  ，你和我， 将到 Watermill Inn欢度第二次蜜月。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, Richard! That's wonderful, bu-
lines.chinese: 噢,Richard!那真是太好了，可是……

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: No buts.
lines.chinese: 不要说可是。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Absolutely not. The world's greatest grandmother, Mrs. Ellen Stewart, has agreed to take care of him for the weekend.
lines.chinese: 绝对不必。 世界上最伟大的祖母,Ellen Stewart 太太， 同意在周末照顾他。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Richard, isn't that too much to ask of your mother?
lines.chinese: 那是不是太麻烦你母亲啦？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Too much? She loves taking care of Max.
lines.chinese: 太麻烦？她喜欢照顾 Max。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: But... I'll miss him.
lines.chinese: 但是……我会想他的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, we'll phone every hour, and you can listen o him over the phone. Come on. Mar. It's time you and I had a romantic weekend alone together. We've earned it. What do you say?
lines.chinese: 好了  ，我们每个小时都可以打电话， 而且,你可以在电话中听到他的声音。 得了，Mar。 是我们俩单独在一起过一个浪漫周末的时候了。 我们有这样做的权利。  你觉得怎样？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It does sound tempting. You're right. We're earned it.
lines.chinese: 听起来很诱人。 你说得对，我们有这样做的权利。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Great! I'll make a reservation right now. Remember that wonderful little balcony where we had our meals...with a view of the Hudson River?
lines.chinese: 太好了，我现在就订房间 。 还记得那个美妙的小阳台 我们用餐的地方…… 可以看到 Hudson 河？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: How could I forget?
lines.chinese: 我怎么会忘呢？

lines.speaker: Richard
