lines.english: Hello. Is Mrs. Montefiore there? Ah. She's out. Yes. This is Richard Stewart. My wife and I spent our honeymoon at the inn. No, we didn't leave anything in the room. It was five years ago. We'd like to make a reservation for this weekend. Yes, a double room, please. What? Are you sure? But...OK. Nothing available. Wait! Don't hang up! Can you recommend someplace nice-someplace nearby? Uh-hold it. Old country Inn? Right. And the phone number? 555-2420. Thank you. He says there's another inn just half a mile down the road from the Watermill. It won't be the same, But what do you say?
lines.chinese: 是的。我是Richard Stewart。 我太太和我在你们旅馆度的蜜月。 不是，我们没有弄丢任何东西在房间  。 那是五年前了。 我们想预订一个这个周末的房间。 对，双人房间，麻烦你了。什么？ 你确定吗  ？但……算了。 没有空房间了。 等等！别挂断电话！ 你能给我们介绍一个好地方吗---一个附近的地方？ 嗯---等一等。Old Country Inn？ 好的。电话是多少？ 谢谢你。 他说还有另一家旅馆。 从Watermill 往下走要半英里。 那就不一样了，  但你说呢？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well...

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: My mom is available to baby-sit this weekend.
lines.chinese: 我妈妈在这个周末可以照顾小宝宝。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, OK. See if they have a room.
lines.chinese: 那，好吧  。看看他们是否还有房间。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Right!
lines.chinese: 好的！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Hello. Is this the Old Country Inn? Yes. This is Richard Stewart. The desk clerk at the Watermill suggested your inn. Would you happen to have a room for two available this weekend? Something really nice. My wife and I are celebrating our fifth anniversary. Yes. I'll hold. He's checking. You do? Great! What is the daily rate? That's fine. Thank you. Yes, we'll be arriving by car about ten 0'clock Friday night. Stewart. S-t-e-w-a-r-t. Thank you. Done! You and I, Mrs. Stewart, are going to have a wonderful, romantic weekend!
lines.chinese: 喂  ，是 Old Country Inn 吗？ 是的。我是Richard Stewart。 的职员推荐你们的旅馆。 你们可有一个双人房间 这个周末？ 要很好的。 我太太和我要庆祝一下我们的结婚五周年纪念日。 好，我会等。他正在查。真的有？ 太棒了！  房间一天租金多少？ 很好。谢谢你。 是的，我们将于星期五晚上十点钟左右开车抵达。谢谢你。 办到了！ Stewart 太太，你和我 将度过一个美妙浪漫的周末！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Richard, that's the baby.
lines.chinese: 是小宝宝醒了。

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.english: Right this way, Mr. and Mrs. Stewart. Well, it's small, but clean.
lines.chinese: 走这边  ，Stewart 先生和太太。 噢  ，它是小了点  ，但是干净。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Clean? You call this clean?
lines.chinese: 干净？这样算干净？

lines.speaker: Clerk
lines.english: Have a nice stay.
lines.chinese: 希望你们住得愉快。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you. I don't believe this. Well, it isn't the Watermill Inn, but let's get a look at the view.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。我简直不敢相信。 算了  ，这不是 Watermill Inn  ， 但让我们来瞧一瞧景色。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: How is the view?
lines.chinese: 景色怎么样？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Great, if you enjoy looking at a parking lot.
lines.chinese: 好得很，假如你喜欢看一个停车场的话。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, maybe we'll see the view in the morning. Right now, we should clean up this room.
lines.chinese: 好了，  也许我们可以在早晨看风景。 现在，我们应当清理一下房间。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Sorry, Marilyn.
lines.chinese: 对不起，Marilyn  。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Why should you be sorry?
lines.chinese: 你何必说对不起呢？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, the rain, the room, the view of the parking lot. It isn't the way I hoped it would be.
lines.chinese: 唉，这雨，这房间，这停车场的景色。 这不是我所盼望的。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Stop blaming yourself. After all, we're here, we're alone, we're together. And I l love you. Isn't that enough?
lines.chinese: 别责备你自己了。 毕竟，我们来到这儿了，我们又单独在一起了。 我爱你。难道这还不够？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Is is for me, but I wanted this weekend to be special for you.
lines.chinese: 对我来说当然足够， 但我希望这个周末对你来说很特别。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It is special. Happy anniversary.
lines.chinese: 确实很特别。结婚周年快乐。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It's still raining. I want to play tennis.
lines.chinese: 仍然在下雨。我想打网球。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I want some breakfast. Let's call room service and order a mice breakfast, and then we'll figure out what to do today.
lines.chinese: 我想吃早餐。 让我们打电话给房间服务部订一份可口的早餐， 然后我们再决定今天做些什么。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Right. Would you give me room service, please? I beg your pardon! Oh, I see. They don't have room service at Old Country Inn.
lines.chinese: 好。请接房间服务部，可以吗？ 你说什么？  噢，我明白了。 没有早餐服务部。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, let's go down to the coffee shop.
lines.chinese: 哦，那我们下去咖啡厅吧。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: They don't have a coffee shop .We can get our meals at Mrs. Montefiore's down the road.
lines.chinese: 他们也没有咖啡厅。 我们可以沿大路走到 Montefiore 太太的旅馆去吃。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: That's OK, honey. I love walking in the rain.
lines.chinese: 没有关系的，亲爱的。 我喜欢在雨中散步。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, you're being a really good sport about this, Marilyn, but I think we should face the truth.
lines.chinese: 噢，在这件事情上，你倒变成一个看得很开的人，Marilyn， 但我认为我们应面对现实。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What's that?
lines.chinese: 什么意思？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: This is not the way to spend our fifth anniversary.
lines.chinese: 这不是我们过五周年纪念日的方式。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, what do you want to do?
lines.chinese: 那你想干什么？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Why don't we get in the car and drive home?
lines.chinese: 我们何不开车回家？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, Richard, it really isn't that bad.
lines.chinese: 喔，Richard，  事情没有那么糟。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Do you want to stick it out for the whole weekend?
lines.chinese: 难道你想在此耗掉整个周末？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, I'll admit the room is uncomfortable.
lines.chinese: 嗯，我承认这个房间很不舒服。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Uh-hum.
lines.chinese: 嗯哼。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And I do feel bad about your mother having to take care of the baby all weekend.
lines.chinese: 而且我觉得很过意不去让你母亲 整个周末照顾孩子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Why don't we just check out?
lines.chinese: 那何不立即结帐离开？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: OK.

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Hello? Yes. Yes, this is she. Oh, hello! How nice of you to remember us! Yes, My husband did call. You do? Really? It won't be any trouble? Oh, yes, I think we'd like that very much. Fifteen minutes! Thank you. Good-bye. You will never guess.
lines.chinese: 好吧。 喂？是的，是的，是我。 噢，你好！你真好还记得我们！ 是的，我丈夫打过电话。有？  真的？  不会有任何麻烦？ 噢，  好的，我想我们非常乐意。 十五分钟， 谢谢。再见。 你绝对猜不到。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Uh... I give up.
lines.chinese: 嗯……我放弃猜。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Mrs. Montefiore from the Watermill Inn.
lines.chinese: 是 Watermill Inn的 Montefiore 太太打来的电话。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: What is she calling about?
lines.chinese: 她打来说什么？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: They have an opening. Someone just checked out, and Mrs. Montefiore has reserved the honeymoon suite for us.
lines.chinese: 他们有一个空房间。 有一个人刚刚结帐走了。 太太为我们保留了蜜月套房。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You're kidding?
lines.chinese: 你在开玩笑！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: No. Isn't it wonderful?
lines.chinese: 不，这不是很美妙吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It's fantastic! Oh, Now all it has to do is stop raining. Let's go.
lines.chinese: 太棒了！ 噢。现在唯一希望的就是停止下雨。 我们走吧。

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: Welcome to the Watermill Inn.
lines.chinese: 欢迎你们来 Watermill Inn。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, Richard, it's exactly as it was when we were married!
lines.chinese: 噢，Richard。 它跟我们结婚的时候一模一样！

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: And when I got married.
lines.chinese: 也跟我结婚时一样。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Even the old patchwork quilt is the same!
lines.chinese: 甚至这条旧的百衲被罩也是一样！

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: My great-grandmother made that quilt when Teddy Roosevelt was President. In those day, when they made quilts, they cut patches from the old clothing of every member in the family os that each one would be a part of it.
lines.chinese: 我曾祖母缝了这个被罩 在Teddy Roosevelt 总统的时代。 那个时代，人们做被罩， 剪下布块 从每位家庭成员的旧衣服 好让每个人都成为它的一部分。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What a lovely tradition!
lines.chinese: 多么可爱的传统！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you for calling us.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你打电话给我们。

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: I remember you and your wife from the first time you stayed with us.
lines.chinese: 我便一直记得你和你太太 自从你们第一次在我们这儿住。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We were very young.
lines.chinese: 我们那时很年轻。

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: And very much in love.
lines.chinese: 而且相爱至深。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We have a baby now.
lines.chinese: 我们现在有一个小宝宝了。

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: How wonderful! A boy or girl?
lines.chinese: 太好了！男孩还是女孩？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: A boy-Max.
lines.chinese: 男孩。叫 Max。

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: Do you have a picture?
lines.chinese: 你们有他的照片吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Would a professional photographer be without a picture?
lines.chinese: 一位专业摄影师会没有照片？

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: Oh, he's adorable!
lines.chinese: 哦，他可爱极了！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And very bright.
lines.chinese: 而且非常聪明。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Like his father. A real Stewart.
lines.chinese: 像他爸爸一样。一个真正的 Stewart 家庭成员。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And very good-looking, like his mother.
lines.chinese: 也很漂亮，像他妈妈。

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: Well, it's nice to have you with us again. next time, bring the baby.
lines.chinese: 噢  ，非常高兴你们又来了。 下次把小宝宝一起带来。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: You see, Richard? Max is welcome here.
lines.chinese: 你看到了吧，Richard？ 他们这儿欢迎 Max 呢。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Not on our anniversary. This vacation is for you and me.
lines.chinese: 可不能在我们的结婚纪念日。 这是我和你的假期。

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: If there's anything you need, please call me. I'll be in the front office all day.
lines.chinese: 假如你们需要什么，请打电话给我。 我整天都在总办公室。

lines.speaker: Innkeeper
lines.english: Oh, I took the liberty of ordering some breakfast for you. Just put it over there, Charles. Thank you, Charles. Compliments of the Watermill Inn.
lines.chinese: 噢，我冒昧给你们订了早餐。 就放在这儿吧，Charles。谢谢你，Charles。 请客。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thank you so much.
lines.chinese: 太感谢了。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Now, this is my idea of a good time. Let's see...Hotcakes and maple syrup, with scrambled eggs.
lines.chinese: 这才是我所谓的享受时光。 让我瞧瞧……烤饼和枫蜜，还有炒蛋。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Umm...smell that coffee... cinnamon and cloves.
lines.chinese: 呀……你闻一闻咖啡……一股肉桂和丁香的香味。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Homemade buttermilk biscuits.
lines.chinese: 自治的酸奶脆饼干。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Slices of orange with burnt honey.
lines.chinese: 上了焦蜜的桔子片。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Let's eat!
lines.chinese: 让我们享用吧！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: First I want to call home and check on your mother and the baby.
lines.chinese: 我得先打个电话回家 问问你母亲和孩子的情况。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Honey, if there were any problems, she would call us.
lines.chinese: 亲爱的，假如有事的话， 她会打电话过来的。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: She doesn't know we've changed hotels.
lines.chinese: 她不知道我们换了旅馆呀。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You're right. Of course.
lines.chinese: 说得也是。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Hello, operator. I'd like to call Riverdale, New York.
lines.chinese: 喂  ，接线生。 请接 New York 的 Riverdale。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: This food is heavenly, isn't it?
lines.chinese: 这一顿太美好了，对吗？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: This whole place is heavenly. Do you remember that old desk? Mrs. Montefiore told me that George Washington sat at that desk and wrote to his wife Martha.
lines.chinese: 这整个地方都太美好了。 还记得这张旧桌子吗？ 太太告诉我曾坐在那儿 给他妻子Martha 写信。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: What do you want to do after breakfast?
lines.chinese: 早餐后想做什么？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Why don't we take a walk down to the river?
lines.chinese: 我们何不一路散步到河边去？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: In the rain?
lines.chinese: 在雨中？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: No. Look out the window. The sun is shining!
lines.chinese: 不。你看看窗外。太阳出来了！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Now this is my idea fo a good time!
lines.chinese: 这才是我所谓的享受！

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: The mailman just dropped some mail in our box Grandpa.
lines.chinese: 邮差刚刚塞进一些信件在我们的信箱,爷爷.

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Probably a lot of advertising and bills. Why don't you write to me, Robbie, so I can get some interesting mail?
lines.chinese: 大概是一大堆广告和帐单. 你为什么不给我写信呢，Robbie. 那样我就可以收到一些有趣的信件了。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: You were right, Grandpa. Advertising, bills, bills, advertising.
lines.chinese: 给你说对了，爷爷。 广告，帐单，帐单，广告。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yes. It's just like I said, Robbie. Nothing interesting.
lines.chinese: 正像我说的那样吧，Robbie。 没有什么有趣的。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: You won't believe it, Grandpa, but there's a letter here addressed to you, Mr.Malcolm Stewart, and it looks like a personal letter.
lines.chinese: 你绝不会相信，爷爷。 这儿确实有一封信是寄给你的， 先生收，而且看来是一封私人信件。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Oh, it must be a bill.
lines.chinese: 噢，还不是帐单。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I don't think so. The return name and address is Pete Waers, RFD Number 1, Chesterton.
lines.chinese: 我想不是吧。 发信人姓名和地址是 乡村免费邮递路一号。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: You're joking. Pete Waters?
lines.chinese: 你在开玩笑吧。Pete Waters？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Pete Waters, RFD Number 1, Chesterton. You know him?
lines.chinese: 乡村免费邮递路一号,Chesterton。 你认识他吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Do I know Pete Waters? You bet I do! He was my roommate in college. He visited with Grandma and me in Florida about five years ago.
lines.chinese: 我认识 Pete Waters？ 你可以打赌我当然认识！ 他是我大学时的的室友。 他到 Florida 看过祖母和我 大约五年前。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: What does he say? Is he OK?
lines.chinese: 他说了些什么？ 他还好吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yeah, he's fine. Just fine. He's writing to invite me to spend a weekend with him at his farm. He's planning a get-together with two or three other college friends. The dindof a fifty-year anniversary reunion.
lines.chinese: 是的  ，他不错。 相当不错。 他写信邀请我 去他的农场跟他一起渡个周末。 他计划办一个聚会。 邀两三个其他的大学朋友一起。 等于是五十年重逢聚会。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Sounds like fun. Fifty Years? Wow!
lines.chinese: 听起来好像蛮好玩的。 五十年？喔！

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: It sounds like fun to me, too, Robbie.
lines.chinese: 对我来说也像是蛮好玩的，Robbie.

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: What kind of farm does he have?
lines.chinese: 他的农场是什么样?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I've never been three, Robbie, but he has chickens and cows and all. That means fresh eggs and fresh milk.
lines.chinese: 我从来没有去过那儿 ,Robbie。 但他有鸡牛等等。 也就是说会有新鲜的鸡蛋和牛奶。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Does he have a family?
lines.chinese: 他有亲人吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: No, he doesn't fobbie. He never married. He's not as lucky as I am to have a family and grandchildren . I'm a lucky man.
lines.chinese: 没有，他没有，Robbie。 他没有结过婚。 他不像我这样幸运 拥有一个家庭和孙子们。我是个幸运的人。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: How come he never got married?
lines.chinese: 为什么他不结婚？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: That's a good question, Robbie. A very good question. He never married because the girl he was in love with in college married someone else. As simple as that. He never got over it.
lines.chinese: 这是个好问题，Robbie。 一个非常好的问题。 他不结婚是因为 他大学时代所爱的女孩跟别人结婚了。 就这么简单。 他一直念念不忘。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: He must have loved her very much.
lines.chinese: 他一定非常地爱她。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yes. Very much. Lillian Winters. She was in our class.
lines.chinese: 是的，非常爱。 她是 Lillian Winters。 她和我们同班。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: And what happened?
lines.chinese: 发生什么事呢？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: She was in love with Donald McGrath, the quarterback on our football team.
lines.chinese: 她爱上了 Donald McGrath， 我们足球队的四分卫。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Football players are always popular with the ladies.
lines.chinese: 足球队员往往很讨女生的喜爱。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: She liked Pete, and they went to dances together. But her heart was with Donald.
lines.chinese: 她喜欢 Pete，他们也一起去跳舞。 但她的心里想的是 Donald。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Did he ever get over it?
lines.chinese: 他有没有忘掉这件事？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: No, he never did.
lines.chinese: 没有，从来没有。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Where is she today?
lines.chinese: 她如今在哪？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I don't know. Maybe Lillian will be at the reunion.
lines.chinese: 我不知道。 或许 Lillian 也会参加聚会。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: You think so?
lines.chinese: 你想会吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Pete's full of surprises.
lines.chinese: 总是出人意料。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I wish I could go there with you, Grandpa. What do you think the surprise will be?
lines.chinese: 我真希望我能够跟你一起去，爷爷。 你想会是什么样的意外惊喜呢？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: With Pete, you never know, Robbie.
lines.chinese: 你不可能猜到 Pete 会耍什么花样的， Robbie。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Won't it be exciting to see all your college friends there again?
lines.chinese: 在那儿又要见到你的大学朋友，难道不兴奋吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: It is already. I'm kind of excited about going now. Next weekend...sleep over Friday and Saturday night and come back Sunday. I can't wait! Ribbie： Don't you think you ought to call Pete and tell him you're coming?
lines.chinese: 现在已经很兴奋了。 我现在就想去了。 下周末…… 星期五和星期六晚上在那儿过 然后在星期天回来。 我迫不及待!你不觉得应该打个电话给 Pete 告诉他你要去吗?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: You're reading my mind, Robbie.
lines.chinese: 你说的正是我想做的，Robbie。

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: Hello. What can I do for you?
lines.chinese: 你好。我能为你做点什么?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Hi. Fill'er up. I need a full tank.
lines.chinese: 嗨,把油箱加满。 我要满满一箱。

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: Check the hood?
lines.chinese: 要不要检查一下机子？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: No, thanks.
lines.chinese: 不用了，谢谢。

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: Whereabouts are you headed?
lines.chinese: 你要去哪儿？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Pete Waters's farm, near Chesterton.
lines.chinese: 的农场，在 Chesterton 附近。

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: I know it well. Pete Waters' lived around here almost as long as I have.
lines.chinese: 我对它熟得很。 住在这儿差不多跟我一样久了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: How long is it going to take for me to get there?
lines.chinese: 到那儿还需要多久时间？

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: About fifteen minutes. There's a shortcut-is you know it.
lines.chinese: 大约十五分钟吧。 假如你识路的话---有一条近路可走。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: No, I don't. Could you tell me how to use the shortcut?
lines.chinese: 不，我不识路。 你能告诉我怎么走这条近路？

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: Sure. You take the next left turn. You'll see a stop sign. Make a right at the stop sign. Stay on that road, and you'll cross a blue bridge. Then you'll see a big old red bam. That's the back of Pete Waters's place.
lines.chinese: 当然。你在下一个路口左拐。 会看到有个停车标志。 就在停车标志那儿向右拐。 沿着路开下去，你会经过一座蓝色的桥。 然后你就会看到一座旧的红色大仓库。 那就是 Pete Watters住处的后面。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: That's quite a difference from the directions that Pete sent me.
lines.chinese: 这和 Pete 寄给我的路的指示完全不同。

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: Now, if you take that route, it's probably a lot simpler, but it'll take you ten minutes longer.
lines.chinese: 哦，假如你走这条路的话，可能会简单得多。 但会多花你十分钟的时间。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: OK. Let me repeat it. I take the next left turn to the stop sign. Then a right across a blue bridge, and then a big red barn.
lines.chinese: 好吧。让我重复一遍。 我在下个路口左转弯到一个停车标志。 然后右转经过一座蓝色的桥，然后就是一座大大的红色仓库。

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: Can't miss it.
lines.chinese: 错不了的。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: How much do I owe you?
lines.chinese: 我应付你多少钱？

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: Well, that'll be eighteen dollars and seventy cent. No charge for the cleanup. It's on the house.
lines.chinese: 嗯，十八美元七十美分。 清洗不用付费。 这是免费的。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Well, here's a twenty.
lines.chinese: 嗯，这是二十美美元

lines.speaker: Attendant
lines.english: Ah. OK. That's a dollar and thirty cents change. Thee we go... will mae twenty. Thanks.
lines.chinese: 啊，好啦。这是一美元三十美分的零钱。 你看……加起来来正好二十美元。谢谢了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Thank you. And thanks for the directions.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。 谢谢你给我指路。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: This must be Peteps barn.Turn right to the house. Hi.
lines.chinese: 这一定是 Pete 的的仓库了。 向右拐就到他的的房子了。 嗨。

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.english: Hi. What can I do for you?
lines.chinese: 嗨，我能帮你吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Is this the Pete Waters farm?
lines.chinese: 这是 Pete Waters 的农场吗？

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.english: It is.
lines.chinese: 正是。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I'm a friend of Pete's . I'm looking for the house.
lines.chinese: 我是 Pete 的朋友。 我正在找他的房子。

lines.speaker: Worker
lines.english: It's just over yonder. Keep along this road till you get to the end of the fence. You'll see the chicken. His house is on the left.
lines.chinese: 他的房子就在那边。 沿着这条路 到栅栏的尽头。 你会看到鸡舍。 他的房子就在左边。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Well, thanks.
lines.chinese: 好的，谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: "have gone to the railroad station. Back soon with a surprise. Make yourself at home. Have a look around Pete." Same old Pete Waters. Always full of surprises.
lines.chinese: 去火车站。 接一个惊喜即回来。 勿客气。 到处看看。Pete。” 还是老样子。 总是出人意料。。

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: Don't tell me. Please don't tell me. I recognize you...
lines.chinese: 别告诉我。 请先别告诉我。 我认得你……

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: Oh, don't be silly, Arnie. Of course you recognize him. Except for the beard, he hasn't changed in fifty years. It's Malcolm Stewart!
lines.chinese: 噢，别傻了，Arnie。 你当然认得他。 除了胡须他五十年来没有变。 他是Malcolm Stewart！

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: I know it's Malcolm Stewart. You haven't changed much in fifty years.
lines.chinese: 我就知道是Malcolm Stewart。 你五十年来没有什么变化。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Peggy-Peggy Pendleton! You're Peggy Pendleton!
lines.chinese: 是 Peggy Pendleton！

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: Who am I, you old rascal? You don't recognize me, do you?
lines.chinese: 我是谁，你这个老玩童？ 你不认识我了，是吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I know who you are. You're Amold Franklin! I know who you are!
lines.chinese: 我知道你是谁。 你是 Arnold Franklin！ 我知道你是谁！

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: You look wonderful, Malcolm!
lines.chinese: 你看起来好得很 Malcolm！

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Sit down. Pete isn't home.
lines.chinese: 请坐。请坐吧。 不在家。

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: Really?
lines.chinese: 真的吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: No. He left a note on the door saying he was going to the railroad station to pick up a surprise.
lines.chinese: 是的。他留了一张条子在门外。 说他要去火车站 接一个意外惊喜回来。 他喜欢开玩笑。 总是出人意料。 五十年之后还是这样。

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: He's so funny. Always full of surprises, even fifty years later.

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: The two of you look unbelievable! Amnold： How's your family? Oh, I was sorry to hear about your wife having passed away.
lines.chinese: 你们俩看起来简直让人难以置信。你的家人好吗？ 噢，我很难过 听到你太太去世的消息。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yes. About four years ago.
lines.chinese: 是的，大约四年前走的。

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: And you're living with your children now? In New York? Pete wrote us and told us.
lines.chinese: 你现在跟你的孩子们一起住在 New York，对吗？ 写信告诉我们的。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yup. Retired and moved to New York to live with my son and his family.
lines.chinese: 是的。 我退休了，搬到 New York。 和我儿子和他的家人住在一起。

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: By the way, what do you think this big surprise is?
lines.chinese: 顺便问一声， 你认为这一件大惊喜会是什么？

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: It could be most anything, knowing Pete.
lines.chinese: 任何事情都可能，你是知道 Pete 的。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Hey, that must be Pete! Now we'll find out about the surprise.
lines.chinese: 嗨，那一定是 Pete！ 现在我们可以知道是什么样的惊喜了.

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: So good to see you all!
lines.chinese: 看到你们大家真高兴。

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: Oh, pete, Pete! Oh, It's so good to see you!
lines.chinese: 噢，Pete,Pete  ！ 噢，看到你真是太好了！

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: Remember Lillian?
lines.chinese: 还记得Lillian？

lines.speaker: Lillian
lines.english: I remember all of you. You haven't changed a bit.
lines.chinese: 我记得你们各位。 你们一点都没有变。

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: Lillian! Oh, my gosh!
lines.chinese: 噢，我的天啦！

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: Lillian Winters. We were together in the Thursday night drama society.
lines.chinese: 我们过去总在星期四晚上的话剧社聚会。

lines.speaker: Lillian
lines.english: Remember me, Malcolm?
lines.chinese: 还记得我吗，Malcolm?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Oh, beautiful as ever, Lillian. How's Donald?
lines.chinese: 噢，还像以前一样漂亮，Lillian。 怎么样？

lines.speaker: Lillian
lines.english: That's OK, Malcolm. Donald Passed away a couple of years ago.
lines.chinese: 没有关系,Malcolm。 几年前去世了。

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: Sorry to hear that, Lillian.
lines.chinese: 很遗憾听到这个消息，Lillian。

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: Oh, I'm so sorry.
lines.chinese: 噢，我真难过。

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: I would not have missed this get-together for the world!
lines.chinese: 世界上任何事情都阻止不了我来参加这次聚会。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: And your little surprise, Pete? You really surprised me by having us all come together.
lines.chinese: 你的小惊喜呢，Pete？ 你已经够让人惊喜了 使我们聚在一起。

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: You don't know what the surprise is yet? Come on! We'll tell you the big surprise.
lines.chinese: 你们还不知道这个惊喜是什么？ 过来！ 我来告诉你们这个大惊喜！

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: I've invited you here for the weekend to help celebrate.
lines.chinese: 我邀请你们到这儿来一起渡过周末帮我庆祝。

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: Celebrate?
lines.chinese: 庆祝？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Our fiftieth reunion?
lines.chinese: 是我们的五十年聚会？

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: No.

lines.speaker: Lillian
lines.english: No. Pete wants to tell you...
lines.chinese: 不是。Pete要告诉你们……

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: Now, let me have the honor, Lillian.
lines.chinese: 现在，容我享有这个荣幸，Lillian。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: For goodness sake, Pete, tell us! I can't wait much longer.
lines.chinese: 天啊，Pete  快告诉我！ 我再也不能等了。

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: Well, I am pouring this iced tea so that we can toast Lillian-and me.
lines.chinese: 嗯，我再给你们倒冰茶 以便我们能敬 Lillian 一杯，也敬我。

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: You don't mean to tell me that you and...
lines.chinese: 你不会是告诉我们你和…

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: Yes, I do. I have loved Lillian all these years, so I asked her to be Mrs.Pete.Waters.
lines.chinese: 是的，我是这个意思。 这些年来我一直爱着 Lillia， 所以我请求她成为 Pete Waters 夫人。

lines.speaker: Lillian
lines.english: And I said yes.
lines.chinese: 而我答应了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I knew it! Congratulations!
lines.chinese: 我就知道是这样，我就知道是这样！ 恭喜你们！

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: Oh, Lillian, I am so happy for you both.
lines.chinese: 噢， Lillian， 我真为你们俩感到高兴。

lines.speaker: Amold
lines.english: It's wonderful!
lines.chinese: 太好了！

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: It's wonderful for me. Lillian will make me a happy man finally.
lines.chinese: 这对我来说太好了。 会使我成为一个快乐的人。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: You are full of surprises, Pete.
lines.chinese: 你总是出人意料，Pete。

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: We are going to spend the entire weekend having a good time together here on the farm. We are going to celebrate all weekend.
lines.chinese: 我们要 一起渡过一个快乐的周末 在农场。 我们整个周末都要进行庆祝  ！

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: When is the wedding?
lines.chinese: 什么时候举行婚礼？

lines.speaker: Pete
lines.english: That's another surprise. Lillian and I were married two weeks ago in Detroit. She's come here to stay.
lines.chinese: 这是另一个惊喜。 和我已经在两周前在 Detroit 结婚了。 她这次来是要住下来。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Wait till I tell my family about this!
lines.chinese: 等我告诉我家人这件事的时候！

lines.speaker: Peggy
lines.english: Why, that's wonderful!
lines.chinese: 哎呀，这太美好！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What are you doing?
lines.chinese: 你在干什么？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I think I've got them.
lines.chinese: 我想我已经拍齐了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: You think you've got what?
lines.chinese: 你想你已经拍齐了什么？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Performing arts-performing arts centers. I think I've got them all, but I'm not sure.
lines.chinese: 表演艺术---表演艺术中心。 我想我都已经拍齐了，但我不能肯定。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I think you have got it, Richard. Lincoln Center, home of the Metropolitan Opera, the New York City Ballet, the New York Philharmonic.
lines.chinese: 我想你已完成了，Richard。 林肯中心，大都会歌剧， 纽约市芭蕾舞团，纽约爱乐大本营。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Shubert Alley, center of the theater on Broadway.
lines.chinese: 百老汇大剧院中心。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And Comegie Hall. And the others. I do have it all.
lines.chinese: 还有其他的。我确实拍齐了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: You have been working on this for some time, Richard. I'm glad you feel you've finally put it all together. What now?
lines.chinese: 你从事这工作已有一段时间了，Richard。 我很高兴你终于就绪了。 下一步呢？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Now for the hard part.
lines.chinese: 现在到了最难的部分。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, you think you're going to have a hard time getting the album published?
lines.chinese: 你认为需要大费周折 才能让摄影集出版？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, it won't be easy.
lines.chinese: 嗯，不很容易。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: So, what do you think you're going to do?
lines.chinese: 那么你打算怎么办？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Does the name Harvey Carlson ring a bell?
lines.chinese: 对 Harvey Carlson 这个名字有印象吗？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh,yes! Harvey Carlson! I remember.
lines.chinese: 噢，是的！Harvey Carlson！我记得。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: He said I should call him. He's the publisher of the Carlson Publishing Company. He said they need a new book of photographs, and he really liked my concept.
lines.chinese: 他说我可以打电话给他。 他是 Carlson出版公司的出版商。 他说他公司需要一本新的摄影集。 而且他真的喜欢我的想法。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: So, why don't you call him in the morning?
lines.chinese: 那么你为何明天早上不打个电话给他？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Do you think I have enough to show him?
lines.chinese: 你觉得我有足够的照片给他看吗？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I've been through every section with you, Richard. It's quite complete. And now that you're satisfied, with the performing arts section, I think you should show it to Mr.Carlson.
lines.chinese: 我和你一起检视过每个部分，Richard。 已经相当齐全了。 而且既然你对表演艺术这部分很满意， 我觉得你应该给 Carlson先生看看。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You're right. My Family Album, U.S.A. feels right. I'll call in the morning and set up an appointment to see him.
lines.chinese: 说的对。我的走遍美国不赖。 我明天早上就打电话给他，跟他约个时间去看他。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: He's going to love your work. Come on. Let's get some sleep.
lines.chinese: 他一定会欣赏你的作品的。 走吧。让我们去睡觉吧。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Is it too early to call Mr.Carlson?
lines.chinese: 现在打电话给 Carlson 先生会不会太早？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Seven after nine? No. I'm sure he's in his office.
lines.chinese: 九点零七分，不会太早的。我肯定他到办公室了。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: His number is five, five, five, seven, five, three, two. Five, five, five, seven, five three, two. Hello. Mr. Carlson, please.
lines.chinese: 他的号码是 5，5，5，7，5，3，2。 哈，请找一下 Carlson 先生。

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Mr.Carlson is busy at the moment. May I help you?
lines.chinese: 先生现在正忙着呢。我可以为你效劳吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'd like to make an appointment with him.
lines.chinese: 我想跟他约个时间谈谈。

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: And your name is...?
lines.chinese: 请问你的名字是……？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: My name is Richard Stewart. He told me to call him about my project, Family Album, U.S.A.
lines.chinese: 我的名字是 Richard Stewart。 他叫我打电话给他跟他谈我的作品， 走遍美国。 请等一下。 怎么样了？ 我猜他们正设法给我安排会谈时间。 先生，我刚才与 Carlson 先生通了话。 他想见你。 但这星期他有空的时间只有 明天早上十点。

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Mr. Stewart, I just spoke to Mr.Carlson. He would like to see you. But the only time he's available this week is tomorrow morning at ten 0'clock.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks. Thanks a lot. Good-bye. Well, it's done. Tomorrow morning at a publisher's office.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。非常感谢。再见。 好了，事情办成了。明天早晨要到出版社的办公室。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, it's so exciting, isn't it, Richard?
lines.chinese: 噢,这太令人兴奋了,是不是,Richard?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, finally a publisher will see my work.
lines.chinese: 好了,终于有一个出版商要看我的作品了。

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Good morning.
lines.chinese: 早安。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Good morning. My name is Richard Stewart. I'm here to see Mr.Carlson.
lines.chinese: 早安。我叫 Richard Stewart。 我来见 Carlson 先生。

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Please sit down, Mr. Stewart. Mr. Carlson will be with you shortly.
lines.chinese: 先生不久就可以见你。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢  。

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Excuse me, Mr. Carlson, but Richard Stewart is here for his ten 0'clock appointment with you. OK. Thank you. Like I said, he'll be with you shortly.
lines.chinese: 对不起, Carlson 先生, 但 Richard Stewart正在这儿 等着十点钟与你的会谈。好。谢谢。 正像我所说,他不久就可以与你会面。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Yes, Mr.Carlson. Yes, sir. He's ready for you, Mr. Stewart.
lines.chinese: 是的,Carlson 先生。是,  先生。 他可以见你啦,Stewart 先生。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you. In there?
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。在那边？

lines.speaker: Receptionist
lines.english: Yes. In there. Good luck.
lines.chinese: 是的,在那边。祝你好运。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Come in, come in. This is a crazy morning. Hello, Richard.
lines.chinese: 进来,进来。 这是一个忙得头昏的早晨。哈,Richard  。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Hello, Mr.Carlson.
lines.chinese: 哈,Carlson 先生。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Sit down, sit down.
lines.chinese: 请坐,请坐。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you for seeing me on such short notice.
lines.chinese: 谢谢您这样快就安排见我。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: I hope you've brought your pictures along. I see that you have. Let's get right to it. We need a new coffee table book, and a book of photos about the United States still feels right. OK, let's take a look. Good. Very good. Family Album, U.S.A. It's an excellent title. If you had to describe the book in one sentence, how would you do it?
lines.chinese: 我希望你带了你的照片一起来。 我看到你带来了。我们就直接谈正事了。 我们需要一本可以摆在客厅的。 而一本关于美国的摄影集仍然很有前景。 好了,让我们来看一看。 不错,很不错。走遍美国。 这是个绝妙的书名。 假如要你用一句话来描述这本, 你怎么说?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, I'd describe it as a book which is a ... a portrait of the United States-the places, the people-mostly the people. The things they do, the ways they live, the placesd they visit, and the landmarks. A photographic journey.
lines.chinese: 嗯,我会把这本书形容为一幅…… 一幅美国的剪影---美国的一些地方,人民---大部分是人。 他们所做的事情,他们的生活方式, 他们所去的地方,以及地标等等。一次透过摄影作品的旅行。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: These are wonderful-these photos in your pen arts section. Carnegie Hall, Lincoln Center.
lines.chinese: 这些好得很--- 你这些表演艺术中心部分的照片。 卡奈基音乐厅,林肯中心。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'm glad you like them.
lines.chinese: 我很高兴你喜欢它们。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: I do, but...
lines.chinese: 我确实喜欢,但是…

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: But?
lines.chinese: 但是?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: There's something missing. You've got a good eye, Richard. You're a terrific photographer. But before I can publish your work. I need to meet with my marketing department, and you've got to do one more thing.
lines.chinese: 少了些东西。你有艺术眼光, Richard。 你是一名出色的摄影师。 但在我出版你的书之前, 我需要跟我的营销部门商量一下, 而且你还要做一件事。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: What's that, Mr. Carlson?
lines.chinese: 什么事,Carlson 先生?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: In the section on culture, you've included performing arts centers, but you've left out street performance.The mimes. The musicians. The dancers-in the parks and on the streets. Richard, if you go out and photograph street performances in the city, you'll have it.
lines.chinese: 在文化这部分, 你拍了表演艺术中心, 但你忽略了街头表演。幽默剧演员。 音乐家啦,在公园或街头跳舞的人啦。 假如你出去 拍摄城市的街头表演, 那你就大功告成了。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: That is a great ieda.The performing arts centers and the street performances. I'll do it.
lines.chinese: 这是个好主意。 表演艺术中心及街头表演。 我会去办。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: If you do it, I'll publish your work.
lines.chinese: 假如你拍好了,我就出版你的作品。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Are you serious?
lines.chinese: 你是说真的吗?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: I've never been more serious. When do you think you can return with street performances?
lines.chinese: 我从来没有比现在更认真了。 你觉得何时可以带来街头表演艺术的摄影?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: A couple of weeks.
lines.chinese: 两三个星期吧。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: If they're as good as the rest of these pictures, it's a deal.
lines.chinese: 假如它们跟这其余的照片一样好的话,  我们的交易就成功。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You won't be disappointed, Mr.Carlson. Thanks.
lines.chinese: 我不会让你失望的, Carlson先生。谢谢。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Good-bye, Richard, and good luck. See you in two weeks.
lines.chinese: 再见,Richard,祝你好运。两三周后见。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Good-bye, Mr. Carlson. Thanks. So if you like the street-performance photos, you'll really publish Family Album, U.S.A.?
lines.chinese: 再见,Carlson 先生。谢谢。 这么说,假如你喜欢街头表演的照片, 你真的会出版走遍美国?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: When I way something, I mean it. Go to work. Goodbye.
lines.chinese: 我说的一定会兑现的。 去工作吧。再见。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Good-bye.
lines.chinese: 再见。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Two weeks. I said I could do it in two weeks, and I did it.
lines.chinese: 两周。我说过我两周就能完成,现在我做到了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: The pictures you've taken are fabulous, Richard. Mr. Carlson will love them.
lines.chinese: 你拍的照片太精采了,Richard  。 先生会喜欢的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Monday morning. I'm going to show him the photos on Monday morning. I can't wait!
lines.chinese: 星期一早晨。 我要在星期一早晨给他看这些照片。 我简直迫不及待!

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I'm very proud of you. You really did a beautiful job. I know he will love the new photographs for your book.
lines.chinese: 我真以你为傲。你真的拍得很漂亮。 我相信他会喜欢你为你的书所拍的这些新照片。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yeah. I really did do a good job, didn't I? Hey, you know? There's still some film left on this roll, and there's one person I haven't photographed in a long time.
lines.chinese: 是的,我确实拍得不错,对不? 嘿,知道吗?这里还有一些底片? 而有一个人我已经很久没为她拍照了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Who?
lines.chinese: 谁呀?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You.
lines.chinese: 你。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Richard! I haven't brushed my hair.
lines.chinese: 我头发没有梳。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You look great!
lines.chinese: 看起来很棒!

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It's not fait, Richard. I'm not even ready.
lines.chinese: 这样不公平, Richard。我甚至一点准备都没有。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, that's wonderful! That's wonderful!
lines.chinese: 噢,真是美极了!  真是美极了!

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Wonderful!
lines.chinese: 好极了!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Fabulous!
lines.chinese: 太精采了!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks!
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Terrific!
lines.chinese: 妙极了!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: They get better and better.
lines.chinese: 它们越来越好。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks a lot.
lines.chinese: 非常感谢。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: This is sensational!
lines.chinese: 太令人激动了!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yeah. Thanks.
lines.chinese: 真的,谢谢。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: What a job! Good work, Richard!
lines.chinese: 你拍得很棒!  好作品, Richard!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'm so glad you like them so much.
lines.chinese: 你这样喜欢它们我真是太高兴了。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Like them? They represent your best work.
lines.chinese: 何止喜欢?它们代表了你最佳的作品。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Really?
lines.chinese: 真的吗?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Absolutely.
lines.chinese: 绝对是真的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well...what do you think? Do I have my book?
lines.chinese: 那么----你看怎么样?我的书可以出版了?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: You do. You do. There's a book here, I'll have a contract and an advance payment waiting for you first thing in the morning.
lines.chinese: 当然。当然。书要出版。 我会准备好一张合同 和预付稿酬等你来 这是我明天早上的第一件事。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: And while you're here I would like to introduce you to your editor. And I want you to meet the people in the art department. I'll set up an appointment with the marketing people.
lines.chinese: 趁你现在在这儿, 我来介绍你认识这本书的编辑。 我也希望你与美术编辑部的人见面。 我会与营销部的人安排一次会谈。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: When do you think we'll be through?
lines.chinese: 你认为我们什么时候能完?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: In about half an hour. Why?
lines.chinese: 大约半个小时。为什么问这个?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I can't wait to tell Marilyn!
lines.chinese: 我迫不及待地想告诉 Marilyn!

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I don't believe it!
lines.chinese: 我简直难以相信!

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: What's wrong?
lines.chinese: 怎么了?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Carter Boswell!
lines.chinese: 谁是 Carter Boswell ?

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Who's Carter Boswell?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: He's running for the school board. The election's next month.
lines.chinese: 他正在竞选联合校董事会董事。 选举就在下个月。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: What's wrong with wanting to be on the school board?
lines.chinese: 想进入联合校董事会有什么不对?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Nothing. But he wants to cut the school budget!
lines.chinese: 没有什么。 但他想削减学校的预算!

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Maybe it needs cutting.
lines.chinese: 也许真需要削减呢。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Cutting the budget is fine, but he wants to do it by cutting all the cultural programs. No music, no dance, no concert, no stage presentations.
lines.chinese: 削减预算是好的, 但他要削减 是透过取消所有的文化活动。 没有音乐,没有舞蹈, 没有音乐会,没有舞蹈表演。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Why does he want to do that?
lines.chinese: 为什么他要那么做呢?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: He says it's to save the taxpayers' money, and I think he believes that the taxpayers' will vote for him if he spends less on the cultural programs.
lines.chinese: 他说这是为了要节省纳税人的钱, 而我认为他相信 纳税人会投他一票 假如他在文化活动上减少开支的话。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: He's probably right. Lots of people want their taxes used for new books and a new paint job in the schoolrooms.
lines.chinese: 他也许是对的。 许多人希望他们缴的税 被用来购买新书 和重新油漆教室。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Maybe some of us would like to pay a little bit more and keep the cultural programs for our kids.
lines.chinese: 也许我们之中有一些人愿意多付一点 以便为我们的孩子保存文化活动。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Well, I'm not sure, Ellen. I hear it from my patients. Lots of people are tired of higher taxes.
lines.chinese: 噢,我无法肯定。Ellen。 我是从我的病人那儿听到这些的。 许多人对于增税很厌烦。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I know, but if Boswell wins he'll be an important decision maker on the school board, and he doesn't know anything about our children's education.
lines.chinese: 我知道, 但是假如 Boswell赢了 他将成为一个重要的决策者 在联合校董事会中, 而他一无所知 对于我们孩子的教育。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Who's running against him?
lines.chinese: 谁与他竞选?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Nobody. That's the problem.
lines.chinese: 没有人。这就是问题所在。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Well, it sounds to me like Carter Boswell is going to win this seat on the board.
lines.chinese: 噢,听起来好像 Carter Boswell 稳登联合校董事会董事的宝座了。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, not if I can stop him!
lines.chinese: 噢,要是我能阻止他,他就甭想!

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: And how are you going to stop him?
lines.chinese: 那你怎么去阻止他呢?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I don't know. Maybe I'll run against him.
lines.chinese: 我不知道。 也许我与他竞选。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Well, You've got my vote.
lines.chinese: 哦,那我一定投你的票。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I'm serious, Philip. Why shouldn't I run?
lines.chinese: 我是认真的,Philip。 为什么我不能竞选?

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Why shouldn't you run for what, Mom?
lines.chinese: 为什么你不可以竞选什么,妈妈?

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Your mother is thinking of running for the school board.
lines.chinese: 你妈妈正考虑 竞选联合校董事会董事。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Hey, that's terrific, Mom!
lines.chinese: 嗨,太棒了,妈妈!

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Against Carter Boswell? Great!
lines.chinese: 和 Carter Boswell 竞争? 太好了!

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Well, if I run for office, the voters will have a clear choice. I stand for everything Boswell doesn't.
lines.chinese: 嗯,假如我出来竞选公职, 投票人可以有明确的选择。 我代表 Boswell所不赞成的观点。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I think a lot of people will vote for you against Boswell, Ellen. I'll vote for you.
lines.chinese: 我想很多人会投票支持你的。 和 Boswell 对抗 Ellen。 我会投你的票。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Will you help me if I do run?
lines.chinese: 假如我真的竞选,你会帮我忙吗?

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Absolutely.
lines.chinese: 当然。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: The trouble is it takes a little bit of money to run a campaign.
lines.chinese: 问题是 竞选活动需要花一些钱。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: I think you can make a difference, Ellen. And in a short campaign you wouldn't need as much money. You know something, Ellen? Why not give the people of Riverdale a clear choice? I'm with you. You can make a difference.
lines.chinese: 我觉得你可以发挥影响力的,Ellen。 而且这一场短暂的竞选 不需要花多少钱。 知道一件事吗,Ellen? 为什么不让 Riverdale 地区的人民有一个明确的选择呢? 我支持你。 你会发挥影响力。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Come in.
lines.chinese: 请进。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Mr. Maxwell?
lines.chinese: 您是 Maxwell 先生吗?

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Yes, Charles Maxwell.
lines.chinese: 是的,我是 Charles Maxwell。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: My name is Ellen Stewart.
lines.chinese: 我叫 Ellen Stewart。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Hello. Please, sit down. You asked to see me. What would you like to see me about?
lines.chinese: 你好。 请坐。 你要求见我。 找我有什么事?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I'd like your help.
lines.chinese: 我要请你帮忙。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Well, I'm editor of the most influential newpaper in Riverdale. Actually, it's the only newspaper. A lot of people would like my help. Do you have a story?
lines.chinese: 噢, 我是Riverdale 最有影响力的报纸的编辑。 事实上,它是唯一的报纸。 许多人要我帮助。 有什么新闻故事呢?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I'm planning to run for the school board.
lines.chinese: 我打算竞选联合校董事会董事。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Against Carter Boswell?
lines.chinese: 和 Carter Boswell 打对手?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Yes.
lines.chinese: 是的。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Well, that is news.
lines.chinese: 噢,这可是个新闻。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Will you announce that I'm running?
lines.chinese: 你会宣布我参加竞选的消息吗?

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Sure. But I need some information.
lines.chinese: 当然。 但我需要一些资料。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Of course.
lines.chinese: 那当然。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Why will the voters vote for you against Boswell, Mrs.Stewart?
lines.chinese: 为什么选民会投票支持你,和Boswell 对抗, 太太?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Because I care.
lines.chinese: 因为我关心。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: "Vote for Ellen Stewart.She cares." Not a bad slogan. But what do you care about?
lines.chinese: 投Ellen Stewart一票。她关心。” 不错的标语。 但你关心什么呢?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Well, I care about the children of our town. I don't want hem to grow up without cultural programs in our school.
lines.chinese: 噢,我关心我们镇上的孩子们。 我不希望在他们成长期 我们的学校没有文化活动。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Do you have a plan?
lines.chinese: 有计划吗?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I want our children to learn more than reading, writing, and airthmetic. I want to keep the after-school programs-the music, the concerts.
lines.chinese: 我要孩子们学习到 比阅读,写作,及算术更多的东西。我要保存这些课外活动 音乐,  音乐会。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: It's not a bad plan. But who's going to pay for all of this?
lines.chinese: 这是个不错的计划。 但谁来为这些付钱呢?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: We are. The citizens of Riverdale, of course. I plan to get help from the businessmen and the corporations of Riverdale.
lines.chinese: 我们呀。 当然是 Riverdale的公民。 我打算争取 的商人和企业的支持。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: That's fair enough. Exactly what do you want from me, Mrs.Stewart?
lines.chinese: 这倒很公平。 究竟对我有什么要求,Stewart 太太?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You don't know me. I can't expect you to take my side a against Boswell. But I do nee some publicity so that the people of our town know that I'm running for office and that I car about our children.
lines.chinese: 你不认识我。 我不敢期望你站在我这一边来对抗 Boswell。 但我需要一些宣传好让我们镇上的人 知道我正在竞选公职 且知道我关心我们的孩子。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: Fair enough. I certainly can print the news. And you are now making news.
lines.chinese: 很公平。 我当然可以刊出这则新闻 而且你现在就是在创造新闻。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Here are the fliers, hot of the press!
lines.chinese: 传单来了,  刚刚印出来!

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Looks good. Simple.
lines.chinese: 看起来很好。 简单明了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Right over there, Grandpa. You fold the fliers, Richard and I will put them into the envelopes.
lines.chinese: 请到这儿来,爷爷。 你来折传单。 我和Richard 把它们放进信封。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We finished addressing over three hundred envelopes.
lines.chinese: 我们写了三百多个信件地址了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Need another box?
lines.chinese: 需要另一个箱子吗?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Good work, gang.
lines.chinese: 做得很好,大伙们。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: Hi, this is Mike Johnson. Can I speak with Mr. Or Mrs.Anderson? Thanks.
lines.chinese: 嗨,我是 Mike Johnson。 我可以跟 Anderson 先生或太太说话吗? 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Mr.Nelson. Hi. This is Robbie Stewart. Did you know my mother is running for the school board?
lines.chinese: 先生。 你好。我是 Robbie Stewart。 你知道 我母亲竞选联合校董事会董事的事么?

lines.speaker: Jimmy
lines.english: Yes, Miss Kim, Ellen Stewart. "She cares." Oh, see you at the polls.
lines.chinese: 是的, Kim小姐,是 Ellen Stewart。 她关心。” 噢,在投票所见。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Certainly, I'll give her your best wishes, Mr.Nelson.
lines.chinese: 当然。 我会转告她你的祝福, 先生。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: Hi, this is Mike Johnson. Can I speak with Mr. or Mrs.Burns? Thank you.
lines.chinese: 嗨,我是 Mike Johnson。 我可以跟 Burns先生或太太通话吗? 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: We have done so much in such a short amount of time. I can't believe it! Wait till Philip come and sees our progress!
lines.chinese: 我们已做了这么多的事 在这么短的时间内。 我简直不敢相信! 等着 Philip 回来看到我们工作的反应。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Everyone saw story in the Riverdale newspaper.
lines.chinese: 每个人都已看到 Riverdale报纸上的新闻。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Mr. Maxwell was very kind to print my announcement.
lines.chinese: 先生很好心 刊登了我宣布竞选的消息。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: It helps enormously. Everybody in riverdale reads his paper.
lines.chinese: 这个帮助很大。 的每个人都读他的报纸。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Your photo in it helped, too.
lines.chinese: 在报纸上的照片也有帮助。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Thanks to you, Richard, it's a good picture.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你, Richard , 那是一张好照片。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Well, hi, all.
lines.chinese: 噢,嗨。

lines.speaker: Richard&Robbie
lines.english: Hi, Dad.
lines.chinese: 你好,爸爸。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Hello, darling.
lines.chinese: 哈,亲爱的。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: May I...may I help?
lines.chinese: 我能……我能帮你忙吗?

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Licking envelopes.
lines.chinese: 粘信封。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I fold the fliers.
lines.chinese: 我折传单。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We stuff them.
lines.chinese: 我们装传单。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: And I lick the envelopes.
lines.chinese: 那我就粘信封 。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Hey, everybody, Mrs.Greenberg is on the phone. She says Carter Boswell is on the TV right now-doing a commercial.
lines.chinese: 嗨,大家听着, 太太打电话 她说 Carter Boswell 现在正在电视上---- 正在做广告。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: What channel?
lines.chinese: 哪个频道?

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Five.
lines.chinese: 号。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Five?
lines.chinese: 号?

lines.speaker: Boswell
lines.english: ...and if you ask what I care about, I'll tell you. I care about the school buildings in need of paint. I care about more lockers for the teachers. I care about new fixtures in the hallways-not music or dancing or entertainment. I care about the practical things. If you do, vote for me, Carter Boswell.
lines.chinese: 假如你们问我关心什么,我可以告诉你们。 我关心学校的建筑物需要粉刷。 我关心教师要有更多的储物柜。 我关心走廊的新灯具--- 而不是音乐,舞蹈或者娱乐。 我关心实用的事物。 假如你有同感请投我一票,Carter Boswell。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: A lot of people will agree with him.
lines.chinese: 许多人会赞同他。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: I told you.
lines.chinese: 我告诉过你了。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Too bad kids can't vote. It's our school, but we can't vote.
lines.chinese: 可惜小孩不能投票。 那是我们的学校,可是我们却不能投票。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: There are people in favor of the cultural programs, Mom.
lines.chinese: 会有许多人赞成文化活动的,妈妈。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: There are, Ellen. Don't be upset by Boswell's commercial.
lines.chinese: 一定有,Ellen。 不要为了 Boswell 的广告而忧心。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: You have to go on television, too.
lines.chinese: 你也应该上电视。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Boswell's a powerful speaker.
lines.chinese: 是个有辩才的家伙。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: You can be, too. Your ideas are good ones.
lines.chinese: 你也能做到。 你的想法很好。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I don't know. I'm not sure I'm up to it.
lines.chinese: 我不知道。 我不能肯定我是否能做得来。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: ...my slogan is "I care". I care about people, not things, Vote for me, Ellen Stewart. I care. How was it?
lines.chinese: 我的口号是  “我关心”  。 我关心人,而不是东西。 请投我,Ellen Stewart 一票。 我关心。 怎么样?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You were terrific!
lines.chinese: 真是太棒了!

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Can I see it?
lines.chinese: 我可以看吗?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Sure.
lines.chinese: 当然可以。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Hello. My name is Ellen Stewart, and I'm running for the open seat on the school board. My slogan is "I care". What does the word care mean?...I care about people, not things. Vote for me, Ellen Stewart. I care.
lines.chinese: 你们好,我叫 Ellen Stewart , 我正在竞选联合校董事会董事的席位。 我的口号是“关心”  。 所谓关心是指什么? 我关心人,而不是东西。 请投我, Ellen Stewart 一票。 我关心。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: I like it, but now what? How can we possibly get it on so Riverdale will see it and hear it?
lines.chinese: 我觉得不错。 但下一步怎么办? 我们怎样才能让 Riverdale 的人 看到和听到它呢?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Leave it to me.
lines.chinese: 交给我来办吧。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: ...I mean that when I say "I care". I care about people, not things. Vote for me, Ellen Stewart. I care.
lines.chinese: 我的意思是当我说“我关心”, 我关心的是人,而不是东西。 请投我, Ellen Stewart 一票。 我关心。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What happened?
lines.chinese: 发生了什么事?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Mom is now on television in every appliance store in Riverdale, except Hamlin's. He's a Boswell voter.
lines.chinese: 妈妈现在出现在电视上 在 Riverdale的每一家电器行, 的店除外。 他是Boswell 的支持者。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: That's a brilliant idea, Richard!
lines.chinese: 这主意太棒了, Richard!

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: You inherited your father's brains.
lines.chinese: 你继承了你父亲的好头脑。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: We got our brains from you, Day.
lines.chinese: 我们从你那儿继承了好头脑,爸爸。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And guess what? I called channel five. Their TV news is going to cover it. "Housewife campaigns in appliance stores." And I'll bet some magazine will pick up the story, too.
lines.chinese: 你们猜怎么着? 我打电话给了第五频道。 他们的电视新闻将报导这一消息。 家庭主妇在电器行进行竞选活动。” 我敢打赌 一些杂志也将刊登这项消息。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Mom, you're going to win! I know it!
lines.chinese: 妈妈,你会赢的! 我知道!

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Hold it, Robbie! Just cool down. I know we're getting some attention now, but in the end the voters will have to decide.
lines.chinese: 停, Robbie! 冷静一点。 我认为我们现在固然渐渐引起一些注意, 但最后却要由选民来决定。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: You're going to win. Trust me!
lines.chinese: 你一会赢的。 相信我!

lines.speaker: Reporter
lines.english: In the hotly contested race for the one seat on the Riverdale School Board, Mrs.Ellen Stewart has taken an early lead.
lines.chinese: 在这场激烈竞赛中 争夺Riverdale 联合校董事会董事的一个席次, 太太初步领先。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: She's winning! Mom, you're winning!
lines.chinese: 她赢了! 妈妈,你赢了!

lines.speaker: Reporter
lines.english: Now returning to other local news...Riverdale High School beat its rival Horace Mann in baseball today...
lines.chinese: 现在再报导其它地方新闻--- 高中 在今天的棒球赛中打败了对手 Horace Mann队----

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: It's too soon to know for certain.
lines.chinese: 现在还太早,不知道确切情况。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: You're ahead. That's better than being behind.
lines.chinese: 你领先呢。 这总比落后要好。

lines.speaker: Reporter
lines.english: More sports after this.
lines.chinese: 广告后还有许多运动消息。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: It's not over yet. Let's just all calm down, and wait for the final results.
lines.chinese: 还没有完呢。 让我们全都冷静下来, 等待最后的结果。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Ellen, why don't you go out to the backyard and get some fresh air?
lines.chinese: 何不去院子 呼吸一些新鲜空气呢?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Thank you, Grandpa. I need some.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你,爷爷。 我确实需要。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: What happened?
lines.chinese: 怎么样?

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: You came very close, Ellen.
lines.chinese: 差了一点点,Ellen。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: You lost by only a hundred and twenty-one votes.
lines.chinese: 仅仅以一百二十一票的微弱差距输了。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I lost.
lines.chinese: 我输了。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You tried, Mom.
lines.chinese: 你尽力了,妈妈。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: You lost by a very small number of votes.
lines.chinese: 你输的票数非常少。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Only one hundred and twenty-one votes! I'm sorry, Mom.
lines.chinese: 仅仅一百二十一票! 我很难过,妈妈。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: There just wasn't enough time.
lines.chinese: 实在是没有足够的时间。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Look, you've made a very strong impressing on our community. You'll have another chance next election.
lines.chinese: 看, 已经留下了非常深刻的印像 给我们的社区。 下次选举时还有机会。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Hello. Oh, yes, yes, Mr.Maxwell.
lines.chinese: 你好。 噢,是的,是的,Maxwell先生。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Hello Mr.Maxwell.
lines.chinese: 哈, Maxwell 先生。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: How are you? I just called to tell you that you are very impressive. You lost the election, but you won the attention of the residents of Riverdale, of Boswell, and of me.
lines.chinese: 好吗? 我打电话是想告诉你 你给人们留下了深刻的印象。 尽管你输了, 但你已引起了 Riverdale地区人们的注目, 和 Boswell,和我的。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Well, thank you, Mr. Maxwell. I appreciate your kind words. I needed that.
lines.chinese: 噢 ,谢谢你,Maxwell先生。 我很感谢你好心的安慰。 我正需要这个。

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: I hear Boswell wants to appoint you to a special arts committee. I'm sending over a reporter in the morning to interview you.
lines.chinese: 我听说 Boswell 将任命你到一个特别艺术委员会。 我将派一位记者 在明天早晨来采访你。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You are?
lines.chinese: 是吗?

lines.speaker: Maxwell
lines.english: I'm going to do an article on "Ellen Stewart-she cares. "Maybe we'll all care now. Good-bye.
lines.chinese: 我准备写一篇文章 叫做”Ellen Stewart----她关心  。” 也许我们大家现在都关心起来了。 再见。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Thank you-and good-bye.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。 再见。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: What was that about?
lines.chinese: 说些什么?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You were right, Philip. I did make a difference in town.
lines.chinese: 你说对了, Philip。 我确实在镇上发挥了影响力。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: And in this family.
lines.chinese: 也在这个家里。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: It looks wrong.
lines.chinese: 看起来不对劲。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Hmmm. It is wrong.
lines.chinese: 嗯。是不对劲。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Are they always that difficult to make?
lines.chinese: 领结这样难看吗？

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: The truth is...yes. I'll try again.
lines.chinese: 老实说……是很难看。 我再试一次。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: At this rate, the wedding will take place tomorrow.
lines.chinese: 照这种速度， 婚礼只有明天举行了。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Not to worry. OK.Here we go.
lines.chinese: 别担心。 好吧。我们重来。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I'm worried, Philip. What if we can't tie the tie?
lines.chinese: 我很担心，Philip。 如果我们不打好领结会怎么样？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: If we can't tie the tie, then there can't be a wedding.
lines.chinese: 假如我们不好打领结， 婚礼就不能举行了。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: You'd better not make Harry any more nervous than he is. Don't worry.We'll figure a way.
lines.chinese: 你最好别让Harry 更加紧张了。 别担心。 我们会想法子的。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: How're you doing, fellas?
lines.chinese: 你们怎么样了，各位？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Not so good, Grandpa. We can't get this bow tie tied. Nobody knows how to do it.Do you?
lines.chinese: 情况不太好，爷爷。 我们打不好领结。 没有人会打。 你会吗？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: No. I never could, either.
lines.chinese: 不会。我也一直都不会。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Well, you have your own tuxedo. How do you tie your bow tie?
lines.chinese: 噢，你有自己的燕尾服。 你怎么打领结的？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Yeah, Grandpa, I've seen you in it. You look great.How do you tie it?
lines.chinese: 对呀，爷爷， 我曾见过你那身打扮。 你看起来很神气。 你怎么打的？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I don't.
lines.chinese: 我没有。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You don't?
lines.chinese: 你没有打？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: What do you mean?
lines.chinese: 你是什么意思呀？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I never could tie one of those...things...bow ties. I have always worn a clip-on bow tie.
lines.chinese: 我从来就不会打那一类的…… 玩意儿……领结。 我都是用夹上去的领结。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: A clip-on?
lines.chinese: 用夹上去的？

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Of course. Now I remember.
lines.chinese: 对啊。 现在我想起来了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Yes, it's so easy. All you do is clip it around under your collar.
lines.chinese: 没错，它真简便。 你所要做的就是 将它夹在领子下方。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We all need one of those.
lines.chinese: 我们都需要一个这样的领结。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: The tuxedo rental store. Do you think they're open?
lines.chinese: 礼服出租店。 你们说现在还开？

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Should be. Sunday's their big day.
lines.chinese: 应该是开的。 星期天是他们生意很火的日子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'll call and find out.
lines.chinese: 我打电话问问看。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Well, if they're not, I'll lend you mine, Harry. You're the only one who really needs to be wearing a tuxedo, anyway.
lines.chinese: 噢，假如店不开的话， 我把我的借给你，Harry。 你是我们当中唯一 真正需要穿燕尾服的，

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Thanks, Grandpa.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你，爷爷。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: I'm the father of the bride. I'm supposed to be worried about my daughter, and here I am with the man that's marrying my daughter-worrying about him.
lines.chinese: 我是新娘的父亲。 我原该为我的女儿担心才是， 如今我却与 将要娶我女儿的人在一起---- 为他操心。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Poor Harry. I know the feeling. Wedding-day litters.
lines.chinese: 可怜的 Harry。 我知道这种感觉。 这叫婚礼紧张症。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Are they open?
lines.chinese: 店开着吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We're in luck. They're open. And they have lots of clip-on bow ties.
lines.chinese: 我们运气好。 店开着。 而且他们有许多夹上去的领结。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I'll bicycle down to the village and get them.
lines.chinese: 我骑脚踏车去镇上拿。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: You'd better hurry, Robbie.
lines.chinese: 你最好快点，Robbie。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: There's lots of time. A little over two hours.
lines.chinese: 时间多得很。 两个钟头多一点。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: In two hours and fifteen minutes I'll be married to Susan.
lines.chinese: 再过两小时十五分 我就要和 Susan 结婚了。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: And be a true member of the Stewart family.
lines.chinese: 也真正成了 Stewart 家庭中的一员。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Oh, you're a lucky guy, Harry. Susan is one of the best women you'll ever find. She's just like her grandma.
lines.chinese: 噢，你是一个幸运的小伙，Harry。 是你能找到的最好的女人之一。 她就像她的祖母一样。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Now, once you put the ring on Susan's finger, you are one of us, Harry. And don't ever forget it.
lines.chinese: 现在，你一旦将戒指戴上 Susan 的手， 你就是我们家中的一员了，Harry。 永远别忘记这一点。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Ring? Ring? Oh my! What did I do with the rings? I put them in the pocket of my sports jacket.
lines.chinese: 戒指？戒指？ 噢 ，我的天！ 我把戒指放哪儿了？ 我把它们放在我运动夹克的口袋。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: No, I think you put them in your tuxedo jacket pocket. Remember?
lines.chinese: 不对， 我想你是放在 礼服上衣口袋。 记得吗？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Right. What did I do with the rings?
lines.chinese: 对。 我把戒指放哪儿了？

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Didn't you give them to Richard? He's your best man. I remember. You gave them to Richard.
lines.chinese: 你不是把它们交给 Richard 了？ 他是男傧相呀。 我记起来了。 你交给了 Richard。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, yeah. I remember now. You handed them to me. What did I do with them?
lines.chinese: 噢，对了。 我现在想起来了。 你将它们给了我。 我把它们放在哪儿？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I hope they aren't lost.
lines.chinese: 但愿没丢才好。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Oh, don't worry, Harry. They have to be here.
lines.chinese: 别担心，Harry。 它们肯定就在这儿。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I remember. I gave them to Robbie to hold so I wouldn't lose them.
lines.chinese: 我想起来了。 我把它们交给 Robbie拿着 免得我弄丢了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Oh, I forget. What time is it?
lines.chinese: 噢，我忘了。 现在是什么时间了？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: It's still a little over two hours, Harry. Just relax.
lines.chinese: 仍然还有两个多小时，Harry  。 不用紧张。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: What about the rings?
lines.chinese: 戒指怎么办？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'm sure Robbie has them.
lines.chinese: 我确定在 Robbie 那儿。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: No, NO, Robbie doesn't have them. He gave them to me to hold on to them because he didn't want the responsibility of holding them. I put them in my tuxedo-but in the lapel pocket. That's a relief. I was really worried.
lines.chinese: 不，不，Robbie没有拿。 他给我戒指要我自己保管 因为他不想承担保管它们的责任。 我将它们放在我礼服的口袋--- 但是在胸前的口袋。 现在我放心了。 刚才我真的很担心。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'll hold on to them for you. The best man always keeps the rings.
lines.chinese: 我为你保管吧。 男傧相总是保管戒指的。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: You're right. You're right. You hold on to them so there won't be problem later.
lines.chinese: 你说的对。 你来保管免得等一下又出问题了。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Well, now, I think we'd better get dressed, fellas.
lines.chinese: 好了， 现在我们最好去穿好衣服，各位。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: All right!
lines.chinese: 好的！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Robbie will bring the ties back.
lines.chinese: 会拿领结回来。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Hey, leaving me?
lines.chinese: 嗨，丢下我啦？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: You'll be fine.
lines.chinese: 你不会有事的。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Try to take it easy. It'll all be over in two hours.
lines.chinese: 放轻松点。 再有两个小时就结束了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Over?
lines.chinese: 结束？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: The wedding ceremony will be over. You'll be husband and wife.
lines.chinese: 婚礼会结束。 你们就成为夫妻了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I guess you're right. Two hours from now.Two hours from now.
lines.chinese: 说的也对。 从现在起还有两个小时 从现在起两小时。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And now for...something old, something new, something borrowed, and something blue.
lines.chinese: 现在接着是……一些旧的， 一些新的，一些借来的， 和一些蓝色的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: OK. Let's see. Something borrowed. That's this dress. Borrowed from Marilyn.
lines.chinese: 好。让我们看看。 借来的东西。 那就是这件礼服。跟 Marilyn 借的。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Something blue. My wristband. I wore it when I married your father.
lines.chinese: 蓝色的东西。 我的腕带。 我和你父亲结婚时戴着它。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, Mother. I forgot you still had it. It's just so lovely.
lines.chinese: 噢，妈妈。我忘了你仍然保留着它。 它是那么可爱。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I bought it in an antique shop when I was about eighteen years old. I saved it for my wedding day.
lines.chinese: 我在一家古董店买的。 那时我差不多十八岁。 我留着等我的结婚日才用。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: And you saved it for me, didn't you, Mother?
lines.chinese: 也留着要给我，对吗，妈妈？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Yes, honey I did. Are we ever going to finish?
lines.chinese: 是的，亲爱的。是这样。 我们这样谈得完吗？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: All right. OK. Something borrowed.
lines.chinese: 好吧。行了。 借的东西。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: The wedding dress. And something blue.
lines.chinese: 结婚礼服。蓝色的东西。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: The wristband. Something old?
lines.chinese: 腕带。旧的东西？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Something old. Right. What's old?
lines.chinese: 旧的东西。 对了，旧的东西是什么？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Of course! Something old. I had planned to wear them.
lines.chinese: 当然有！ 旧的东西。 我早就预备戴它们。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Grandma's pearls! Oh, Grandpa will be so pleased that you're wearing them. I'm sure he misses Grandma on a day like this.
lines.chinese: 祖母的珍珠项链！ 噢，戴了它，祖父一定会非常高兴。 我想在这样的日子祖父一定会思念祖母。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Help me with them, Marilyn. I've never worn them before. I've been saving them for today.
lines.chinese: 帮我戴上，Marilyn。 我从来没有戴过这个。 我一直留着等今天才戴。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Saving them for today? Oh, you're a real Stewart!
lines.chinese: 留着等今天？ 噢，是一个真正 Stewart家的成员！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, there you go. Lovely!
lines.chinese: 噢  一切都好了。 太可爱了  ！

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Lovely!
lines.chinese: 太可爱了  ！

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Lovely!
lines.chinese: 太可爱了  ！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Something borrowed, something blue. Something old... and now for something new.
lines.chinese: 借的东西，蓝色的东西。 旧的东西…… 现在轮到新的东西了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: The veil. Oh, it's so beautiful, Marilyn!
lines.chinese: 面纱。 噢，它好漂亮， Marilyn！

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You really are a fabulous designer, Marilyn.
lines.chinese: 真是一个绝佳的设计师  ，Marilyn。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Doesn't it look jst right on Susan?
lines.chinese: 戴起来正好，不是吗？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Perfect!
lines.chinese: 太棒了！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And when you both say, "I do," Harry will lift this veil over your head and kiss the bride.
lines.chinese: 当你们两人都说,“我愿意”  时， 会掀起面纱 吻新娘。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, I'm so excited!
lines.chinese: 噢，我真是太兴奋了！

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: When you said, "I do, " Marilyn, it suddenly became real.
lines.chinese: 当你说 “我愿意” 时， Marilyn， 一切突然成真了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: That's all right, Susan. You've got the wedding-day jitters! In less than two hours, you will be Mrs.Harry Bennett.
lines.chinese: 没有错，Susan。 你已经得了婚礼紧张症！ 在不到两个小时之后， 将成为Harry Bennett 太太。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, that reminds me. If we don't get dressed, we won't be there to see Susan become Mrs. Harry Bennett.
lines.chinese: 噢，倒提醒了我。 假如我们不去打扮， 我们将无法 目睹 Susan成为 Harry Bennett 太太。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Before you leave-do I look all right?
lines.chinese: 在你们离开之前---告诉我我这样子可以吗？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You never looked better.
lines.chinese: 再漂亮不过了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Mom.
lines.chinese: 妈妈。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: She's right. And that's dear little Max. Got to go and feed him.
lines.chinese: 她说得没错。 那是亲爱的小 Max。 我得走了该去喂他了。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: What am I Supposed to do?
lines.chinese: 我该做什么？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Take off the veil, kick off your shoes, and sit down.
lines.chinese: 拿掉面纱， 脱掉你的鞋子，坐下来。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: We'll come upstairs and get you in a little while.
lines.chinese: 我们待会儿就上楼来带你下去。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Richard's going to take some wedding pictures before the ceremony. So just relax.
lines.chinese: 准备拍一些结婚照片 在婚礼前。 所以放轻松点。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Are you kidding? Relax?
lines.chinese: 在开玩笑吗？放轻松？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Susan Stewart...you are about to become Susan Bennett-Mrs. Harry Bennett.
lines.chinese: 即将成为 Susan Bennett---- 太太。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: Philip...Ellen.

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Ah, Judge.
lines.chinese: 噢，法官。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: How are you? How are you?
lines.chinese: 你好吗？ 好吗,亲爱的？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Hello.
lines.chinese: 你好。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: I think it's time for the wedding to begin.
lines.chinese: 我想是婚礼开始的时候了。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: All right, ladies and gentlemen. Please take your places. The wedding ceremony is about to begin.
lines.chinese: 好啦，女士们，先生们。 请大家就座。 婚礼就要开始了。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: OK, Jane. Start the music.
lines.chinese: 好，Jane。音乐开始。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: Dearly beloved, we are gathered here today to join this man and this woman in holy matrimony. Do either of you have any reason why you should not legally be joined in marriage? Is there anyone present who can show any just cause why these two people should not be legally joined in marriage? Then, Harry Bennett, do you take Susan Stewart to be your lawful, wedded wife?
lines.chinese: 敬爱的人们， 我们今天聚集在这儿 见证这位男士和这位女士的神圣婚姻。两位之中可有哪位因为某项原因。 在座可有人能提出 任何正当理由说明这两位 不该合法结婚的？ 那么，Harry Bennett，你愿意接受 Susan Stewart 做你合法妻子吗？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I do.
lines.chinese: 我愿意。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: And you, Susan Stewart, do you take Harry Bennett to be your lawful, wedded husband?
lines.chinese: 那么，Susan Stewart ， 你愿意接受 Harry Bennett 为你合法的丈夫吗？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I do
lines.chinese: 我愿意。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: The rings, please.
lines.chinese: 请戴戒指。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I have them, Harry.
lines.chinese: 在我这儿,Harry  。

lines.speaker: Judge
lines.english: By the power vested in me by the laws of the State of New York, I now pronounce you husband and wife. You may kiss the bride now, Harry.
lines.chinese: 凭着纽约州法律赋予我的权利， 我现在宣布你们成为夫妻。 你可以吻新娘了，Harry。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Where's Mom?
lines.chinese: 妈妈呢?

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: She went to a school-board meeting.
lines.chinese: 她去参加联合校董事会会议了。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I don't know how she does it. She sure keeps busy.
lines.chinese: 我不知道她怎么办到的。 她真的是忙个不停。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: It's import to her.There are lots of places to go, lots of things to do. She can't sit around and do nothings to do. She can't sit around and do nothing. Philip works late.
lines.chinese: 这对她来说很重要。 有很多地方要去， 许多事情要做。 她总不能闲坐着无所事事呀。 工作到很晚。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I guess you're right. I wish I had her energy.
lines.chinese: 我想你说得对。 我希望我有她那样的精力。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Anybody home?
lines.chinese: 有人在家吗？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: We're in here, Dad.
lines.chinese: 我们在里面，爸爸。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Oh. Hi, gang.
lines.chinese: 噢。嗨，你们好。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Hello, Philip. How was your day?
lines.chinese: 哈，Philip。 今天好吗？

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: My day was just fine. So was my night. It's almost ten 0'clock. Mmm.I'm starving. Um...where's Mom?
lines.chinese: 这一天还好。 晚上也还不错。 现在已经快十点钟了。 嗯，我饿坏了。 唔……妈妈呢？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: She went to a school-board meeting.
lines.chinese: 她去参加联合校董事会的会议。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: There's a note for you on the refrigerator.
lines.chinese: 冰箱上有她留给你的字条。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Oh?

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Did you have dinner, Robbie?
lines.chinese: 你吃晚餐了吗， Robbie？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Yeah. Mike and I had a hamburger at the diner. I came home a little while ago. You've been working late almost every night this week, Dad. Aren't you exhausted?
lines.chinese: 吃了。 和我在火车厢餐厅那儿吃了汉堡。 我也是刚刚到家。 你一直工作到很晚 几乎这个星期的每天晚上都如此，爸爸。 你不会精疲力尽吗？

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: I don't have time to be exhausted.
lines.chinese: 我没有时间去精疲力尽。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: You and Mom haven't had dinner together with us in almost a full week.
lines.chinese: 你和妈妈没有和我们一起吃晚饭 差不多有一整个星期了。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Yeah, I feel bad about us not having dinner with the family, but our schedules are so different. Either I'm at the hospital doing paperwork, or Mom is at a committee meeting. I frankly don't know what to do about it.
lines.chinese: 是啊。 我觉得很不安 对于我们无法与全家人共进晚餐， 但我们的工作时间实在差太多了。不是我在医院办公事， 就是妈妈参加小组会议。 我真不知道该怎么解决。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I'm worried about you and Mom. You really have been working too hard.
lines.chinese: 我很担心你和妈妈。 你们确实太忙了。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Well, I think I've had enough of that sandwich.
lines.chinese: 好啦， 我想我已经吃够了三明治。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: You didn't finish it.
lines.chinese: 你没有吃完它。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: It's not good to eat before going to bed. A cookie can't hurt, though.
lines.chinese: 在上床前吃太多不好。 但一片小甜饼不要紧。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Well, I'm heading off for bed and a good night's sleep.
lines.chinese: 好啦， 我先去睡觉了 睡一个好觉。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Well, good night.
lines.chinese: 好吧，晚安。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Good night, son. Good night, Dad. I'm going to bed.
lines.chinese: 晚安，儿子。 晚安，爸爸。 我去睡觉了。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Good night, Philip.
lines.chinese: 晚安，Philip。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Haven't you finished balancing that checkbook?
lines.chinese: 你还没有核对完支票簿帐目？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I found another mistake. I'll be off to bed myself in a minute.
lines.chinese: 我又发现一个错误。 我再过一会也要上床睡觉了。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: OK.Good night.
lines.chinese: 好，晚安。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I'm really concerned about them, Grandpa.
lines.chinese: 我真的为他们担心， 爷爷。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Concerned about whom?
lines.chinese: 为谁担心？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: About Mom and Dad. They hardly ever see each other. Dad often works late, and Mom has all these committees she's on.
lines.chinese: 爸爸和妈妈。 他们几乎彼此不碰头。 爸爸老是工作到很晚， 妈妈又有那么多的小组会议要开。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: What do you propose to do about it? You have that look in your eye.
lines.chinese: 那你建议要怎么办？ 你看起来好像有了主意似的。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I don't know, but there must be a way of getting them to spend more time together. Quality time.
lines.chinese: 我不知道， 但总该有一个办法 能让他们有更多时间相处。 黄金时光。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Hello there, Robbie. What are you doing up this late?
lines.chinese: 哈，Robbie。 这么晚了你还没睡在做什么？

lines.speaker: Robbie
