lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Oh, by he way, when is your plane reservation for your flight to Athens?
lines.chinese: 噢,顺便问一下 飞 Athens订的是什么时候的班机?

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Sunday. Why?
lines.chinese: 星期天。为什么问这?

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Would you be able to come over Saturday night?
lines.chinese: 星期六晚上能过来吗?

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Yes. I should be finished packing by then.
lines.chinese: 能。 到那时候我应该已经照理好行李了。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: My folks are giving me a little graduation party.
lines.chinese: 我的亲人要为我办一个小小的毕业派对。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Terrific!
lines.chinese: 太棒了!

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I'll pick you up.
lines.chinese: 我去接你。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: That isn't necessary.
lines.chinese: 这倒不必。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: My dad's letting me borow the car.
lines.chinese: 我爸爸借我用汽车。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Oh, well, that would be very nice.
lines.chinese: 噢,好吧,  那倒很好。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: About eight 0'clock?
lines.chinese: 差不多八点钟好吗?

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Eight 0'clock is fine.
lines.chinese: 八点钟可以。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Great! It's a date.
lines.chinese: 太好了! 就说定了。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Who's going to be there?
lines.chinese: 有谁要参加?

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Just my friend Mike and a few kids from school.
lines.chinese: 只有我的朋友 Mike 和一些学校的几个伙伴。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: I'm going to miss all of you. You've been like a second family to me.
lines.chinese: 我会想念你们大家的。 你们就像我的第二个家庭一样。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: We're going to miss you.
lines.chinese: 我们也会想念你的。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Maybe you could come to visit me in Greece?
lines.chinese: 也许你可以到希腊来玩?

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I'm counting on it.
lines.chinese: 我有这种打算。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Wonderful! Are you excited about graduating from high school?
lines.chinese: 太棒了! 你从高中毕业会不会感到很兴奋呢?

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Sure...and a little scared.
lines.chinese: 当然……还有一点紧张。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Scared? Why?
lines.chinese: 紧张?  为什么?

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Aren't you a little scared?
lines.chinese: 你不会感到有点紧张?

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: I was when I first came to the United States. I'd never been away from home, and I didn't know what it would be like. But then I found out that people are the same everywhere once you get to know them.
lines.chinese: 当我刚到美国来的时候是感到有点紧张。 我从来没有离开过家, 而且我也不知道会是怎么一个样子。 但是后来我发现哪儿的人都是一样的 一旦认识了周围的人以后。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I'm glad I got to know you.
lines.chinese: 我很高兴能够认识

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Thatnk you. I'm glad I got to know You-and your family.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。 我也很高兴能够认识你和你的一家人。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Wouldn't it be nice if we could skip the examinations and get right to the graduation party?
lines.chinese: 要是我们可以略过考试那岂不是很好 直接就是毕业派对？

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: It would be very nice. But that isn't the way it works. So...back to work.
lines.chinese: 那当然是很好。 但是现实不是如此。 因此……用功吧。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Good night, Robbie. Good night, Mrs.Stewart. Thank you for dinner.
lines.chinese: 晚安,Robbie。 晚安, Stewart 太太。 谢谢你的晚餐。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Good night, Alexandra.Will you be all right?
lines.chinese: 晚安,Alexandra。 不会有问题吧?

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Yes.The Molinas are waiting for me.
lines.chinese: 不会。Molina一家人正等着我呢。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Good night, Alexandra. I'll pick you up on Saturday night, OK?
lines.chinese: 晚安, Alexandra。 我星期六晚上去接你，好吗？

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Yes. Eight 0'clock.
lines.chinese: 好的。 八点钟。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: I have to run.
lines.chinese: 我得赶紧走了。

lines.speaker: Allen
lines.english: Good night, Robbie. She's really a good friend, isn't she?
lines.chinese: 晚安，Robbie。 她真是个好朋友，不是吗？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Yeah.
lines.chinese: 是的。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You are going to miss her.
lines.chinese: 你会想念她的。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Am I going to miss her?
lines.chinese: 我会想念她吗？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: That's what I said! You are going to miss her!
lines.chinese: 我正是这么说的！ 你会想念她的！

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: I'm going to miss her, and my math teacher's going to miss her.
lines.chinese: 我会想念她， 而我的数学老师也会想念她。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Well, you can write to her.
lines.chinese: 嗯，你可以写信给她。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: It's not the same.
lines.chinese: 那可不一样。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: How does that look, Rob?
lines.chinese: 看起来怎么样，Rob？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Great! Pass me the hammer.
lines.chinese: 很好！把钉子给我。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: You got it!
lines.chinese: 给你啦！

lines.speaker: Sandra
lines.english: You're sure Alexandra's going to be surprised?
lines.chinese: 你确定Alexandra 会很惊讶？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Absolutely. She has no idea that the party is in her honor.
lines.chinese: 绝对。 她不知道这个派对是为她办的。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: Millie, did you bring the papes for dancing?
lines.chinese: 带来了跳舞的录音带吗？

lines.speaker: Millie
lines.english: They're in my bag.
lines.chinese: 在我袋子里。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: What did you bring?
lines.chinese: 带的什么带子？

lines.speaker: Millie
lines.english: Some rock 'n' roll.
lines.chinese: 一些摇滚乐。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Perfect! Alexandra will love it! I can't wait to see her face when she walks in here tonight.
lines.chinese: 太好了！ Alexandra会喜欢的！ 我迫不及待想看到她吃惊时脸上的表情。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: What about the cake?
lines.chinese: 蛋糕怎么样？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: My mom's decorating it right now.
lines.chinese: 我妈妈正在淋奶油。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Robbie, there's a phone call for you. It's Alexandra. She sounds upset.
lines.chinese: 有你的电话。 是 Alexandra。 她听起来有点不对劲。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: OK. Thanks, Mom. I'll take it in there. Hi, Alexandra. What? You what? Oh no!
lines.chinese: 好。 妈，谢谢。 我在那儿接。 嗨，Alexandra。 什么？ 你怎样？ 噢，糟糕！

lines.speaker: Millie
lines.english: How about some music?
lines.chinese: 来点音乐怎么样？

lines.speaker: Sandra
lines.english: No, let's wait till Robbie gets back from the airport.
lines.chinese: 不要。我们等 Robbie 从机场回来吧。

lines.speaker: Millie
lines.english: Did she say why she had to leave today?
lines.chinese: 她说了她为什么今天非离开不可吗？

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: She told Robbie that her flight tomorrow was canceled, so she had to take an earlier flight today.
lines.chinese: 她告诉 Robbie 说，她明天的班机取消了， 所以她不得不乘今天早一班的班机。

lines.speaker: Millie
lines.english: This is terrible.
lines.chinese: 真是太糟了。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: Well, that must be him.
lines.chinese: 噢，一定是他。

lines.speaker: Sandra
lines.english: Why is he blowing his horn like that?
lines.chinese: 为什么他这样子按喇叭？

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: I don't know. Maybe he's angry.
lines.chinese: 我不知道。 也许他在生气。

lines.speaker: Sandra
lines.english: Do you think we should take down the decorations? They'll just make him sad.
lines.chinese: 你觉得我们应当取掉这些装饰品吗？ 那些只会让他悲伤。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: Too late now. We should have done it sooner. Oh, here he comes.
lines.chinese: 现在太迟了。 我们早就该取下来的。 噢，他来了。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: Hi, How'd it go?
lines.chinese: 嗨。 怎么样啦？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: OK, I guess. Especially when Alexandra gave me a surprise...
lines.chinese: 我想还好吧。 特别是 Alexandra给了我一个惊喜……

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: Yeah? What was it?
lines.chinese: 是吗？是什么惊喜？

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: This!
lines.chinese: 这个！

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: Alexandra!
lines.chinese: 嗨！怎么回事？

lines.speaker: Sandra
lines.english: Hi! What happened?

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: I called my parents from the airport. When I told them my friends were giving me a party, they insisted that I stay. So now I'm taking a flight on Monday instead.
lines.chinese: 我从机场打电话给我父亲。 当我告诉他们 我的朋友们为我举办了一个派对时， 他们坚持要我留下来。 所以我现在要改搭星期一的班机走。

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: That's great! Terrific!
lines.chinese: 那太好了！太棒了！

lines.speaker: Sandra
lines.english: But how did you know the party was for you?
lines.chinese: 但是你怎么知道派对是为你举办的呢？

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Well, Robbie told me when he gave me this.
lines.chinese: 嗯，当Robbie 送给我这个的时候他告诉了我。

lines.speaker: Sandra
lines.english: Oh, it's so pretty!
lines.chinese: 噢，它好漂亮！

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: It's lovely, Robbie.
lines.chinese: 它非常可爱，Robbie。

lines.speaker: Millie
lines.english: All right! Now we can really start the party!
lines.chinese: 好啦！ 现在我们可以真正开始舞会了！

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: No. Would you mind? I'd like to say something first.
lines.chinese: 不。你们不介意吧？ 我想先说几句话。

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Hear, hear!
lines.chinese: 我们洗耳恭听！

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: I should just like to thank all of you, my friends, who have made my stay in the Unite States so wonderful. And to Robbie and the Stwart family for opening their home to me.
lines.chinese: 我只是想谢谢你们大家， 我的朋友们， 是你们使得我在美国的生活如此多采多姿。 而且感谢 Robbie 和 Stewart全家 为我敞开大门。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: We should thank you. You're a real friend!
lines.chinese: 我们应当感谢你。 你是个真正的朋友！

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: And I also have a little surprise for you, Robbie. A little going-away present.
lines.chinese: 我也有一个小小的惊喜给你，Robbie。 一个小小的临别礼物。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Open it, please. I think you might be amused.
lines.chinese: 请打开看看。 我想你可能会觉得有点迷惑。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: OK. I can't believe it!
lines.chinese: 好的。我简直无法相信！

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: I guess we were thinking the same thought.
lines.chinese: 我想我们的心意相同。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: You had it engraved!
lines.chinese: 在上面刻了字！

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Read it, Robbie.
lines.chinese: 读出来，Robbie。

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: "In friendship, always. Alexandra." I knew we thought alike, but this is too much! Thank you.
lines.chinese: 友情永不渝，Alexandra。” 我知道我们心意相同， 但是这太令人难以置信！ 谢谢  。 好了！ 我们来点音乐吧！

lines.speaker: Mike
lines.english: All right! Let's have some music!

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Miss Pappas?
lines.chinese: 小姐？

lines.speaker: Alexandra
lines.english: Mr.Stewart...

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Mrs. Stewart, may I have this dance?
lines.chinese: 太太，我可以请你跳这支舞吗?

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: My pleasure, Doctor.
lines.chinese: 非常荣幸,  医生。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Hot dogs. I love hot dogs. There is nothing better than a hot dog in the country.
lines.chinese: 热狗。我喜欢热狗。 在美国乡村没有比热狗更好的东西了。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Hot dogs and mustard.
lines.chinese: 热狗和芥末。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Cooked outdoors over an open fire. I wish I had one now.
lines.chinese: 在户外的火堆上烤。 我真希望现在就来一个。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Do you remember when Daddy and I used to take you and Susan and Robbie to Jones Beach?
lines.chinese: 你还记得爸爸和我常带你 去 Jones海滨的事吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, I sure do.
lines.chinese: 哦，我当然记得。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: We'd wait until dark and make a fire, and we'd cook the hot dogs. Oh, don't forget the mustard. And, oh, does anybody want ketchup?
lines.chinese: 我们等到天黑就升火， 然后烤热狗。 噢，不要忘了芥末。噢，有人要番茄酱吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I might as well take it along. And now to make sure we've got the hamburger patties. I have to remember to put them in the bag tomorrow morning before we leave.
lines.chinese: 我不妨带着。 现在务必确信带上汉堡碎肉饼。 我必须记得将它们放进袋子 在明天早上临走之前。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I'll remind you, Richard.
lines.chinese: 我会提醒你的， Richard。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Mom, we really appreciate your taking care of Max for the weekend and giving up your free time.
lines.chinese: 妈， 我们真心感谢你周末照顾 Max 放弃你的闲暇时间。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I love doing it. Susan and Harry have a sitter for Michelle in the city, and I'm taking care of Max. It's no big deal. I am happy to do it for you.
lines.chinese: 我爱这样做。 和 Harry在城里给 Michelle 找到一位临时保姆， 我就来照顾 Max。 这没有什么。 我很高兴为你们做这件事。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I guess Max is asleep by now. He's not crying anymore.
lines.chinese: 我想 Max现在已经睡觉了。 他不再哭了。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, poor baby. He's teething.
lines.chinese: 噢，可怜的宝贝。 他正在长牙齿。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, he's asleep-finally. I feel so bad for him. It hurts so much when a baby gets his first teeth.
lines.chinese: 好了，他总算睡着了。 我为他难过。 小孩子长第一颗牙齿时好痛哟。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: He'll be fine, Marilyn.
lines.chinese: 他不会有问题的，Marilyn。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, he wakes up several times during the night, and the pain is so bad.
lines.chinese: 噢，他一晚醒了好几次， 疼得很厉害。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I'm really concerned about going away for the weekend, Ellen, and leaving you with the full responsibility of taking care of Max. Especially with his teething. I wish he felt better.
lines.chinese: 我这个周末外出真快乐，Ellen， 还要把照顾 Max 的全部责任留给你  。 特别是他现在开始长牙。 我真希望他现在好一点。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Please don't worry, Marilyn. Remember your father-in-law's a pediatrician. We have a live-in doctor if there's a problem I can't handle.
lines.chinese: 请别担心，Marilyn。 记住你公公是小儿科医师。 假如我遇到不能解决的问题我们还有位驻家医生。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I agree, Marilyn. We really don't have to be overly concerned.
lines.chinese: 我同意，Marilyn。 我们真的不需要过分担心。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'll go upstairs and stay with him until he falls asleep again.
lines.chinese: 我上楼去陪他直到他又睡着。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thanks, Richard. Try putting him across your lap on his stomach. He likes that.
lines.chinese: 谢谢，Richard。 试试让他趴着睡在你大腿上。 他喜欢那样。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I"ll try it.
lines.chinese: 我试试吧。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: When are Susan and Harry Picking you up, Marilyn?
lines.chinese: 和 Harry什么时候来接你们，Marilyn？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: They're coming by at six tomorrow morning, so we can get an early start.
lines.chinese: 他们明天早晨六点钟过来。 这样我们可以早点出发。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: That's nice. And you'll have a full day in the country.
lines.chinese: 很好。 你们就可以在乡下呆一整天啦。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And a full night. Tomorrow night we'll be camping out in tents.
lines.chinese: 还有一整个晚上。 明天晚上我们将露宿在帐蓬。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: And coming home on Sunday?
lines.chinese: 星期日回来？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We'll be heading back late in the afternoon.
lines.chinese: 我们会在黄昏时打道回府。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: You're going to have the time of your lives. Camping out is such great fun.
lines.chinese: 你们将会玩得非常痛快。 露营是件很好玩的事。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: We'll have a great time camping out, I'm sure. But I'm still a little worried about you, Ellen.
lines.chinese: 我确定我们会在露营时玩得很开心。 但我还是有点牵挂着你， Ellen。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: It will be my great pleasure, Marilyn. Remember, it's only one night.
lines.chinese: 我也会很快乐的，Marilyn。 记住，只是一个晚上。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: He's asleep. I think he'll sleep through the night now.
lines.chinese: 他睡着了。 我想他现在会睡到天亮。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thanks, honey. I hope he's good when we're away.
lines.chinese: 谢谢，亲爱的。 我希望我们不在时他很乖。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, so do I. Now, to check the list of things we need for the camping trip. We need to bring a flashlight.
lines.chinese: 嗯，我也希望如此。 现在，让我们检查一下露营旅行所需物品的清单吧。 我们需要带一个手电筒。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: It's in the right-hand drawer, next to the bottle openers.
lines.chinese: 在右手边的抽屉里，就在开瓶器旁边。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Do we have a bottle opener on the list, Marilyn?
lines.chinese: 清单上有开瓶器吗，Marilyn？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: No.No bottle opener. Is that one of the things Susan and Harry are bringing?
lines.chinese: 没有，没有开瓶器。 那是不是 Susan 和 Harry要带的物品之一？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: No. And we're bring the ketchup, mustard, relish-all that stuff. And cooking utensils. Well, here's the bottle poener, and here's the flashlight.
lines.chinese: 不是。 我们还要番茄酱呀，芥末，调味酱诸如此类的东西。 还有厨具。 哦，这是开瓶器，这是手电筒。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Matches.
lines.chinese: 火柴。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Matches?
lines.chinese: 火柴？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yes, of course. For when we build our campfire. I can't build a fire by rubbing two pieces of wood together.
lines.chinese: 是的，要的。 用来生营火的。 我总不能靠磨擦两片木柴来取火吧。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And don't forget your camera and film, Richard.
lines.chinese: 别忘了你的照相机和底片，Richard。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: All packed and ready.
lines.chinese: 都打包准备好了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And let's not forget our cassette player and some tapes. Some music tapes and some blank tapes so that we can record our thoughts about the trip.
lines.chinese: 还别忘了带上我们的卡式录音机和一些录音带。 带一些音乐带和一些空白带 以便我们录下旅行中的感想。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, that's a nice idea. An audio diary.
lines.chinese: 噢，这是个好主意。 来一段录音游记。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: That's what I thought.
lines.chinese: 我就是这么想的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Got it! The cassette player and the tapes.
lines.chinese: 找到了！ 卡式录音机和录音带。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Oh, I love it! To be away from the city on such a beautiful day is my idea of heaven! The grass. The trees. The fresh air.
lines.chinese: 我太喜欢它了！ 在这样美好的一天远离都市 正是我所想象的天堂！ 草地，树木，新鲜空气。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I told you you'd like it. I've been doing this for years.
lines.chinese: 我说过你会喜欢的。我多年来一直从事这项活动。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You have the spirit of a teenager, Susan. Wait till you see yourself jumping around.
lines.chinese: 有一股青少年的朝气，Susan。 等着看你到处乱蹦的样子吧。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: That's what I love about Susan. She works hard. She plays hard. She's a real Stewart.
lines.chinese: 这就是我喜欢 Susan的一点。 工作起来努力。 玩起来也拼命。 她是一个真正的 Stewart 家的人。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Come on, Richard. Help me get this stuff out of the car.
lines.chinese: 过来，Richard。 帮我将这些东西拿下车子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Where does it go?
lines.chinese: 放在哪儿？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, we'll put everything over there. We'll set up our tents over there by the edge of the woods. Then we'll be able to make our fire there where it's safe.
lines.chinese: 嗯，我们将所有的东西都放在那边。 我们在林边搭帐蓬。 然后，我们就可以在那儿找一个安全的地方生火了。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, there's our table and benches. All set for eating.
lines.chinese: 哦，那是我们的桌子和长板凳。 吃饭用的东西都摆好了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: That's what makes this spot so good.
lines.chinese: 那就是为什么这个地点这样好的道理。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Is anybody hungry?
lines.chinese: 有人饿了？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I am.
lines.chinese: 我饿了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: We just got here.
lines.chinese: 我们才刚到这儿。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: When you're out in the fresh air like this, it makes you hungry. Aren't you hungry, Marilyn?
lines.chinese: 当你来外面呼吸这么新鲜的空气时，就很容易饿。 你不饿吗，Marilyn？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I sure am. How about you, Richard?
lines.chinese: 我确实饿了。 你呢，Richard？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Starving.
lines.chinese: 很饿。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: You guys are like three kids.
lines.chinese: 你们就像三个孩子。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Aren't you hungry, Harry?
lines.chinese: 你不饿吗，Harry？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, yeah. Now that you mention it, I guess I am. I mean, how could a guy not be hungry with all this talk about eating?
lines.chinese: 嗯，饿啊。 你们一提到我想我也饿了。 我的意思， 当大家都谈吃的，谁能不饿呢？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Where's the bag with the chicken salad sandwiches?
lines.chinese: 放鸡肉色拉三明治的袋子在哪？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Right here, next to the ice packs. Here. Put the tablecloth on the picnic table, and I will bring the cola and the plastic cups.
lines.chinese: 就在这，在冰袋旁边。这儿。 将桌巾铺在餐桌上， 我拿可乐和塑胶杯来。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, how do you like it so far?
lines.chinese: 好了，到现在为止你们感觉怎么样？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I've never been more relaxed.
lines.chinese: 我从来没有这样轻松过。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Me, Neither.
lines.chinese: 我也没有。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I wish we had brought Michelle, Harry. She would have loved it.
lines.chinese: 我真希望你们带了 Michelle 来，Harry。 她会喜欢的。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, You're right. I wish we had brought Max.
lines.chinese: 哦，说得对。 我也希望我们带了 Max来。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Marilyn, Susan, let's not begin to worry about Michelle and Max. We're having a good time, and they're in good hands.
lines.chinese: 不要开始担心 Michelle和 Max。 我们玩得快乐，他们也得到很好的照顾。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Richard is right. Are you having a good time, Susan? You haven't answered my question.
lines.chinese: 是对的。 玩得高兴吗，Susan？ 你还没有回答我的问题呢。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I am having a good time, Harry. I promise not to think about the city. We're in the country. Let's all just enjoy this wonderful place and this wonderful weather.
lines.chinese: 我玩得很高兴，Harry。 我保证不去想城里的事。 我们在乡村。 让我们大家都来享受这个美妙的地方 和这种美妙天气吧。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Good.
lines.chinese: 好。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: This is heaven, Harry! It was such a great idea to spend the weekend this way.
lines.chinese: 这简直是上帝过得日子，Harry！ 这样过周末的主意简直太棒了。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Thanks, Susan. I thought you'd all like it.
lines.chinese: 谢谢，Susan。 我料想你们都会喜欢的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Like it? I love it! Listen to the sounds of the summer that surround us. It's so calming.
lines.chinese: 喜欢？ 我爱上它了！ 仔细倾听环绕我们的夏夜声息。 好宁静。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I've always liked camping out. Away from the telephones and account books. It's refreshing for me. I always go back to the city in a wonderful state of mind.
lines.chinese: 我一直喜欢露营。 远离电话和帐本。 这是我提起精神的方法。 我再回到城市时，精神总是十分饱满。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: We've only been here for a day, and I've completely forgotten about all my business problems. The office seems so far away.
lines.chinese: 我们到这儿仅仅一天， 但我已经抛开了我所有的业务上的问题。 办公室好像变得那么遥远。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: I'm glad you like it, Susan. We'll spend many more weekends like this.
lines.chinese: 我很高兴你喜欢它，Susan。 我们以后要多度几次这样的周末。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: And next time we'll bring Michelle, I wish she were here with us to enjoy the country.
lines.chinese: 下次我们会带 Michelle 来。 我真希望现在她就和我们在一起享受这乡村美景。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: And next time we'll bring Michelle.
lines.chinese: 下次我们带 Michelle 来。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Just listen to the sound. It's so soothing.
lines.chinese: 听听这些声音。 是这样的安详。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I can hardly keep my eyes open. If only we could bottle the fresh air.
lines.chinese: 我简直睁不开眼睛了。 要是我们能用瓶子将新鲜空气装回去多好。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Wouldn't it be wonderful?
lines.chinese: 那样岂不是太美妙了吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: We could call it "Deep Sleep Country Air."
lines.chinese: 我们可以称它为“沉睡的乡村空气”  。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: It puts you to sleep. So does the sound. I've bee having a hard time keeping my eyes open just listening to it. It's like a special music.
lines.chinese: 它让你很想入睡。 这些声音便是。 我一直挣扎着要让双眼睁开 听着这些声音时。 它就像是一种特别的音乐。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Too bad we can't bottle the sound.
lines.chinese: 不能将声音装进瓶里真是太可惜了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: But we can!
lines.chinese: 但我们能！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Can what?
lines.chinese: 能做什么？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Bottle the sound.
lines.chinese: 将声音装起来呀。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: What are you talking about?
lines.chinese: 在说什么？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, yeah? Great idea! Let's do it!
lines.chinese: 噢，做什么？ 好主意！ 我们来录！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I'm so glad everyone is asleep. I thought Max would be crying, and everybody would be a wake.
lines.chinese: 很高兴大家都睡了。 我还以为 Max会哭个不停，让大家都睡不着。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: What did I tell you? Nothing to worry about.
lines.chinese: 我不是告诉你吗？ 没有什么可担心的。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: I'm sure everything is fine. My mother knows all there is to know about taking care of babies, I assure you.
lines.chinese: 我确信一切很好。 我母亲完全懂得 如何照顾好孩子，我向你保证。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Let's put some of this stuff away and then take off. We've got a forty-minute drive into the city.
lines.chinese: 让我们把这些东西拿出来然后就走啦。 我们还要开车四十分钟到城里去。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Welcome home-and I do mean welcome home.
lines.chinese: 欢迎归来--- 我真心欢迎你们回来。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Something wrong?
lines.chinese: 有什么不对劲吗？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, nothing's wrong, Richard. Believe me, Max is fine. But his teeth hurt, and he just can't get to sleep, poor dear.
lines.chinese: 噢，没有什么事，Richard。 相信我，Max 很好。 但他的牙疼，他不能入睡，可怜的小宝贝。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Neither can you.
lines.chinese: 你也不能睡。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, I feel so bad.
lines.chinese: 噢，我真感到难过。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, I'm fine. How was your weekend?
lines.chinese: 噢，我很好。 你们的周末过得怎样？

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: We had a great time, Mom.
lines.chinese: 我们玩得十分开心，妈。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It was wonderful. The weather couldn't have been better.
lines.chinese: 太好了。 天气再好不过了。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: It was nice here, too.
lines.chinese: 这儿也很好。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Did you get a chance to get outside at all?
lines.chinese: 你们有时间出去了？

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, yes. Grandpa helped me yesterday afternoon. I went to the supermarket to get a few things, and I stayed out an extra half hour. The village was filled people-the weather was so nice.
lines.chinese: 噢，是的。 昨天下午祖父帮我的忙。 我去了一趟菜市场买了几样东西， 我还特别多呆了半个小时。 镇上挤满人---由于天气好的缘故。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Harry's a professional camper, Mom. He knows all there is to know, and he made the weekend very easy for us to enjoy.
lines.chinese: 是露营专家，妈。 他无所不知， 使得我们周末过得非常轻松愉快。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Come on. You all helped.
lines.chinese: 别这样说，你们都帮了忙。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: You were wonderful, Harry!
lines.chinese: 你真是了不得，Harry！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Why don't you go to your room, Mom, and get some sleep.
lines.chinese: 为什么不到你的房间去，妈妈，去睡一会儿。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh, I'm fine. Tell me more about your weekend. Did you do anything special?
lines.chinese: 噢，我不要紧。 多告诉我一点你们的周末。 你们做了什么特别的事情吗？

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Lots of special country things. We picked flowers.
lines.chinese: 有许多特别的乡村趣事。 我们采花。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: And we brought some home for you. It was so nice to be out in the country.
lines.chinese: 而且带了一些回来给你。 出去置身乡下真是太好了。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: They smell wonderful.
lines.chinese: 闻起来很香。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Everything smelled so special. It would have been great if we had been able to bottle the smells.
lines.chinese: 所有的东西还有特别的味道。 假如我们能将气味装进瓶里该有多好。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: It would be a great business if you could do that.
lines.chinese: 假如能做到那样的话可是一笔大生意。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh!

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Uh, we're home now, Ellen. We'll take care of it.
lines.chinese: 噢，我们到家了，Ellen。 我们去照顾他。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'll take care of it. Let's see if it works.
lines.chinese: 我来照顾他。 让我们看看录音是不是有效果。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: What's that?
lines.chinese: 那是什么？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: A little we'd better head home. It's getting late, and we have a bit of a drive.
lines.chinese: 我想我们最好上路回家啦。 已经晚了，而我们还要开好一段路呢。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Well, all your things are inside. There's your sleeping bag.
lines.chinese: 嗯，所有你们的东西都拿来了。 这是你们的睡袋。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, thanks, Harry.
lines.chinese: 噢，谢谢，Harry。

lines.speaker: Harry
lines.english: Say good-bye to Richard. We'll call you all tomorrow night.
lines.chinese: 替我向 Richard 说声再见。 我们明天晚上打电话给你们。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Good-bye.
lines.chinese: 再见。

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.english: Bye, Mom.
lines.chinese: 再见，妈妈。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Bye-bye.
lines.chinese: 再见。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Bye.
lines.chinese: 再见。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: I'm so tired I think I'm overtired. I don't know if I can get to sleep.
lines.chinese: 我好累，我想我是累过头了。 我不知道我能否睡得着。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Max has stopped crying.
lines.chinese: 不哭了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Yes. It works!
lines.chinese: 不错。有效果！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It works!
lines.chinese: 有效果！

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: What works?
lines.chinese: 什么有效果？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: This.
lines.chinese: 这个。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: Oh. Oh, where did you get that? It sounds so nice. I think I'm falling asleep.
lines.chinese: 噢。噢，你们从哪儿弄来的？ 很好听。 我想我要睡着了。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Like Max did.
lines.chinese: 就像 Max 一样。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Good night.
lines.chinese: 晚安。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Good night. Sounds of the country. The soothing sounds of the country.
lines.chinese: 晚安。 乡村音乐。 安详的乡村音乐。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: A little further to the right, Tom.
lines.chinese: 再往右一点,Tom。

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: Is this OK?
lines.chinese: 这样可以吗?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Good. What do you think ,Richard?
lines.chinese: 好。你看呢?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I like it there.
lines.chinese: 摆在那儿很好。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: It's the first thing people will see when they come in. It sets the tone for the whole show. The next thing they'll see is this enlargement with the words Family Album, U.S.A.
lines.chinese: 这是人们走进来所看到的第一件东西。 它决定了整个展览的气氛。 他们接着看到的 这几个放大的字。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I can't believe this is really happening!
lines.chinese: 我不敢相信这一切真的发生了！

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: You've earned it. Years of work went into these pictures.
lines.chinese: 这是你应得的报偿。 多年心血换来这些照片。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I know, but it's still a dream come true.
lines.chinese: 我知道，但这终究是一个梦想实现了。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Well, remember, we're not sure what the critics are going to write about your show yet. And you never know what the man from the New York Times is going to say about it.
lines.chinese: 嗯，记住， 我们还不能肯定 评论家们会对你的展览写些什么。 而你永远也不知道 纽约时报的那个人会说些什么。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Are you worried?
lines.chinese: 你担心吗？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: I always worry. The reviews of this show are important for the sales of your book.
lines.chinese: 我一向担心。 与这次展览有关的评论 对于你这本书的销售很重要。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: When do we see the reviews?
lines.chinese: 什么时候我们可以看到评论？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Soon. One of the critics is coming over this morning for a preview. I hope he's in a good mood.
lines.chinese: 很快。 一位评论家今天早上要来看预展。 我希望他心情不错。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: So do I. Marilyn and I hope to use money from the sales of this book to buy a new house.
lines.chinese: 我也是。 和我希望用这本书销售的钱 来买一栋新房子。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: The book will be a success. And the show will help promote it.
lines.chinese: 这本书会成功的。 而这次展览会有利于促销它。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Speaking of promoting the book, do I really have to autograph copies for the guests at the opening?
lines.chinese: 说起促销书的事, 我是不是真的有必要 在展览开幕时为客人在书上签名？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: It's common practice.
lines.chinese: 这是惯例。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I feel uncomfortable about it.
lines.chinese: 我对此感到满不好意思的。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: A lot of people come to openings just so they can get the autograph of somebody who may be famous someday.
lines.chinese: 许多人参加开幕式 就是为了得到 将来有可能成名的人的签名。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Couldn't we wait until I'm famous?
lines.chinese: 难道不能等到我成名了再说？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: A little further back, Tom. It's too close to the refreshments.
lines.chinese: 再往后一点，Tom。 它离茶点太近了。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: No, I think this one belongs in the "people-at-work" section.
lines.chinese: 不，我认为它属于“工作中的人们”那一区。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: You may be right. Try it there, Tom
lines.chinese: 也许你说的对。 放在那儿试试，Tom。

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: Right.
lines.chinese: 好。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Nervous about the opening tonight?
lines.chinese: 对今晚开幕式感到紧张？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Nervous? Me? No. I'm scared to death.
lines.chinese: 紧张？ 我？ 不。我害怕得要命。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: How about guests? How many people will you be bringing?
lines.chinese: 来宾呢？ 你约了多少人来？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, my family, I guess. My wife Marilyn, my father and mother, my brother Robbie, my sister and her husband and their daughter. And my grandfather. Is that too many?
lines.chinese: 嗯，我一家人，我想。 我太太 Marilyn，我父亲和母亲。 我弟弟Robbie， 我妹妹 Susan 和她丈夫以及他们的女儿。 还有我祖父。 是不是太多了？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: No such thing as" too many" at an opening.
lines.chinese: 展览开幕怎么会嫌人太多呢。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I hope they like it.
lines.chinese: 我希望他们会喜欢。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Your family or the pubic?
lines.chinese: 你的家人还是公众？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Everybody. But especially my family. They've stood by me through all this.
lines.chinese: 每一位。 但尤其是我的家人。 他们一直支持我做这件事。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: I'm sure they'll like it.
lines.chinese: 我相信他们会喜欢的。

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Harvey, how are you?
lines.chinese: 你好吗？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Mitchell, so nice of you to come. Richard, this is Mitchell Johnson. Mitchell is one of the most important syndicated reviewers in the country.
lines.chinese: 欢迎光临。 这是 Mitchell Johnson。 是 我国最重要的报团评论家之一。

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Well, you're the young man who did all this.
lines.chinese: 嗯，你就是那位拍这些照片的年轻人。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I'm afraid so.
lines.chinese: 不敢当。

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Nice to meet you.
lines.chinese: 很高兴见到你。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Nict to meet you, Mr. Johnson.
lines.chinese: 很高兴见到你，Johnson 先生。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Mitchell has always encouraged new talent.
lines.chinese: 总是在鼓励新人。

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Well...you mind if I look around and see what it says to me?
lines.chinese: 嗯……你们不介意我到处瞧瞧 看它给我什么样的感受？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Be our guest.
lines.chinese: 请随意。

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Mm-hmm, hmm, uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: What does that mean when he goes, "Mm-hmm, mm-hmm,mm-hmm"?
lines.chinese: 他发出的声音代表什么意思，

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: It probably means he's clearing his throat. I don't know.
lines.chinese: 也许表示他正在清嗓子。 我不知道。

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: I don't care what the critics say, Mr.Stewart. Your work is brilliant.
lines.chinese: 我不管评论家怎么说，Stewart 先生。 你的作品很好。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you, Tom.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你，Tom。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Tom is studying photography at NYU. He's working with me during the summer months as an intern.
lines.chinese: 正在 NYU 学摄影。 在夏天的这几个月到我这儿来当实习生。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, really? I'd like to see your work.
lines.chinese: 噢，真的？ 我希望能看到你的作品。

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: It's not good enough to show. I'm still learning.
lines.chinese: 还不值得展示。 我还在学习当中。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, I'd still like to see your work. You may be the next Ansel Adams and not even know it.
lines.chinese: 噢，我仍然希望看到你的作品。 你或许是第二个 Ansel Adams 而不自知呢。

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: If you really mean it, I'll bring some of my pictures into the gallery.
lines.chinese: 假如你是说真的， 我就带一些我的摄影到艺廊来。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I do mean it.
lines.chinese: 我的确是说真的。

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Very interesting pictures, Mr.Stewart. You have a most unusual eye.
lines.chinese: 你的照片很有意思，Stewart 先生。 你有着极为不寻常的眼光。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you. I hope that's a compliment.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。 我希望这是赞美之词。

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: It is.
lines.chinese: 它是的。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Are you going to be reviewing the show, Mitchell?
lines.chinese: 你会评论这项展览吗，Mitchell?

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Oh, yes. It's definitely worth reviewing.
lines.chinese: 噢，是的。它着实值得评论。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Favorably?
lines.chinese: 是好评？

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Oh, you know I never answer that question, Harvey. I'd like an advance copy of the book, though, so I can study it.
lines.chinese: 噢，你知道我从不回答这类的问题，Harvey。 但我想要一本新书的试印， 以便我好好研究。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: I have an autographed one in my office. Tom, would you give Mr.Johnson the copy of Richard's book on my desk?
lines.chinese: 在我办公室，有一本签了名的。 拿给 Johnson 先生 在我桌上那本 Richard 的书，好吗？

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: Just follow me, Mr.Johnson.
lines.chinese: 请跟我来，Johnson 先生。

lines.speaker: Mitchell
lines.english: Sure.
lines.chinese: 好的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Do you think he liked my photographs?
lines.chinese: 你觉得他喜欢我的摄影？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: We'll know when tonight's papers come out. Keep your fingers crossed.
lines.chinese: 今晚的报纸出来的时候，我们就知道了。 希望你一切顺利。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Do you like the dress?
lines.chinese: 你喜欢这套衣服？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I love it.
lines.chinese: 我喜欢。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: I designed it myself.
lines.chinese: 这是我自己设计的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: It's beautiful.
lines.chinese: 它很漂亮。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Are you ready? We're supposed to be there before the guests arrive.
lines.chinese: 你准备好了吗？ 我们应当先到那儿 在客人到达之前。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I know. I know. Help me with this tie, will you? Honey, I'm scared to death.
lines.chinese: 我知道。我知道。 帮我打领带，好吗？ 亲爱的，我害怕得要命。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: But this is what you've been working for all these years.
lines.chinese: 但你多年来的工作，为的就是这个呀。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: No, no. I worked to put together a book of photographs. This is show business.
lines.chinese: 不，不。 我的工作是创作出一本摄影书。 而今天这个却是演艺事业。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Well, it's all part of the same job. Just relax and enjoy it.
lines.chinese: 嗯，它们都是同一项工作的一部份。 放轻松点，好好地享受它。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: You're right. I earned this, and I'm going to enjoy it. As soon as I recover from my nervous breakdown.
lines.chinese: 说得对。 我辛苦赢得的，我要去享受它。 只等我从神经崩溃中恢复过来。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What are you afraid of?
lines.chinese: 你怕什么？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Everything. A critic was there this morning. He probably hates my work. I have to sign copies of my book for a lot of people I never met before. My new shoes hurt my feet...
lines.chinese: 每一样事情。 一个评论家早上去过了。 他也许讨厌我的作品。 我还必须为许多人在我的书上签名 我从未谋面过的的人。 我的新鞋弄痛了我的脚……

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: You're going to be a great success. Are you ready?
lines.chinese: 你会获得很大的成功的。 你准备好了吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: As soon as I get these cuff links on.
lines.chinese: 只等我将袖扣扣好。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Let me help. Mom and Dad are already on their way down to the gallery.
lines.chinese: 我来帮忙。 妈妈和爸爸已经出发去画廊了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: There! You look very attractive.
lines.chinese: 天！你看起来真引人注目。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you. Well, I suppose I'v run out of excuses.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。 噢，我想我再也找不出籍口了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Mm-hmm. Let's go...
lines.chinese: 嗯哼。我们走吧……

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, one minute. Before we go to the gallery, I just want to tell you that I never could have done this book without your help and your love. I appreciate it.
lines.chinese: 噢，再等一分钟。 在我们去画廊之前， 我想告诉你 我不可能完成这本书 要不是你的帮助和爱。 我很感激。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Thanks. Now, no more stalling.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。 现在，不要再拖延了。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: What is it?
lines.chinese: 怎么回事？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: There's nobody here!
lines.chinese: 这儿一个人都没有！

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Of course not, Richard. Your show doesn't begin until eight thirty.
lines.chinese: 当然不会有，Richard。 你的展览八点半才开始。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh. Right.
lines.chinese: 噢。对了。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Richard! Welcome! Good luck tonight!
lines.chinese: 欢迎！祝你今天晚上好运！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, thanks. This is my publisher. Harvey Carlson. You've met my wife Marilyn...
lines.chinese: 噢，谢谢你。 这是我的出版商 Harvey Carlson。 你曾经见过我太太 Marilyn…

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Charmed.
lines.chinese: 荣幸见到你。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Hi.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: My mother, Ellen Stewart. Harvey Carlson.
lines.chinese: 我母亲 Ellen Stewart。 这是 Harvey Carlson。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: It's good to meet you.
lines.chinese: 很高兴见到你。

lines.speaker: Ellen
lines.english: It's nice to meet you.
lines.chinese: 很高兴见到你。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: This is my father, Dr.Philip Stewart...
lines.chinese: 这是我父亲 Philip Stewart 医生……

lines.speaker: Philip
lines.english: Nice to meet you, Mr. Carlson.
lines.chinese: 很高兴见到你，Carlson 先生。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: My brother Robbie...
lines.chinese: 我弟弟 Robbie……

lines.speaker: Robbie
lines.english: Hi.

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Hi.

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And this is my sister Susan and her husband Harry Bennett and his daughter Michelle.
lines.chinese: 嗨。 嗨。 这是我妹妹 Susan和她丈夫 Harry Bennett 以及他女儿 Michelle。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: It's nice to meet you.
lines.chinese: 很高兴见到你。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: And this gentleman is my grandfather, Malcolm Stewart.
lines.chinese: 这位绅士是我祖父 Malcolm Stewart。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Welcome, Mr.Stewart. Well, make yourselves comfortable. There are hors d'oeuvres at the table, fruit punch at the bar. Help yourselves. Can I get you something, Mr.Setwart?
lines.chinese: 欢迎你，Stewart 先生。 噢，请大家自便。 在桌子上有小吃，有水果调味饮品。 请随便用。 要我替你拿点什么吗，Stewart 先生？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: No, thank you.
lines.chinese: 不用了，谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: You can feel very proud of your grandson, Mr.Stewart.
lines.chinese: 你可以为你的孙子感到非常的骄傲，Stewart 先生？

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: I do. I'm proud of all my grandchildren, Mr.Carlson.
lines.chinese: 我就是啊。 我为我所有的孙子孙女感到骄傲，Carlson先生。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Of course. Feel free to look around. If you need anything, just ask.
lines.chinese: 当然。 请随意到处看看。 假如你需要什么，请尽管讲。

lines.speaker: Grandpa
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢你。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Harvey?

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Yes?

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Did Mitchell Johnson's review come out yet?
lines.chinese: 的评论出来了没有？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Not yet. The newpapers don't come out till about ten 0'clock. When they come out, we'll get it.
lines.chinese: 还没有。 报纸要差不多十点钟才出来。 一出来，我们就会拿到手。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Ready?
lines.chinese: 准备好了吗？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Yes. Have the people arrived?
lines.chinese: 是的。客人来了？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: The guests are waiting. Tom's about to open the doors. Good luck! And stop worrying! They're going to love it.
lines.chinese: 客人们正等着呢。 准备要开门。 祝你好运！别再担心！ 他们会喜欢的。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: What's this?
lines.chinese: 这是什么？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Read it.
lines.chinese: 读出来吧。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: I can't. Would you read it, Marilyn?
lines.chinese: 我不敢读。 请你读好吗，Marilyn？

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: "Richard Stewart's show at the Carlson Gallery is a collection of photographs from his new book entitled Family Album, U.S.A. There is power and beauty in. Mr. Stewart's work, and his book introduces us to a remarkable new talent." Oh, Richard, it's wonderful!
lines.chinese: 在Carlson 画廊展出的 是选出的摄影作品 从他的新书 Family Album, U.S.A. 先生的工作中显出力与美。 他的书 让我们看到一位卓越的新秀。” 噢，Richard，太棒了！

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Congratulations!
lines.chinese: 恭喜你！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Wow! I'm overwhelmed.
lines.chinese: 喔！我太感动了。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Ladies and gentlemen, if I may have your attention for a moment, please? I hope you're all enjoying the exhibition. I know that I am. And I would like to introduce the young man who spent the last five years taking these remarkable pictures and writing the background for Family. Album, U.S.A-Mr Richard Stewart.
lines.chinese: 各位女士，各位先生， 我想占用你们一点点时间，好吗？ 我希望你们都喜欢这个展览。 我知道我是很喜欢。 现在我希望向大家介绍这位年轻人 用过去五年的时间 来拍摄这些作品 为 Family Album,U.S.A.撰写背景说明的年轻人 先生。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Hello. I want to thank all of you for coming here tonight. I'd like to thank Harvey Carlson for his faith in my project. But most of all, I would like to thank my family for their love and support all through this adventure. Thank you.
lines.chinese: 你们好。 我非常感谢你们大家今天晚上赏光。 我非常感谢 Harvey Carlson。 对我工作计划的信心。 但最重要的， 我要感谢我的家人 在这整个工作期间给予我的爱和支持。 谢谢各位。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, I'm glad this's over.
lines.chinese: 噢，我真高兴终于结束了。

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: Would you mind?
lines.chinese: 你们不介意吧？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, not at all.
lines.chinese: 噢，一点也不。

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: Thanks.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Our pleasure. Hope it wins a Pulitzer Prize.
lines.chinese: 非常荣幸。 希望它能得 Pulitzer奖。

lines.speaker: Tom
lines.english: I'll settle for an "A" in my photography course.
lines.chinese: 我只要摄影课能得"A"就满足了。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Ah, Richard, Marilyn, I want you to meet John O'Neill.
lines.chinese: 啊，Richard，Marilyn，我向你们介绍 John O'Neill。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh,how do you do ?
lines.chinese: 噢，你好。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Nice to meet you.
lines.chinese: 很高兴见到你。

lines.speaker: O'Neill
lines.english: I've been looking forward to meeting your husband, Mrs. Stewart. I'm really impressed by your show.
lines.chinese: 我一直希望能见到你先生，Stewart 太太。 你的展览让我留下了深刻的印象。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thank you.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: O'Neill
lines.english: In fact, I'd like your autograph.
lines.chinese: 事实上，我想要你的签名。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Oh, well, really...
lines.chinese: 噢，哦，真的……

lines.speaker: O'Neill
lines.english: On a contract.
lines.chinese: 在一份合同上面。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: What's this about, Harvey?
lines.chinese: 这是怎么回事，Harvey？

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Mr. O'Neill is the publisher of several magazines.
lines.chinese: 先生是一些杂志的发行人。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Of course. I've seen you on television.
lines.chinese: 当然。我在电视上看见过你。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Mr. O'Neill was so impressed with your work that he wants to develop it into a magazine concept.
lines.chinese: 先生被你的作品深深打动 他想要将它加以发展使之成为一个杂志方面的构想。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Well, that sounds very exciting, but where would I fit into the plan?
lines.chinese: 嗯，听起来很令人兴奋， 但是我在这项计划中怎么定位？

lines.speaker: O'Neill
lines.english: I'd like you to be the photo editor of the magazine.
lines.chinese: 我希望你能做杂志的摄影编辑。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: Oh, Richard, how exciting!
lines.chinese: 哦，Richard，太令人兴奋了！

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: It's a wonderful opportunity, Richard.
lines.chinese: 这是一个好机会，Richard。

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Hold on. Wait a minute, please.
lines.chinese: 等等。请稍等一下。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: What's the problem, Richard?
lines.chinese: 有什么问题吗，Richard？

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: The problem is that I'm a photographer, not an editor. I like what I do. In fact, I love what I do...which is going out with a camera and a roll of film and seeing the wonder of humanity. Now, I appreciate your offer, but I've worked so hard on Family Album, U.S.A. and I'm giving some thought to a new book on a different subject. I'm flattered, but I enjoy taking pictures, and I want to continue doing that. Thank you, but I'm happy being a photographer.
lines.chinese: 问题是我是一个摄影师，不是一个编辑。 我喜欢我所做的工作。 事实上，我热爱我的工作… 那就是带着照相机和一卷底片出去 看众生奇景。 因此，我感激你的提议， 但是我一直为 Family Album, U.S.A.辛勤工作， 如今我又开始在构想另一本不同题材的新书。 你的厚爱使我受宠若惊， 但我喜欢拍照片，而且我要继续做这一行。 谢谢你， 但我乐于当一名摄影师。

lines.speaker: O'Neill
lines.english: I understand, Richard.
lines.chinese: 我懂了，Richard。

lines.speaker: Carlson
lines.english: Richard, I know your next book will be a success. Congratulations!
lines.chinese: 我相信你的下一本书会成功的。 恭喜你！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks.
lines.chinese: 谢谢。

lines.speaker: Marilyn
lines.english: You're a real Stewart!
lines.chinese: 你真不愧是 Stewart 家的人！

lines.speaker: Richard
lines.english: Thanks. PAGE PAGE - 2 -
lines.chinese: 谢谢。
